Standing in the doorway of the walk-in closet was a figure dressed in a pristine, black velvet uniform. Her gray hair was pulled back into an impossibly tight,
severe bun.

It was Agnes Novak, the head housekeeper and Germaine Chambers' absolute, ruthless enforcer.

Agnes stood with her hands clasped neatly in front of her waist. Her face was a mask of terrifying,emotionless stone. She didn't look surprised to see an intruder dressed in black tactical gear. She looked like a spider who had just felt a fly hit the center of her web.

"Ms. Wilder," Agnes said. Her voice was dry,mechanical, and completely devoid of wvarmth. "It is quite late to be rummaging through the closets."

Elisa slowly stood up. She didn't bother pulling down her mask. She stared at the old woman, her black eyes burning with a lethal intensity.

"Where are my private legal documents, Agnes?"Elisa demanded, her voice a low, vibrating threat.

Agnes didn't flinch. She didn't answer the question.She simply took a half-step backward, clearing the doorway, and offered a stiff, incredibly mocking bow.

"Madam has been expecting you," Agnes said
smoothly. "She is waiting in her study. Please,follow me."

Elisa's jaw locked so tight her teeth ached.

She,was trapped. The documents were gone. The element of surprise was completely destroyed. She had walked blindly into a trap set by the apex predator of the Chambers family.

But Elisa didn't panic. She didn't run. Running without the documents meant losing the war.

She stepped out of the closet, her posture radiating a cold, aristocratic defiance that rivaled the old money surrounding her.

"Lead the way," Elisa said.

She followed Agnes out of the bedroom and down the long, silent corridor. They stopped in front of a set of massive, double mahogany doors.

Agnes reached out and pushed the heavy doors open.

Elisa took a deep breath, filling her lungs with the scent of old paper and cigar smoke, and walked into the slaughterhouse.
###Chapter 67


The study was cavernous and suffocatingly dark.The only illumination came from a dying fire in the massive stone hearth, the embers casting erratic,blood-red shadows across the walls.

Sitting behind a colossal mahogany desk, looking like a monarch presiding over an execution, was Germaine Chambers.

She wore a dark silk robe. Her gnarled, heavily jeweled fingers were casually playing with an antique silver magnifying glass.

Resting perfectly centered on the polished wood of the desk, illuminated by a single brass reading lamp,were two distinct documents.

Elisa instantly recognized the thick, textured parchment of her Marriage Certificate, bearing the heavy embossed seal of New York State. Right next to it lay a stack of crisp, white medical records-documents that absolutely did not belong in the manor.
Elisa walked slowly toward the desk. She stopped three feet away, her eyes locked onto the documents.

"Stealing private legal documents from a locked safe," Elisa said, her voice a flat, freezing monotone."That is a federal felony, Germaine. Even your lawyers can't make that disappear."

Germaine let out a dry, rattling laugh. It sounded like dry leaves scraping across pavement.

"A federal felony?" Germaine mocked, leaning back in her high leather chair. "My dear, naive girl. In this zip code, the Chambers name is the law. The federal government asks my permission before they audit my charities."

Germaine reached out and placed her manicured finger directly on top of the Marriage Certificate.

"I know exactly why you came here tonight,"Germaine said, her voice dropping to a harsh,venomous whisper. "You wanted to take this piece of paper to the press. You wanted to prove you are the legal wife. You wanted to destroy Allena's little victim narrative."
Elisa didn't break eye contact. "I am going to clear my name. I am not going to let your son and his parasite ruin my life to cover up their own filth."

Germaine's eyes flashed with sudden, terrifying rage.She slammed her hand flat against the desk. The loud smack echoed in the quiet room.

"Your life?" Germaine hissed, leaning forward, her face twisting into a mask of pure aristocratic malice."Your life is entirely irrelevant! Do you have any idea what happened to the Chambers Group stock today when those photos leaked? We lost three percent of our market cap. Billions of dollars, erased in hours,because of a trashy taboloid scandal!"

Germaine stood up. She looked at Elisa with absolute disgust.

"The public has already decided you are the villain,"Germaine stated coldly. "The narrative is set. If you release this certificate now, it won't just destroy Allena. It will prove that August is a liar. It will prove the CEO of this family is a manipulative adulterer.The board of directors will crucify him, and the stock will freefall."
Germnaine pointed a shaking, furious finger at Elisa.
"I will not allow you to burn my family's empire to the ground just to save your pathetic pride. You are going to be the sacrifice. You are going to stay the villaín, and you are going to keep your mouth shut."

Elisa stared at the old woman. A cold, dark amusement flickered in her eyes.

"You are delusional," Elisa whispered. She took a step forward,reaching her hand out toward the desk to take her documents. "I am walking out of here with my property. Try to stop me."

Before Elisa's fingers could touch the parchment,Germaine's hand shot out like a striking snake. She slammed her palm down hard, pinning the stack of medical records to the wood.

"I didn't just open your safe, Elisa," Germaine said,her voice dropping to a terrifying, absolute calm."I also had my private investigators look into your weekly routine. And they found a very interesting file regarding a patient named Phineas."

"My family is the primary benefactor of the entire lvy League Medical Group," Germaine continued, her
eyes locking onto Elisa's, a cruel smile playing on her lips. "A position on the board of trustees grants one certain... privileges. It's amazing what you can find when you bypass patient confidentiality protocols.I know all about the old man's severe Alzheimer's. I also know that the experimental, highly expensive targeted therapy drug keeping him stable is entirely funded by a backdoor grant from the Chambers Medical Foundation."

"If you touch that certificate," Germaine whispered,"I will make a single phone call. And your grandfather, Phineas, will be violently evicted from the lvy League Memory Care Center before the sun comes up. The grant will be revoked. The medication stops tonight. Without it, the doctors say his brain will completely deteriorate in less than a month. He will die, Elisa. And he will die screaming in confusion."

Elisa's hand froze in mid-air.

The blood in her veins instantly turned to solid ice. A massive, suffocating weight crashed onto her chest,driving the air from her lungs.

Germaine smiled. It was a smile of pure, sadistic
victory. She saw the terror flash in Elisa's eyes.
"You evil, rotting bitch," Elisa breathed, the words tearing out of her throat like jagged glass.

Germaine didn't even blink at the insult. She simply pressed a button on her intercom.

"Agnes," Germaine called out. "Send them in."

The side door of the study opened. Three men in immaculate, thousand-dollar suits walked in carrying leather briefcases. They were senior partners from a top-tier white-shoe law firm. They moved like silent,efficient machines.

They approached the desk and laid out a thick stack of legal documents.

"This is an absolute, ironclad Non-Disclosure Agreement," Germaine explained, her voice retuning to a brisk, businesslike tone. "You will sign it. You will legally forfeit your right to ever publicly discuss your marriage to August, the divorce, or Allena Mills."

Germaine gestured to a second document.

"In exchange foryour silence, and your agreement to take the fall in the media," Germaine continued, "fifty
million dollars will be immediately wired into an offshore trust in your name. And your grandfather's medical care will be guaranteed for the rest of his natural life."

Germaine picked up a heavy Montblanc fountain pen and held it out toward Elisa.

"Sign the papers, Elisa," Germaine commanded.′′Okill your grandfather. The choice is yours."

Elisa looked at the pen. She looked at the NDA. She was trapped in a flawless, inescapable cage of pure capitalistic power.②
###Chapter 68


The heavy silence in the study was deafening. The three lawyers stood like statues, their eyes fixed on the thick stack of legal documents resting on the mahogany desk.

Elisa stared at the heavy gold nib of the Montblanc pen Germaine was holding out to her.

Her mind raced, desperately searching for a loophole, a tactical exit, a way to flip the board. But there was nothing. Germaine held the absolute high ground. The threat to Phineas was immediate,physical,and lethal.

Elisa closed her eyes.

Behind her eyelids, she didn't see August's betrayal.She didn't see Allena's smug face. She saw the frail,trembling hands of her grandfather. She saw the terror in his eyes when the dementia took hold. He was the only innocent person in this entire war.

She could not gamble with his life.
When Elisa opened her eyes, the burning, violent rage was completely gone. Her black eyes were flat,dead,and terrifyingly calm. The last remnants of her humanity, her pride, and her desire for conventional justice were permanently extinguished.

She reached out and snatched the heavy gold pen from Germaine's hand.

Elisa didn't read the fifty pages of legal jargon. She didn't need to. She knew exactly what it was: a digital and social death sentence.

She flipped to the signature page of the Non-Disclosure Agreement. She pressed the pen to the paper. The scratching sound of the nib cutting across the thick parchment echoed loudly in the quiet room.

She signed her name. Sharp, aggressive, and final.

She pulled the compensation agreement forward and signed that as well.

Germaine watched her, a look of supreme,arrogant satisfaction settling over her wrinkled features. She had won. She had successfully bought the silence of a problem and protected her family's stock price.
The lead lawyer stepped forward, quickly gathering the signed originals. He slid a single, thin folder containing the copies across the desk toward Elisa.

Germaine sat back in her leather chair, steepling her fingers.

"You made the smart choice, Elisa," Germnaine said,her voice dripping with a sickening, patronizing pity."Take the fifty million. Disappear from New York. Go buy a quiet little house somewhere and live the mediocre life you were always destined for."

Elisa picked up the folder. She didn't put it in her bag.She casually, almost disrespectfully, rolled the expensive legal documents into a tight cylinder in her fist.

She looked at Germaine. A slow, chilling smile spread across Elisa's face.

"Fifty million dollars to buy the Chambers family's honor," Elisa mocked, her voice a soft, lethal whisper."You got a terrible deal, Germaine. Your honor isn't worth fifty cents."

Germaine's smug expression faltered. A flash of genuine anger tightened her jaw, but she forced
herself to remain calm. She viewed Elisa's insult as the pathetic, final barking of a beaten dog.

Elisa turned on her heel. She walked toward the heavy double doors.

Just as her hand touched the brass handle, she stopped. She didn't turn around.

"Make sure the checks to the medical center clear on time," Elisa said, her voice echoing off the dark wood paneling. "If my grandfather misses a single dose of his medication... if he loses a single hair on his head... I swear to God, Germaine, I will burn this entire estate to the ground with you locked inside it."②

Before Germaine could respond, Elisa shoved the doors open and walked out.

Bang. The heavy doors slammed shut behind her,sealing the old woman in her tomb of a study.

Elisa walked down the long, silent corridors of the manor. Every step she took felt lighter, yet infinitely more dangerous.

She pushed through the servants' door and stepped out into the freezing night air. The cold wind whipped
against her face, but she didn't shiver.

She walked to the shadows where she had parked the Porsche. She slid into the driver's seat and closed the door.

As soon as the cabin sealed, her phone vibrated in her pocket.

She pulled it out. It was an automated, encrypted alert from a Swiss banking server.

Transfer Complete. $50,000,000.00 USD deposited into Offshore Trust Account.

Elisa stared at the glowing numbers.

Fifty million dollars. It was an obscene amount of wealth. But looking at it made her stomach churn with physical nausea. This money was the price of her reputation. It was the price of being branded a psychotic homewrecker by the entire world.

She rested her forehead against the cold leather of the steering wheel.

In the absolute darkness of the car, a single, hot tear slipped from the corner of her eye and tracked down her cheek.
It wasn't a tear of sadness for her marriage. It wasn't a tear of fear. It was a tear of mourning for the woman she used to be. The quiet, enduring nurse who believed that the truth would eventually win.That woman was officially dead.

Elisa lifted her head. She wiped the moisture from her cheek with the back of her hand.

She opened the Twitter app one last time. The JusticeForAllena hashtag was still trending. The world still thought she was a monster.

Because of the NDA, her mouth was legally sewn shut. She could never go to a journalist. She could never post a defense online.

But as she stared at the screen, her eyes hardened into two chips of black ice.

The law had silenced Elisa Wilder.

But the law had absolutely no jurisdiction over Faye.

Germaine thought she had bought peace. She didn't realize she had just handed a fifty-million-dollar wvar chest to the most lethal digital architect on the planet. If Elisa couldn't destroy them in the tabloids,
Faye would slaughter them on the global financial stage.

Elisa reached down and twisted the ignition.

The Porsche's engine roared to life, a deep, guttural scream that shattered the quiet night. She slammed the car into gear and tore out of the estate, heading straight for Manhattan.
###Chapter 69


The gears of the financial war had already begun to turn. While waiting for the first round of data feedback from Alastair's team, Elisa knew she had one final, physical tie to sever. After a sleepless night at the temporary apartment, she returned to the cold Manhattan penthouse one last time the next morning to retrieve her remaining personal items.

The morning sun pierced through the floor-to-ceiling windows. Elisa stood in the center of the massive master walk-in closet. Two large, black hard-shell suitcases sat fully zipped by the door. She had spent the last hour methodically packing the final remnants of her life. She was leaving this sterile, cold museum of a home forever.

Suddenly, the heavy front door of the penthouse was thrown open.

A chaotic flurry of footsteps and loud, imperious voices echoed through the marble foyer.

Elisa frowned. She walked out of the closet and
stepped into the massive living area.

Germaine Chambers was standing in the center of the room, leaning heavily on her silver-handled cane.Behind her stood two of her personal security guards and August.

August looked exhausted. His eyes were bloodshot,his jaw tight with barely suppressed rage. He looked like a man who had been awake all night fighting a losing battle.

Germaine spotted Elisa. The old woman's eyes narrowed into sharp slits.

"I see you haven't left yet," Germaine barked,her voice echoing harshly off the glass walls. "The market opened twenty minutes ago. The Chambers Group stock took another massive hit. The board is panicking over the Page Six scandal. Your continued presence in this city is a liability."

Germaine pointed her cane directly at Elisa.

"The agreement was that you would disappear.Quietly. Immediately," Germaine ordered. "My men are here to escort you to JFK. There is a one-way ticket to Zurich waiting for you at the private
terminal. You will get on that plane, and you will not return."

Elisa stared at the old woman. A cold, incredulous laugh bubbled up in her chest.

Less than twelve hours ago, this woman had threatened an old man's life to force her to sign a gag order. Now, she was breaking into her home with armed guards to forcibly exile her.

Elisa crossed her arms over her chest. She leaned casually against the doorframe of the guest room.

"Are you developing dementia, Germaine?" Elisa asked, her voice dripping with absolute, freezing mockery. "I signed your NDA. I am legally a ghost.I haven't spoken to a soul. Your stock problem is your own.I will leave when I am ready."

Germaine's face flushed with anger. She slammed her cane against the marble floor.

"You took fifty million dollars of my money!"Germaine snapped. "You will leave on my timeline!You are a stain that must be scrubbed out now!"

August ran a frustrated hand through his hair. He
looked at Elisa, his expression a mix of humiliation and desperate command.

"Just go, Elisa," August gritted out, his voice tight. "It will take five minutes. Allena is having a complete meltdown at her apartment because people are calling her a mistress online. If you just disappear, it calms the board down, and it takes the heat off her."

Elisa looked at him. The sheer, astronomical audacity of his request was staggering. He was asking his abused wife to flee the country to protect his mistress's feelings.

"Grab your bags. Now," Germaine commanded,gesturing to her guards. One of the large men took a step forward, his hand reaching for Elisa's arm.

The moment his body moved into her personal space,it hit her.

A thick, suffocating wave of bespoke, heavy rose perfume wafted off August's suit jacket from where he stood nearby. It was Allena's signature scent. It was baked into the fabric of his clothes. He had come straight from Allena's bed to watch her be exiled.
A violent, uncontrollable spasm of physiological revulsion ripped through Elisa's stomach.②

The smell was the physical manifestation of every lie,every betrayal, every ounce of abuse she had suffered. Her throat closed up. The acid in her stomach violently surged upward.

She couldn't breathe. She was going to vomit.

Smack!

The sound was as loud as a gunshot in the quiet penthouse.

Elisa's hand shot up with blinding, desperate speed.She violently, viciously slapped the guard's approaching hand away from her. The force was so unexpected it made the large man stumble back a step.

August, completely caught off guard by the explosive physical violence, instinctively moved to intervene,his hand clamping down on her shoulder to restrain her.

"What the hell is wrong with you?!" August roared,his voice shaking the windows.
Elisa shot up from her leaning position. She stumbled back two steps, her hand pressing hard against her stomach as she wrenched herself out of his grip. She looked at her own shoulder where his hand had rested, brushing at the fabric in frantic, disgusted motions, as if trying to wipe away a flesh-eating virus.

She looked up at August. Her black eyes were wide,filled not with anger, but with pure, unadulterated physical sickness.

"You smell like her," Elisa choked out, her voice trembling with disgust. "You reek of her perfume.Don't you ever touch me again. You make me want to vomit."

The words hung in the air, a brutal, undeniable execution of his ego.

Germaine's face turned purple. "You ungrateful,psychotic bitch!"

Elisa didn't look at the old woman. She kept her eyes locked on August's shocked face.

"You have my signature," Elisa whispered coldly. "But you will never have my obedience. Get out of my
way."

Elisa turned and walked quickly back into the walk-in closet. She slammed the heavy door shut, twisting the deadbolt with a loud, final click, leaving the billionaire and his mother standing in the wreckage of their own humiliation.
###Chapter 70


The penthouse was dead silent.

The security guards stood awkwardly in the foyer,unsure of their orders. Germaine, sputtering with rage, finally realized that physically forcing Elisa onto a plane would create an even bigger scandal if she fought back. She stormed out, vowing to freeze every one of Elisa's accounts.

Elisa stood in the walk-in closet. Her breathing had finally slowed. The nausea had receded, leaving behind a cold, hollow emptiness.

She walked over to the two black suitcases by the door and pulled the telescopic handles up. It was time to leave.

Suddenly, the lock on the guest room door clicked.

Elisa frowned. She had thrown the deadbolt, but August had the master key to every door in the penthouse.

The door pushed open. August stood in the frame.
The explosive rage from the living area had vanished,replaced by a dark, brooding, incredibly arrogant calm.

In his right hand, he held a small, expensive tube of Arnica bruise cream that Agnes, the housekeeper,had hastily procured from the medical kit.

He walked into the room, his eyes scanning her packed bags, but he completely ignored them. He walked straight toward her.

As he approached, his gaze dropped. Because Elisa was wearing a loose silk top, the violent movement of her slapping the guard earlier had caused the fabric to slip. A massive, horrifyingly dark purple bruise was clearly visible on her lower back, blooming angrily against her pale skin.

A flicker of intense annoyance crossed August's eyes. He hated imperfections, especially ones that reflected poorly on his domain.

He tossed the tube of cream onto the vanity counter next to her. It wasn't an apology. It was a cold,aesthetic command.

"The housekeeper prepared this cream," August
said, his voice a low, authoritative rumble, dripping with disdain. "Deal with that disgusting bruise. Don't walk around looking like a battered stray and embarrass me."

He actually believed it. He actually believed that his wealth and his name were so intoxicating that she would accept physical abuse and public humiliation just to keep them.

Elisa looked at his hand gripping her wrist.

She didn't slap him this time. She simply reached up with her free hand, dug her fingers into his thumb joint, and applied a sharp, agonizing pressure to the nerve.

August hissed in pain, his fingers instantly releasing her wrist.

"Your charity," Elisa whispered, stepping back, "is more repulsive than your perfume."

Before August could recover his temper, a sharp,cheerful ringtone shattered the tension in the room.

It was August's private cell phone. The custom ringtone was exclusively set for Allena.
August instinctively reached into his pocket and pulled the phone out. He looked at the screen. He looked at Elisa standing right in front of him.

He didn't step out into the hallway. He didn't decline the call. His pathological need to soothe his mistress completely overrode any basic human decency.

He swiped to answer and pressed the phone to his ear.

"Allena, baby, what's wrong?" August's voice instantly transformed. The cold dictator vanished,replaced by a soft, sickeningly sweet, panicked lover.

Through the quiet room, Elisa could hear Allena's high-pitched, hysterical sobbing through the speaker.

"August, the internet is so mean!" Allena cried."They are saying I faked the fall! They are saying l'm a liar!You have to fix it! You promised you would protect me!"

August turned his back slightly to Elisa, hunching his broad shoulders as he spoke into the phone.

"I know, baby, I know," August cooed, his voice thick
with desperate devotion. "I'm fixing it right now. My mother is handling the press. No one is going to hurt you. I'm leaving right now to come see you, okay?Just breathe."

Elisa stood perfectly still.

She watched her legal husband stand in her bedroom, whispering sweet, protective promises to the woman who had destroyed her life.

A month ago, this sight would have shattered her heart. A week ago, it would have ignited a blinding rage.

But right now? In this exact second?

Elisa felt nothing but a massive, overwhelming,glorious wave of absolute relief.

The heavy, suffocating chains of the last seven years simply evaporated. She saw August Chambers for exactly what he was: a weak, easily manipulated,incredibly stupid man. He wasn't a monster to be feared. He was a clown to be pitied.

He wasn't worth her anger. He was only worth her strategy.
Elisa turned away from him. She grabbed the handles of her two black suitcases.

The loud clatter of the plastic wheels rolling across the hardwood floor made August spin around.He pulled the phone away from his ear, his brow furrowing in deep, arrogant confusion.

"Where do you think you're going?" August demanded, his voice hardening back into a command. "I didn't say you could leave."

Elisa didn't stop walking. She didn't even turn her head.

"I am going to a world where you don't exist,August," Elisa said. Her voice was calm, steady, and incredibly light.

August let out a harsh, barking laugh. It was the sound of a man completely blinded by his own ego.

"A world without me?" August mocked loudly,following her into the foyer. "You have nothing, Elisa!You are a nobody! Without my money, you won't last three days in this city. You'll be back here on your knees, begging me to let you back in!"
Elisa reached the heavy front door.She opened it.

She finally stopped and looked back over her shoulder. She looked at the billionaire standing in his empty, cold penthouse, clutching his phone like a lifeline.

She didn't argue. She didn't tell him about the fifty million dollars. She didn't tell him about Faye.②

She just gave him a soft, incredibly pitying smile.

Elisa stepped out into the hallway.

Bang.

The heavy oak door slammed shut, the sound echoing like a gunshot in the quiet corridor. The physical barrier fell, permanently severing Elisa Wilder from the Chambers empire.

Inside the penthouse, August stood completely alone. The silence of the massive apartment suddenly felt incredibly heavy. A strange, cold spike of panic pierced his chest, a terrifying, irrational thought that he had just made the biggest mistake of his life.

But then Allena's voice whined through the phone
speaker, and he pushed the panic down, burying it under his own arrogance. He was August Chambers.He was untouchable.
###Chapter 71


The heavy oak door of the penthouse had slammed shut, but the echo of it was already gone.Germaine's eviction order had been intended to break her, to leave her stranded and helpless. It had failed spectacularly. On the cold, windswept drive out of Manhattan, Elisa had made only a single, encrypted call. "Alastair," she'd said, her voice a blade of ice."Activate Plan B."①

Now, twenty-four hours later, she stood not in a university but in the sterile, high-security heart of Aethel Intelligence's primary East Coast data haven.The air didn't smell of chalk dust or old books; it smelled of ozone, chilled server racks, and cold, hard facts. Alastair had fast-tracked her access, using a board-level emergency directive to grant her temporary operational command while the formal paperwork was being rammed through legal. The first step of her revenge was simply to live better, to stand higher, and to prove that their cage could never hold her.
She wore a pristine white lab coat over her black athletic gear. She stood in front of a massive holographic data table. Her fingers flew across the virtual keyboard, adjusting the neural interface algorithms for Project Chiron. The glowing blue light of the data streams reflected in her dark, unblinking eyes. She was completely, utterly focused. The physical pain in her bruised lower back was pushed deep down, buried under layers of pure, mechanical concentration.

A sharp beep from the laboratory's smart access door shattered the quiet hum of the servers.

Alastair's executive assistant, a woman named Anya with a perpetually severe expression, practically ran into the room. Her usually perfect posture was gone.Her face was flushed, and she was clutching a tablet so tightly her knuckles were white.

"Faye," Anya said, her voice dropping to a harsh,anxious whisper. "Mr. Cromwell just called from the top floor. He's on his way down here right now. He is absolutely furious."

Elisa's fingers paused over the glowing blue keys. A slight frown creased her forehead.
"Is there a calculation error in the Chiron algorithm?"Elisa asked, her voice calm and steady/. "Or did the JHU ethics board request supplementary data?"

Anya shook her head rapidly, her expression a mix of pity and panic.

"Neither," Anya said. "It's the public relations department. They forwarded a massive pile of entertainment gossip blogs to his office. The articles are calling you a homewrecker. They are saying you destroyed someone's marriage. He read them, and his face went completely cold. I've never seen him like that."

Elisa's stomach gave a slow, cold twist.

The temperature in her veins dropped. She knew exactly what this was. It was the fallout from Devon's multi-million dollar smear campaign, the digital execution August had allowed, and the direct result of the non-disclosure agreement Germaine had forced her to sign. They had locked her in a cage and were now throwing stones at her in public.

She reached out and tapped a single button. The holographic data panels instantly collapsed into a
single,secure line of code.

"I understand," Elisa said. Her voice was terrifyingly flat. "I will explain it to him."

Before Anya could say another word, the heavy electronic doors of the lab hissed open violently.

Alastair Cromwell marched into the room. His usually immaculate suit jacket was gone, his tie loosened.His face was a mask of dark, dangerous calm.

Right behind him walked two massive Aethel corporate security guards, their hands resting on their belts. Alastair had clearly marched straight out of a board meeting, ready fora war.

He stopped in the middle of the room. His sharp eyes locked onto Elisa. For a fraction of a second, the pure, corporate rage in his face softened into a look of deep, personal concern. But it was instantly swallowed by a much darker, protective fury.

He walked right up to her. He looked at her with the intense, scrutinizing gaze of a surgeon examining a fatal wound.

"Faye," Alastair demanded, his voice booming in the
sterile room. "This absolute garbage on the internet.Is any of it true?"

Elisa didn't look away. She met her boss's furious gaze with absolute, unshakeable calm.

"The media reports are maliciously edited and entirely out of context," Elisa said, her voice steady."But I am, in fact, currently navigating a very hostile...marriage dispute."

Alastair let out a heavy, disgusted snort that echoed off the metal walls.

"A marriage dispute?" Alastair barked. "Do you know what those articles are calling you? A manipulative,calculating nurse who slept her way into a billionaire's life! They are trying to destroy your entire professional existence!"

He spun around so fast his white coat flared out behind him.He pointed a shaking finger at Anya.

"Anya!" Alastair ordered, his voice vibrating with authority. "Any collaboration request, any funding proposal, any communication whatsoever from the Chambers family or their corporate entities-you block it! You throw it in the incinerator! They do not
exist to Aethel Intelligence!"

Anya nodded quickly,pulling out her digital notepad.

Suddenly, the intercom on the wall lit up with a bright yellow light. The top-floor receptionist's voice crackled through the speaker, sounding slightly intimidated.

"Mr. Cromwell, I apologize for the interruption," the receptionist said. "But Mr. August Chambers and Ms.Allena Mills are currently in the lobby. They are requesting an immediate audience. They said they have urgent business to discuss."

Alastair's thick dark eyebrows shot up. A terrifying,freezing light exploded in his eyes.

"They actually have the audacity to walk through my front doors?" Alastair hissed, his hands curling into tight fists.

Elisa's heart gave a slow, heavy thump against her ribs. She knew exactly why August was here. He was trying to buy Allena a spot in the most prestigious medical Al project in the country to solidify her fake "hero" status.
A siow, incredibly cold smile curved the corners of Elisa's mouth.

Alastair saw the look on her face. The raw anger in his eyes shifted into a sharp, calculating understanding.

"What do you want me to do, Faye?" Alastair asked quietly.

Elisa took a step closer to him. Her voice was soft,but it carried the lethal edge of a razor blade.

"Alastair," Elisa whispered. "Please, let them in. Some theatrical performances need to be finished in person."

Alastair stared into her black eyes for a long moment.He gave a single, sharp nod. He pressed the button on the intercom.

"Send them up," Alastair commanded. "Put them in the main client reception area on this floor."

Five minutes later, the secondary doors slid open.

August Chambers walked in. He was wearing a perfectly tailored charcoal suit, projecting absolute,billionaire dominance. He was holding Allena's hand.
Allena was wearing a designer pastel dress, walking with a slight, exaggerated limp in her medical boot,her face painted with a mask of perfect, humble sweetness.

The second August stepped into the room, his dark eyes landed on Elisa standing next to the data table.

His jaw instantly clenched. A flash of dark, ugly shock rippled across his face, followed immediately by a surge of arrogant annoyance. He clearly thought Elisa had run here to hide and cry to her new boss.He quickly masked his reaction, lifting his chin in a postureof supreme superiority.

Allena didn't even notice Elisa at first. She immediately stepped forward, dropping August's hand, and bowed deeply toward Alastair.

"Mr. Cromwell," Allena said, her voice dripping with thick, sugary adoration. "I have viewed you as the ultimate beacon of the tech community for my entire life. To be able to visit your headquarters in person is the greatest honor I could ever imagine."

Alastair didn't even look at her.
He didn't blink. He didn't acknowledge her bow. His
eyes bypassed her completely and locked onto August like a pair of sniper scopes.

"Mr. Chambers," Alastair said. His voice was a flat,freezing slab of ice. "I believe I have made it incredibly clear in the past. The doors to my company are not open to unsolicited pitches."①

August didn't flinch. He offered a smooth, practiced,incredibly fake smile. He reached up and casually adjusted his platinum cufflink.

"Mr. Cromwell, I assure you, this is not a corporate visit," August said smoothly. "I am here strictly in a personal capacity. I am here to discuss the personnel selection for the JHU joint venture. Her talent, her clinical experience, and her absolute passion for medicine will undoubtedly be a valuable asset."

Alastair let out a harsh, scraping laugh that sounded like metal tearing.

"Talent? Passion?" Alastair repeated, his voice dripping with pure, corporate disgust. "In this room,Mr. Chambers, I only recognize one standard.Absolute academic rigor and a completely flawless,unassailable background."
He finally turned his head. His sharp eyes sliced over Allena's designer dress, stripping away her fake humility in a single second.

"To be involved with an Aethel project," Alastair stated, his voice dropping to a lethal, vibrating pitch,"requires the most brutal background investigation on the planet. We verify academic history. We verify clinical ethics. And most importantly... we verify personal character."

The fake, sweet smile instantly froze on Allena's face.

A cold sweat broke out on the back of her neck. She instinctively looked back at August, her eyes wide with sudden,raw panic.

August frowned slightly, stepping closer to Allena to project a shield of wealth.

"A background check will not be an issue," August said, his tone hardening with irritation. "Ms. Mills is a recognized star in the medical community. Her record is spotless."

Alastair didn't argue with him. He simply turned his head and looked at Elisa. He spoke in a tone that only the two of them fully understood.
"Faye," Alastair asked, his voice echoing in the quiet room. "What is your professional assessment? Does a person with a spotless record usually find themselves at the dead center of a massive,disgusting tabloid scandal?"

Elisa looked at Allena. She saw the absolute terror vibrating in the other woman's eyes.

Elisa's lips parted. A tiny, freezing smirk touched her face.

"Alastair," Elisa said, her voice smooth and deadly."A resume can be bought. A public image can be fabricated by a PR firm. But some things cannot be packaged. For example... whether or not a person possesses the basic human decency to respect another woman's marriage and family."
###Chapter 72


The words hung in the cold, sterilized air of the Aethel data center.

Elisa's statement about respecting marriage and family acted like a physical needle, sliding directly under Allena's ribs and piercing her lunngs.

Allena's face drained of all color, turning a sickly,ashen white. Her chest heaved. She forced her frozen facial muscles to stretch into a painful,trembling smile.

"Ms. Wilder," Allena stammered, her voice high and tight with panic. "I think... I think you might have a severe misunderstanding about me. I hold the sanctity of marriage in the highest possible regard."

Alastair let out a sudden, sharp scoff.

It wasn't a loud noise, but in the absolute quiet of the reception area, it sounded like a gunshot. The room instantly fell into a suffocating, dead silence.

He reached into the breast pocket of his shirt. He
moved with agonizing, deliberate slowness. He pulled out his sleek, black smartphone.

He unlocked the screen and tapped open a file he had received just that morning. He held it up, angling the screen so August and Allena could see it clearly.It was a detailed background report.

"Allena Mills," Alastair read aloud. He chewed on the syllables, his tone dripping with dark, intellectual mockery."Mills. That is fascinating. According to this preliminary data provided by my analysts, the primary beneficiary of the Sinclair Family Trust...does not share that surname."

The temperature in the room plummeted to absolute zero.

Allena's body violently jerked as if she had been hit by a live electrical wire. August's jaw locked so. hard the muscles in his neck bulged.

The Sinclair family was Allena's biological father's family. It was the family that had abandoned Elisa's mother. Allena's current public persona was built entirely on the name 'Mills' to hide her illegitimate origins. Alastair had just casually, brutally ripped the
floorboards out from under her entire existence.

August immediately stepped in front of Allena, his broad shoulders blocking Alastair's view of her. His dark eyes burned with aggressive, billionaire entitlement.

"Cromwell," August demanded, his voice a low,threatening rumble. "I warn you, stop right there.Allena's private affairs have absolutely zero
relevance to this project, and it is certainly not a topic you are permitted to publicly humiliate her with here. If you say another single word about her origins, the Chambers Foundation will immediately re-evaluate all of our funding to JHU."

Alastair slowly lowered his phone. He pointed the corner of the device directly at August's chest.

"Zero relevance?" Alastair repeated, his voice slicing through the air like a scalpel. "In my company, Mr.Chambers, a person's fundamental character and the truth of their origins are intimately connected. We do not build Al on a foundation of lies."

He shifted his gaze around August's shoulder, his eyes locking onto Allena with the terrifying intensity
of a predator.

"My intelligence division is quite thorough," Alastair said, his voice dropping to a freezing whisper. "And my analysts have flagged a very disturbing pattern. It seems a certain illegitimate daughter of the Sinclair family has developed a very passionate hobby for...squatting in another's nest. For intervening in other people's homes."

Allena let out a pathetic, strangled gasp. Her fingernails dug so deeply into the palms of her hands that the skin nearly broke.

"Mr. Cromwell, no!" Allena cried out, real tears of panic finally spilling over her eyelashes. "That is malicious media slander! August and I are..we are..."

"I have absolutely no interest in hearing your pathetic excuses," Alastair barked, cutting her off with brutal force. "I only want to know one thing. How can l possibly trust the clinical data of a woman who cannot even be honest about her own legal surname?"

He didn't wait for her to answer. He turned sharply to his assistant.
"Anya," Alastair ordered. "Initiate a full, unrestricted background sweep on Ms. Mills. I want every single academic publication from the last ten years. I want the raw clinical data she claims to have collected.And I want a comprehensive pull of any and all legal disputes involving her name."

Anya nodded sharply. "Right away, sir." She turned on her heel and marched toward the secure records room.

Total, suffocating panic seized Allena's throat. She couldn't breathe. She knew exactly how much water was in her "academic achievements." If a man like Alastair Cromwell dug into her past, she wouldn't just be rejected; her medical license could be revoked.

She grabbed August's sleeve, her fingers trembling violently. She looked up at him with wide, desperate eyes,begging him to fix it.

August's face was a mask of dark, boiling fury. He hated being challenged. He hated his money being ignored.

He took a heavy step forward, invadng Alastair's personal space.
"Cromwell," August said, his voice dropping to a dangerous, vibrating threat. "The Chambers Group is one of the most significant financial partners to the entire tech and medical sector in this city. I strongly suggest you afford Ms. Mills the basic respect she deserves."

Alastair stared at the billionaire. For three full seconds, the room was dead silent.

Then, Alastair threw his head back and laughed.

It wasn't a polite laugh. It was a booming, echoing roar of pure, unadulterated mockery. The sound bounced off the metal walls, completely humiliating August in front of the entire room.

"Respect?" Alastair gasped, wiping a tear of amusement from his eye. "Mr. Chambers, do you honestly believe you can purchase my respect with a checkbook?"

The laughter vanished from his face instantly. His expression hardened into solid granite.

"My company respects exactly two things:undeniable truth and raw talent," Alastair growled. He raised his hand and pointed to a sleek, framed patent
certificate on the far wall. It was for the core Aethel Al predictive engine. "My company's valuation does not rely on the pathetic charity of arrogant trust-fund children."

August's face turned a violent, mottled shade of purple. He had never, in his entire life, been spoken to like this in a professional setting. His hands curled into massive fists at his sides.

Alastair ignored him completely. He turned his terrifying gaze back to Allena.

"Furthermore," Alastair said, his voice dripping with venom. "Regarding your background check, I have one additional, mandatory requirement."

Allena swallowed hard, her throat clicking audibly in the quiet room. "What...what requirement?"

Alastair leaned forward, resting his hands on the reception desk. He looked at her like she was a disease under a microscope.

"I require a fully documented, written statement detailing the exact timeline and nature of your relationship with Mr. Chambers," Alastair demanded.

He paused, letting the weight of his words crush her.

"I need to know the exact date you inserted yourself into his life," Alastair continued, pronouncing every word with agonizing clarity. "'And, more specifically, I need it in writing whether or not he was legally married to another woman at the time of your insertion."

The demand was a lethal, public execution. It ripped away the last microscopic shred of Allena's dignity.

Allena's face flushed a dark, humiliating red. Her entire body shook. "You... you cannot humiliate me like this!" she sobbed.

"Humiliate you?" Alastair asked coldly. "I am simply protecting my institution. I refuse to allow a person who specializes in squatting in another's nest to contaminate my projects."

The phrase hit Allena like a physical blow to the stomach. She let out a loud, ugly wail, burying her face in her hands as the tears poured down her cheeks.

August couldn't take it anymore. He grabbed Allena by the shoulders and pulled her firmly behind his
back. He glared at Alastair with pure, homicidal rage.

"Enough!" August roared, his voice shaking the glass partitions. "Your arrogance is absolutely sickening,Cromwell!"

Alastair didn't flinch. He stood perfectly straight, his eyes burning with righteous fury.

"Arrogance?" Alastair fired back. "Mr. Chambers, I am simply refusing to let my company become a sanctuary for filth."

He turned away from the billionaire, completely dismissing his existence. He looked at Elisa. His voice instantly softened back into a professional tone.

"Faye," Alastair said. "Show them the door. We have wasted enough oxygen on this."
###Chapter 73


Elisa stood perfectly still by the holographic data table. She watched the absolute destruction of August's ego and Allena's fake persona. Her heart beat with a slow, steady, incredibly satisfying rhythm.

She walked over to Alastair's side. She gave him a small,respectful nod.

"I will handle it, sir," Elisa said softly.

She turned to face August. Her black eyes were completely devoid of emotion. She looked at him not as a husband, not even as an enemy, but as a piece of trash that needed to be swept out of her workspace.

"Mr. Chambers," Elisa said, her voice a flat, freezing monotone. "I believe this meeting has concluded.The exit is behind you."

August stared at her. His chest heaved with violent,erratic breaths. The humiliation of being rejected by Alastair was burning a hole in his brain, but seeing
Elisa-his wife, the woman he thought he had crushed-standing there with such cold, untouchable authority completely shattered his control.

A dark, vicious, retaliatory light ignited in August's eyes. He let out a harsh, cruel laugh.

"Cromwell," August sneered, pointing a large,accusing finger directly at Elisa's face. "You talk about filth? You might want to look a little closer at your prized student. You have absolutely no idea who is standing next to you."

August took a step forward, his voice rising to a furious shout.

"She is the one with the rotting character!" August roared, his face twisting with malice. "She is the one obsessed with money! She is the one trying to destroy a genuine relationship out of pure, psychotic jealousy! Those news articles about her being a deranged stalker? I didn't invent them. They are the absolute truth!"

The air in the data center instantly turned to gasoline.

Alastair's face went completely rigid. The veins in his
neck popped against his skin. He slammed his open palm down onto the metal reception desk with a force that made the heavy computer monitors violently shake.

"Absolute garbage!" Alastair bellowed, his voice exploding like a bomb in the confined space.

He marched around the desk, closing the distance between himself and the billionaire. He pointed his finger inches from August's nose.

"Mr. Chambers," Alastair warned, his voice a low,terrifying growl. "I will give you exactly one warning.You are standing in my territory. And in my territory,insulting Faye is a direct, unforgivable insult to me!"

August physically recoiled from the sheer, dominant force of the CEO's anger, but his massive ego refused to back down.

"Facts are facts!" August yelled back, his face flushed. "Allena is the innocent victim here! Elisa is a liar!"

Alastair let out a sound of pure, aggressive disgust.He reached into his pocket and pulled out his smartphone. He remembered the encrypted file he
had prepared early this morning, along with the brief mental note: 'Just in case they try to leverage their way in.' His thumb swiped rapidly across the screen.He found what he was looking for and shoved the glowing screen directly into August's line of sight.

"Read it," Alastair commanded.

On the screen was a highly classified, encrypted PDF document. The bold black header read: Financial Investigation Report: Associated Transactions of the Sinclair Family Trust and Chambers Group.

"According to this preliminary financial sweep,"Alastair read aloud, his voice dripping with lethal precision, "Ms. Allena Mills occupies a highly lucrative, heavily shielded beneficiary position within a Sinclair family trust fund. A fund that was established and funded years before she supposedly 'met' you, Mr. Chambers."

Allena's sobbing instantly stopped.

Her breath hitched in her throat. Her face turned the color of dead ash. The report Alastair was holding proved she wasn't a poor, innocent victim who had stumbled into love. It proved she was a deeply
embedded financial parasite, tied to the very capital that had destroyed Elisa's mother.

Alastair lowered the phone. His eyes locked onto Allena with the cold, dead stare of an executioner.

"So, Ms. Mills," Alastair said, his voice dropping to a whisper that chilled the blood. "How much longer do you plan to perform this pathetic 'victim' routine?"

Allena's entire body began to violently shake. The absolute exposure of her lies shattered her fragile mind. She let out a piercing, hysterical scream.

"That is a lie!" Allena shrieked, tears and mascara running down her face. "You are slandering me! Elisa gave you that! She is just jealous of me!"

She lunged forward, pointing a shaking, venomous finger at Elisa.

"You are just a pathetic nurse!" Allena screamed, her voice cracking with pure hatred. "Why are you even standing in a room like this? You have absolutely no right to judge me!"

Alastair's patience completely snapped.

He took a massive step forward, putting his body
between Elisa and Allena. He raised his hand, his finger pointing directly between Allena's eyes. When he spoke, his voice was a roar of absolute, terrifying thunder.

"Shut your mouth!" Alastair exploded.

His finger trembled with rage, inches from Allena's tear-stained face.

"Homewrecker!" Alastair spat the word out like it was poison on his tongue. "In my eyes, intervening in a legally married household is an act of sheer social parasitism. My company does not study parasites,Mr. Chambers, and we certainly do not incubate them. That is my complete, final, and absolute professional assessment of your character!"

The word parasite echoed off the sterile metal walls.It hung in the air, heavy and undeniable. The two security guards by the door physically winced.

August looked as if he had been struck by lightning.He staggered back half a step. He could not process what was happening. A globally respected, billionaire tech titan was standing in his own headquarters,screaming the most degrading, humiliating insult
possible directly into the face of the woman August loved.②

"You cannot speak to her like that!" August roared,reaching out and grabbing Alastair's forearm in a desperate attempt to stop the verbal slaughter.

Alastair violently ripped his arm out of August's grip.He looked at the billionaire with a level of disgust usually reserved for cockroaches.

"Mr. Chambers," Alastair said, his chest heaving."You are a legally married man. And this woman is standing in my company, publicly parading herself as your romantic partner. In my dictionary, what exactly does that make her?"

Alastair leaned in, pronouncing every single syllable with agonizing, brutal force.

"It. Makes. Her. A. Homewrecker."

Allena let out a hollow, devastated whimper. Her knees buckled. She swayed heavily against August's side, practically collapsing into his arms, her face buried in his expensive suit jacket.

A dark, chaotic madness flared in August's eyes. He
whipped his head around and glared at Elisa. His eyes were bloodshot, filled with a homicidal rage.

"Elisa!" August snarled, his voice shaking. "You orchestrated this! What lies did you feed him?!"

Elisa stood by the data table. She looked at the pathetic, broken couple clinging to each other. Her face was a mask of absolute, chilling indifference.

"I didn't have to say a single word, Mr. Chambers,"Elisa replied softly. "The truth doesn't need my help to speak."

Alastair turned his back on them. He walked over to the reception desk and sat down, instantly resuming his posture as the untouchable corporate authority.

"Get out," Alastair ordered, not looking up. "My company does not welcome homewreckers. And it certainly does not welcome the weak men who protect them."

August's chest heaved violently. His breathing was ragged. He wrapped his arm tightly around Allena's waist, holding her up. He stared at Elisa with a look of pure,toxic hatred.
"Fine," August whispered, his voice a dark, vibrating promise of violence."Remember this day, Elisa."

Elisa met his gaze. Her black eyes were as cold as the deep ocean.

"I will," Elisa said. "It was the most entertaining moral execution I have ever witnessed."

August ground his teeth together so hard the sound was audible. He turned around, practically dragging the sobbing Allena toward the heavy electronic doors.

Just as they reached the threshold, Allena suddenly stopped. She twisted her head around. Her eyes were red, swollen, and filled with a demonic,venomous spite.

"You won't win this, Elisa Wilder," Allena hissed, her voice a raspy, ugly sound. "I swear to God, I will make you crawl on your knees and beg for my forgiveness."

Elisa didn't flinch. She didn't argue. She simply tilted her head slightlya tiny, pitying smile touching her lips. She didn't say a word:①

The heavy metal doors hissed shut, sealing with a
loud, final click. The pathetic, screaming performance was over. The data center was finally clean again.
###Chapter 74


The harsh fluorescent lights of the Aethel Intelligence hallway buzzed overhead.

Allena leaned heavily against the cool glass wall,clutching a crumpled tissue. She wiped the black streaks of mascara from her cheeks. The hysterical,pathetic sobbing she had displayed inside the lab vanished the exact second the heavy doors closed behind them.

Her face, previously pale and trembling, hardened into a mask of dark,ugly calculation.

She looked up at August. His jaw was clenched so tight the muscles twitched. He was staring blankly at the closed data center doors, his chest rising and falling with suppressed rage.

"August," Allena whispered, letting a slight,manufactured tremble enter her voice. "What are we going to do? Mr. Cromwell... the things he called me.How could he use that word?"①
August wrapped a heavy armm around her shoulders,pulling her against his side. His voice was a cold,vibrating hum of billionaire arrogance.

"Don't waste your tears on him, Allena," August ordered sharply. "Cromwell is just an arrogant tech bro who thinks his stock price makes him a god. We don't need his pathetic company. I will buy you a better one."

Allena bit her lower lip, her eyes darting nervously down the hallway.

"But my reputation, August," she whined softly."The people in his company heard him. They heard him call me a... a homewrecker."

August's eyes darkened. "I will have the PR team bury any rumors before they start. And as for Elisa..."He paused, a vicious, retaliatory light flashing in his eyes. "I will make sure she chokes on her own arrogance."

Allena looked down at the polished concrete floor. A toxic, venomous thought began to bloom in her mind.

"August, I have to tell you something I saw with my own eyes," Allena lied smoothly, her eyes wide with
fake innocence. "That day at Aethel headquarters, I saw Alastair Cromwell coming out of his private office, and Elisa was right there inside... his tie was crooked, August. You know exactly what that means.She isn't using her technical skills to pave her way.She's paying for it on her back."

August's pupils violently contracted.

A sudden, sickening jolt of pure, irrational jealousy punched him directly in the stomach. The image of Elisa-his wife, the woman who had just looked at him with absolute disgust-being touched by Alastair Cromwell made his blood boil. But his massive ego instantly violently rejected the idea.

"Don't be ridiculous," August snapped, his voice harsher than he intended. "Cromwell is a ruthless capitalist. He doesn't trade corporate power for cheap favors. He only cares about profit."

Allena saw the flash of jealousy in his eyes before he buried it. She didn't push the issue with him. She didn't need to. The seed of the lie was planted. Now,she just needed to water it.

Three hours later. Manhattan.
The glass-walled executive office of Aethel Intelligence headquarters floated high above the city,silent and immaculate.

Alastair Cromwell sat behind his massive, minimalist desk. He was reviewing a complex string of code on his monitor. His face was a mask of relaxed, absolute control.

The intercom on his desk chimed softly.

"Mr. Cromwell," his executive assistant said. "Ms.Allena Mlills is in the lobby. She is requesting an urgent meeting regarding the upcoming joint venture with Johns Hopkins University."

Alastair raised a single, dark eyebrow.

He knew exactly what had happened in the data center that morning. Elisa had already texted him a brief, highly amusing summary of his explosive execution of Allena's character. The fact that Allena was now showing up at his office meant she was desperately looking for a new angle of attack.

"Send her up," Alastair said, his voice smooth. "But tell her I only have exactly ten minutes before my next board meeting."
A few minutes later, the heavy glass doors to the office swung open.

Allena walked in. She was wearing a fresh, perfectly tailored designer suit. Her makeup was flawless.There was absolutely no trace of the weeping,broken woman from the morning. She wore a bright,confident, entirely fake smile.

"Mr. Cromwell," Allena said, walking toward the desk with an extended hand. "Thank you so much for squeezing me into your busy schedule."

Alastair didn't stand up. He didn't reach for her hand.He simply gestured to the leather chair opposite his desk.

"Have a seat, Ms. Mills," Alastair said, his tone polite but completely devoid of warmth. He began tapping his index finger rhythmically against the polished wood of his desk. "You have nine minutes. What can l do for you?"

Allena awkwardly pulled her hand back and sat down. She maintained her bright smile, but a flicker of irritation crossed her eyes.

"I will get straight to the point," Allena said, leaning
forward slightly. "I am here to discuss the personnel structure of the Aethel and JHU joint medical Al project. I have some... serious concerns."

Alastair's tapping finger paused for a fraction of a second, then resumed its steady rhythm. "Concerns?Please,enlighten me."

Allena lowered her voice, adopting a tone of deep,professional concern.

"It is regarding Elisa Wilder," Allena said, watching Alastair's face closely for a reaction. "I understand she has been appointed to a consulting role. But Mr.Cromwell, I feel it is my duty to warn you. Her professional background is severely lacking. And worse...her personal conduct is highly problematic."

Alastair stopped tapping his finger entirely. He leaned back in his chair. His eyes, usually warm and amused, turned into two chips of sharp flint.

"Problematic?" Alastair repeated softly."How so?"

Allena took his quiet tone as encouragement. She leaned in closer, her voice dripping with toxic sincerity.
"You are a busy man, Mr. Cromwell, you might be blind to the gossip," Allena whispered. "But Elisa's private life is a complete disaster. She is manipulative. She uses her... physical assets to secure favors from powerful men. She is, quite frankly, a cancer in the industry. If you allow her to remain on the core team, she will inevitably drag Aethel's pristine reputation through the mud."

Alastair stared at her.

His facial expression didn't change a single millimeter. But deep inside his chest, a massive,overwhelming wave of pure, aristocratic disgust rolled through him.

He knew exactly who Elisa was. He knew she was Faye, the genius architect who had built his company's most valuable asset. He knew she had spent seven years enduring psychological torture to protect her child.

And now, this woman-a literal parasite who was currently sleeping with a married man and stealing credit for medical miracles-was sitting in his office,trying to accuse Elisa of sleeping her way to the top.It was so profoundly hypocritical it bordered on
comedy.

Alastair slowly placed his hands flat on his desk. He looked at Allena with eyes that held absolutely zero respect.

"Ms. Mills," Alastair said, his voice a smooth,terrifyingly calm purr. "I appreciate your... creative storytelling.However, Aethel Intelligence utilizes a highly sophisticated, proprietary metric for evaluating our personnel."

He stood up, buttoning his suit jacket in a clear,undeniable gesture of dismissal.

"We evaluate based on raw genius," Alastair stated coldly. "Not tabloid fiction. Now, if you will excuse me, my nine minutes are up."

Allena's fake smile shattered. She stared at him, her face flushing with humiliation. She realized instantly that her poison hadn't worked.

She quickly stood up, grabbing her designer purse."Mr. Cromwell, for the sake of the project's success,I strongly urge you to reconsider my warning."

Alastair didn't even look at her. He picked up a file
from his desk. "Goodbye, Ms. Mills."

Allena turned and practically fled the office, her heels clicking angrily against the hardwood floor.

The second the glass doors closed behind her,Alastair let out a heavy sigh, running a hand over his face to wipe away the feeling of slime she had left in the room. He picked up his encrypted secure phone and hit a single speed-dial button.

The line connected instantly.

"Faye," Alastair said, a dark chuckle rumbling in his chest. "I just had the most fascinating ten minutes.Your 'sister' just left my office. She came all the way here to warn me that you are a cancer in the tech industry who sleeps her way to the top."

A soft, freezing laugh echoed through the phone speaker.

"Is that right?" Elisa's voice was calm and laced with lethal amusement. "She really overestimates my destructive capabilities. A cancer slowly kills the host. I have no intention of being that slow. I prefer to be the scalpel that cuts the tumor out entirely."
###Chapter 75


The heavy velvet curtains in the study of the Chambers Manor were drawn tight, blocking out the afternoon sun. The room smelled of expensive scotch and old leather.

August stood behind his massive mahogany desk.He was staring down at Allena, who was sitting on the edge of the leather sofa. Her eyes were still slightly red, and she was aggressively twisting a tissue between her manicured fingers. She had just finished recounting her humiliating rejection at Alastair Cromwell's office, carefully editing the story to make herself sound like a victim of corporate bullying.

A dark, protective rage boiled in August's chest. He hated seeing her upset. More than that, he hated the idea that Elisa and Alastair were sitting in their glass towers, looking down on him and his choices.

He walked around the desk and crouched down in front of Allena. He reached out and took her trembling hands in his.
"Allena," August said, his voice a low, absolute promise. "Stop crying. Cromwell is a fool, just like Blevins. If they won't open the door for you, I will simply buy the entire building and tear the door off its hinges."

Allena sniffled, looking up at him through her thick eyelashes. "What do you mean, August?"

August stood up. He walked back to his desk and tapped the screen of his secure laptop. He opened a highly classified corporate file.

"Aethel Intelligence and the Johns Hopkins University Medical School are officially launching their joint Al diagnostic project this week," August stated, his eyes scanning the data. "It is the most heavily funded,high-profile medical tech initiative of the decade.It makes Aethel's in-house R&D look like a high school science fair."

Allena's eyes instantly widened. The greed in her chest flared hot and bright. "The Project Chiron initiative?"

"Exactly," August said, a cold, arrogant smile touching his lips. "I am going to secure you a position
on the JHU side of the collaboration team. You will be directly involved in the core research. You will have the prestige, the access, and the public platform."

Allena stood up, ignoring the pain in her booted foot.She threw her arms around August's neck, pressing her body against his.

"August,you are amazing!" she breathed into his ear.But then she pulled back slightly, a frown creasing her forehead. "But... Cromwell is running the Aethel side. He hates me. He will never allow it."

August let out a harsh, dismissive scoff.

"Cromwell is a capitalist," August sneered, his massive ego blinding him to reality. "He worships money. And JHU is an academic institution; they worship funding. There is no moral high ground that a large enough check cannot level."

He reached for the heavy brass telephone on his desk and hit the button for his executive secretary.

"Get the Dean of the JHU Medical School on the line immediately," August commanded. "And contact the legal department. Tell them to draft a targeted,
restricted-use donation agreement. Fifty million dollars. Earmarked exclusively for the Aethel joint Al project."

Meanwhile, fifty miles away in Manhattan.

The main conference room at Aethel Intelligence was bathed in bright, cold LED light. Elisa sat at the head of the long glass table. She wore a sharp, black tailored blazer. Her posture was perfect, radiating absolute,terrifying competence.①

Alastair sat next to her, sliding a thick, freshly printed legal dossier across the glass.

"The JHU board just finalized the collaboration roster," Alastair said, his voice tight with annoyance."They officially signed off on you acting as the Chief Technical Advisor for Aethel. You have full operational control over the Chiron algorithm integration."

Elisa picked up the dossier. She flipped past the executive summaries, her black eyes scanning the dense columns of names and titles with machine-like speed.

Suddenly, her eyes stopped. Her finger traced a
single line of text on the third page.
JHU Clinical Data Consultant: Dr. Allena Mills.

Elisa's jaw tightened. A cold, sharp breath hissed through her teeth.

"Why is her name on this list?" Elisa asked, her voice dropping to a freezing, dangerous whisper.

Alastair leaned back in his chair, rubbing the bridge of his nose. He looked exhausted.

"Because capital is a virus, Faye," Alastair explained grimly. "August Chambers just wired a fifty-million-dollar 'special research grant' directly to the JHU Dean's office. The donation came with a very strong,legally ambiguous 'recommendation' that Ms. Mills be included on the team to provide clinical oversight."

Elisa let out a dark, humorless laugh.

"Fifty million dollars," Elisa murmured, staring at the name on the paper. "He is literally burning his family's legacy to buy a participation trophy for his mistress."①

Alastair looked at her, his eyes filled with genuine
concern.
"I can fight this, Faye," Alastair offered, his voice firm."I can threaten to pull Aethel out of the joint venture entirely unless JHU removes her. I will not let August force his garbage into your workspace."

Elisa slowvly closed the dossier. She placed her hands flat on the glass table. The anger in her chest evaporated, replaced by a cold, absolute, predatory calm.

"No," Elisa said. Her voice was crystal clear. "Do not pull the plug.Let her stay."

Alastair frowned in confusion. "Are you sure? August is going to use this to pave a golden road for her right over your head."

Elisa turned her head and looked out the floor-to-ceiling windows at the sprawling Manhattan skyline.

"The wider the road he paves, the harder the concrete will be when she face-plants into it," Elisa said softly. "She wants to play in the major leagues?Fine.Let her step onto the field. This isn't a charity gala, Alastair. This is a battlefield built on raw code and clinical data. This is my domain."
She stood up, her black blazer snapping sharply.

"Notify Professor Finch at JHU," Elisa commanded,her eyes burning with a lethal light. "Tell him the official project kickoff meeting is in exactly three days. I want every single member of the core team in that room. I want them to see exactly who holds the keys to the kingdom."

Alastair smiled. It was a dark, predatory smile that matched hers perfectly. "Consider it done. I'll have the legal and PR teams on standby."

"One more thing," Elisa said, walking toward the door."I need a complete, unredacted data dump of every single piece of medical research Allena Mills has ever published. I want to know exactly how empty her brain is before I start dismantling it in public."

Alastair tapped his tablet. "Already done. I sent it to your secure server ten minutes ago. Spoiler alert,Faye: it's ninety percent ghostwritten fluff and zero original data."

Elisa paused at the door. A terrifying, freezing smirk touched her lips.
"Perfect," Elisa whispered. "Class is about to be in session."
###Chapter 76


The Dean's office at the Johns Hopkins University School of Medicine smelled of lemon polish and nervous sweat.

August Chambers sat in a high-backed leather chair,his posture radiating absolute, untouchable power.He casually slid a heavy,cream-colored folder across the polished mahogany desk.

The Dean, a balding man with wire-rimmed glasses,opened the folder. His eyes instantly locked onto the cashier's check nestled inside. The number 50,000,000.00 USD was printed in bold, undeniable black ink.

The Dean's breath hitched. He looked up, instantly pasting on a wide, incredibly accommodating smile.

"Mr. Chambers," the Dean practically gushed, his voice trembling slightly with greed. "Your generosity is simply staggering. The entire JHU medical network will forever be in your debt. This grant will revolutionize our Al research capabilities."
August didn't smile back. He looked at the Dean with the cold, evaluating eyes of a man buying a piece of meat.

"I expect the funds to be utilized efficiently," August said, his voice a low, authoritative rumble. "And, as we discussed on the phone, I have a minor...structural suggestion for the Aethel collaboration team."

The Dean swallowed hard. He knew exactly what this was. It was a bribe wrapped in a philanthropic bow.

"Of course, Mr. Chambers," the Dean said quickly,nodding his head. "We are always open to suggestions from our most esteemed benefactors."

"I strongly recommend that Allena Mills be integrated into the core team," August stated, leaning forward slightly to press his dominance. "Her clinical experience and her... unique perspective will be invaluable to the project's success."

The Dean hesitated for a fraction of a second. He knew Allena's reputation was currently burning in the tabloid fires. He knew Dr. Gottlieb had explicitly banned her from the building. But fifty million dollars
could buy a lot of fire extinguishers.

"I understand completely," the Dean agreed, closing the folder and pulling it safely to his side of the desk."We will create a specialized role for Ms. Mills immediately. I will personally coordinate with the Aethel representatives."

August stood up, adjusting his platinum cufflink. A cold, victorious smirk touched his lips. "Excellent. I knew you were a man of vision, Dean."

Three days later.

The primary executive conference room at JHU was packed. The air was thick with the hum of laptops and the tense energy of top-tier academics and tech executives.

Elisa stood at the front of the room, illuminated by the glow of a massive digital whiteboard. She wore a tailored black suit, her dark hair pulled back into a severe, professional knot. She held a digital stylus,rapidly drawing out the complex neural network architecture that would allow Project Chiron to interface with JHU's raw patient data.

Her voice was calm, authoritative, and razor-sharp.
She didn't use notes. She spoke the language of advanced algorithms with the fluency of a native speaker.

Sitting in the front row, Professor Sterling Finch-the lead researcher for JHU-was nodding vigorously,his eyes wide with genuine academic awe.

Sitting three seats down from Professor Finch was Allena.

Allena was wearing a pristine, incredibly expensive white blazer. She was sitting up perfectly straight,holding a pen over a blank notepad, desperately trying to look like she belonged there. But her eyes were glazed over. She couldn't understand a single word Elisa was saying. The complex math and architecture were completely alien to her. The only thing visible in her eyes was a dark, burning, toxic jealousy.

Elisa drew the final connection line on the board and set the stylus down.

"That concludes the data ingestion pathway," Elisa stated, looking out over the room. "Are there any questions regarding the security protocols?"
Professor Finch immediately started clapping. The rest of the JHU team joined in.

"Brilliant, Ms. Wilder," Professor Finch praised, his voice loud and enthusiastic. "The architecture is flawless. It completely bypasses the latency issues we were worried about."

Before Elisa could respond, the heavy double doors at the back of the conference room swung open.

The Dean walked in, followed closely by August Chambers.

The ambient chatter in the room died instantly.Elisa's black eyes locked onto August. She felt a cold, familiar spike of disgust in her stomach, but her face remained a mask of absolute stone. Alastair,sitting at the table, subtly shifted his weight, his eyes narrowing.

"Apologies for the interruption, everyone," the Dean announced, walking to the front of the room with a wide, artificial smile. "But I have a very exciting announcement regarding the future of this project."

The Dean turned and gestured grandly toward August.

"Thanks to a historic, fifty-million-dollar research grant from the Chambers Group," the Dean declared loudly, "we are expanding our operational capabilities."

A smattering of polite, incredibly awkward applause rippled through the academics. Everyone in the room knew what that money meant. They all looked at Allena.

"Furthermore," the Dean continued, his smile straining slightly. "After careful consideration, JHU has decided to invite Dr. Allena Mills to join the core collaboration team. She will serve as our... Clinical Data Consultant."

It was a perfectly fabricated, meaningless title. It sounded important enough to satisfy August's ego,but vague enough to keep her away from the actual coding.

Allena immediately stood up. She offered a gracious,sickeningly sweet smile to the room, nodding her head. When she looked at Elisa, her eyes flashed with pure, arrogant triumph. She had bought her way into Elisa's sanctuary.
Professor Finch's brow furrowed deeply. He looked at Elisa and Alastair, hisexpression filled with silent,apologetic agony. He was a scientist, not a politician;he hated this.

Alastair placed his hands on the table, preparing to stand up and verbally eviscerate the Dean.

But Elisa reached out and gently placed her hand over Alastair's wrist, stopping him.

She stepped away from the whiteboard. She walked slowly toward the center of the room, stopping.directly in front of the Dean and August. Her posture was perfectly relaxed, but she radiated a terrifying,lethal coldness.

"Mr. Chambers," Elisa said, her voice echoing clearly in the silent room. "We are all incredibly moved by your...financial generosity."

August lifted his chin, looking down at her with a smug, victorious sneer. "I am always happy to support genuine talent, Ms. Wilder."

Elisa didn't blink. She turned her gaze to the Dean.

"Dean," Elisa said, her voice dropping to a sharp,
interrogative pitch. "I have a critical question regarding Ms. Mills' new role. As the Chief Technical Advisor for Aethel, I need to know the exact parameters of a 'Clinical Data Consultant."

The Dean sweated slightly. "Well, Ms. Wilder, she will be providing...oversight."

"Does this oversight include administrative access to the core Chiron algorithm?" Elisa demanded,her words hitting like hammer strikes. "Does she have read-and-write permissions for the encrypted patient databases?"

The question was a lethal trap. If Allena had access,she could destroy the project.

The Dean panicked. He looked at August, then at Professor Finch. "Ah,well, the permissions are..."

Elisa cut him off, turning her terrifying gaze directly onto Professor Finch.

"Professor Finch," Elisa commanded, her voice ringing with absolute authority. "Is she touching my code?"

Professor Finch stood up. He took a deep breath, his

academic integrity finally overriding his fear of the Dean.

"No, Ms. Wilder," Professor Finch stated firmly,looking Elisa right in the eye. "Ms. Mills will be restricted to reviewing sanitized, preliminary clinical outputs. She will have absolutely zero access to the core algorithm, the secure servers, or the raw patient data. Her permissions are strictly read-only, and heavily firewalled."

Allena's smug smile instantly vanished. Her face flushed red with humiliation. She had been publicly demoted to a glorified proofreader in front of the entire team.

Elisa stared at Professor Finch for three long seconds.

"Academic purity requires constant vigilance,Professor," Elisa said, her voice a freezing whisper that carried across the room. "I expect that firewall to be made of solid titanium. Because if a single line of my code is compromised by incompetence, Aethel will burn this partnership to the ground."

Professor Finch nodded solemnly. "You have my

absolute guarantee, Ms. Wilder."

Elisa turned her back on August and Allena,completely dismissing them. She picked up her digital stylus. "Now. Let's get back to actual work."
###Chapter 77


The Grand Ballroom of the Waldorf Astoria was a sea of glittering crystal chandeliers, heavy silk drapery,and the suffocating scent of expensive champagne and predatory wealth.
