Elisa stared at him. A cold, hollow laugh bubbled up from her throat.
She pulled out her phone and held up the photo of
the crushed SUV.
"Are you completely devoid of basic physics,August?" Elisa asked, her voice vibrating with lethal mockery. "The impact crater is on the side of Jewel's car. The McLaren hit them. Not the other way around."
Jewel stood up from the bench. She ignored the pain in her head and pointed at Tate.
"Their driver was doing eighty in a thirty zone!"Jewel screamed across the room. "They blew straight through the red light! They hit us!"
Several police officers in the lobby turned their heads. The two cops guarding Jewel shifted uncomfortably. The skid marks at the scene perfectly matched Jewel's statement, and they knew it.

Before the tension could escalate, the precinct doors opened again.
Simon, August's lead executive assistant, hurried into the lobby. He was carrying a sleek leather briefcase. He walked straight to August and leaned in, whispering rapidly into his ear.
August gave a single, sharp nod.
Simon immediately turned and walked toward the precinct captain, who had just stepped out of his office. Simon handed the captain a thick folder stamped with the gold crest of the Chambers Group.
The captain opened the folder. He read the top
page. His face paled slightly. He swallowed hard,closed the folder, and cleared his throat loudly.
"Attention," the captain announced, his voice booming over the noise of the lobby. "We have a new development in this case."
The room went quiet.
"Our technical division just reported a critical malfunction," the captain stated, keeping his eyes fixed on the wall above Elisa's head. "The specific traffic cameras at the Queensboro intersection are older models. They failed to upload to the cloud server due to a localized network timeout, and the local storage drives were reportedly damaged during the impact. The footage is completely corrupted."
Elisa's pupils violently contracted.
Her brain processed the information in a microsecond. There was no network timeout.Simon had just used the massive political and financial leverage of the Chambers Group to fabricate a technical glitch and physically destroy the drives. He had erased the evidence in less than thirty minutes.
Jewel let out a scream of pure frustration. She lunged forward, grabbing the captain by the collar of his uniform.

"You corrupt pigs!" Jewel roared,spitting in his face."Check my dashcam! The camera in my car caught the whole thing!"
The captain roughly shoved Jewel backward.She stumbled and hit the wooden bench.
"Your dashcam was crushed in the side impact,"the captain said coldly, adjusting his collar. "The memory card is destroyed. We have no objective evidence."

Allena's lips twitched. A sickening, triumphant smirk flashed across her face before she quickly morphed it back into an expression of pure terror. She grabbed August's lapels.
"August, please," Allena begged, her voice trembling. "They tried to kill Tate. You have to get justice for us."
August reached up and slowly twisted his platinum cufflink. It was his signature gesture of absolute control. He looked at the captain.
"Captain," August commanded, his voice echoing with billionaire authority. "Until this attempted murder is fully investigated, I expect the suspect to be held without bail. She is a flight risk."
"Understood, Mr. Chambers," the captain nodded.He gestured to the two officers. They immediately stepped forward. They grabbed Jewel's arms,twisting them behind her back, and pulled out a pair of heavy steel handcuffs.
The violent movement jostled the bench.
Kayden woke up.
The little girl opened her eyes. The bright lights blinded her. She felt the burning fever in her brain
and saw the strange men grabbing Jewel. Kayden let out a weak, terrifying scream. Her tiny hand shot out and clamped onto the fabric of Elisa's coat.
Elisa's maternal instinct exploded.
She lunged forward. She shoved her hands hard against the chest of the nearest police officer,using her entire body weight to violently push him away from the bench. She stepped in front of Kayden, spreading her arms wide. The aura of violence radiating from her body was so intense the officers actually froze.
Elisa slowly turned her head. She locked her black eyes onto August.
"If you do not let my child go to a hospital right now," Elisa whispered. Every single syllable was coated in venom and absolute certainty. "I will make you pay a price you cannot even begin to comprehend."
August looked down at her. He saw the fire in her eyes, but his massive ego completely blinded him to the reality of the threat. He thought she was just using the kid as a shield to get her friend out of jail.
"No one is above the law in New York, Elisa,"August stated coldly. "Especially not someone who tries to murder Allena's family."
Elisa stared at his arrogant,self-righteous face.
The last, microscopic illusion she had ever held
about this man shattered into dust.

She turned her head and looked at Simon. Her eyes narrowed. The desperate mother vanished. Faye,the apex predator of the digital world, took over.

Elisa reached into her pocket. She pulled out her phone. Her tthumb flew across  the 10screen,bypassing the standard operating system. She tapped a sequence of hidden icons, bringing up a black screen filled with glowing green code.

She was ready to burn his empire to the ground.
###Chapter 93

Kayden's small body suddenly jerked.
A violent tremor ripped through the five-year-old.Her back arched off the wooden bench, her eyes rolling back into her head. The fever had spiked too high. She was going into a febrile convulsion.
Elisa's heart slammed against her ribs like a sledgehammer. The physical panic seized her throat, choking off her air. She did not have time to launch a complex cyberattack. She did not have time to wait for Alastair's lawyers. If she did not break this stalemate in the next sixty seconds,her daughter's brain would suffer permanent damage.
Elisa shoved her phone back into her pocket. The suffocating wave of maternal panic suddenly receded, leaving behind the freezing, hyper-rational calculations of Faye. Legal threats were useless here. Power was the only language August understood, and she needed a weapon that could instantly decapitate his leverage. Her mind raced through her digital arsenal, locking onto a specific file she had secured days ago.
She stood up. She did not look angry. She looked like a corpse. Her face was completely devoid of blood, her expression locked into a terrifying,unnatural stillness.
She walked directly toward August.
understood, and she needed a weapon that could instantly decapitate his leverage. Her mind raced through her digital arsenal, locking onto a specific file she had secured days ago.
She stood up. She did not look angry. She looked like a corpse. Her face was completely devoid of blood, her expression locked into a terrifying,unnatural stillness.
She walked directly toward August.
Two of August's massive bodyguards immediately stepped forward, raising their hands to block her path. August raised two fingers. The bodyguards instantly stepped back, parting like the Red Sea.August wanted to see her beg. He wanted to watch her break.

Elisa stopped exactly eighteen inches from his chest.

She raised her own phone. She tapped the screen once, opening an audio file she had transferred weeks ago. She cranked the volume to maximum.
She pressed play.
The chaotic noise of the precinct lobby was suddenly pierced by a crystal-clear, digital recording.
"Get rid of the video," August's deep, arrogant voice echoed from the phone speaker. "Wipe the servers. I don't care what it costs. No one can ever see that Allena faked that fall. We are going to pin the entire assault on Elisa."
It was the secret recording from the security room at the Belmont estate.
August's entire body went rigid. The blood completely drained from his face. His pupils dilated so fast his eyes looked entirely black. He stared at the small device in Elisa's hand as if it were a live hand grenade with the pin pulled.
Allena let out a sharp, suffocated gasp. She slapped both hands over her mouth,her eyes wide with absolute, paralyzing terror. Her knees buckled slightly, and she had to lean heavily against August's arm to keep from collapsing onto the floor.
Elisa hit pause.
The silence between the three of them was deafening.
Elisa looked up into August's shattered, horrified eyes. The arrogant billionaire had just been gutted.
"This audio file," Elisa whispered. Her voice was so low only August and Allena could hear it. "Is currently sitting on a secure, automated server. It is scheduled to mass-distribute in exactly fifteen minutes."
She leaned in half an inch closer.
"The recipient list includes the front-page editor of the New York Times, the senior columnist at the Wall Street Journal, and the lead investigator at the SEC."
August broke out in a cold sweat. A drop of
moisture rolled down his temple. He knew exactly what this meant. If the public heard him ordering the destruction of evidence to frame his wife, the scandal would be apocalyptic. The SEC would immediately halt trading on Chambers Group stock.The board of directors would crucify him.
And Allena's carefully crafted image as the innocent, heroic victim would be instantly incinerated. She would become a national joke.
August's jaw clenched so hard his teeth ground together. The muscles in his neck bulged.
"If you send that file," August hissed, his voice vibrating with desperate, cornered rage, "I will make sure you never see the light of day again. I will destroy you."
Elisa did not flinch. She smiled. It was a cruel, dead smile that showed teeth.
"If my daughter dies on that bench," Elisa whispered, her eyes burning with absolute,homicidal intent, "I guarantee you,your mother,and this pathetic parasite will all burn in hell with her."
August stared into her eyes. He searched for a bluff. He searched for hesitation.
He found nothing but the absolute certainty of mutually assured destruction.
August swallowed hard. The lump in his throat felt like broken glass. He looked at Allena, who was shaking violently, tears ruining her makeup. He
looked at the police captain, who was watching them with growing suspicion.
Logic violently overrode his ego. He had to kill the story.
August took a deep breath. He smoothed the lapels of his cashmere coat, forcing his face back into a mask of calm, corporate authority. He turned his back on Elisa and walked over to the police captain.
"Captain," August said smoothly, though his voice was slightly tight. "It appears there has been a massive miscommunication.After speakingwithmy...associate, we have realized this was simply a tragic accident. The Chambers family will not be pressing any charges."
The captain frowned. He looked at the bloody bandage on Jewel's head, then at August.He knew he was being played by billionaires, but he did not have the political capital to fight it.
The captain nodded slowly. "Release her."
The two officers immediately stepped back.They unlocked the handcuffs.

Jewel rubbed her bruised wrists. She shot a look of pure, venomous hatred at Allena, then sprinted back to the bench. She scooped Kayden up into her arms, wrapping the jacket tightly around the convulsing child.
August walked back to Elisa.He leaned in close,his voice a low, threatening growl.
"Cancel the send. Now."
Elisa did,not even look at him. She turned her back on him completely.
"When my daughter is safe in a hospital bed, I will pause the timer," Elisa said coldly over her shoulder."Now, get out of my way."
She walked to Jewel. Together, they rushed toward the precinct doors.
August stood frozen in the center of the lobby. He watched Elisa's back as she pushed through the glass doors and disappeared into the night. His hands curled into fists so tight his fingernails pierced the skin of his palms, drawing tiny drops of blood.
Allena rushed to his side. She wrapped her arms around his bicep, pressing her chest against him.
"Oh, August, you saved us," Allena whimpered,trying to sound sweet and vulnerable.
For the first time in his life, August did not feel protective. He felt a sudden, sharp spike of absolute revulsion. He violently yanked his arm out of her grip, shoving her away.
"Don't touch me," August snapped.
Outside in the freezing air, Elisa threw open the heavy door of Alastair's armored SUV. Jewel scrambled into the back seat with Kayden. Elisa jumped in after them and slammed the door shut.
Elisa leaned over. She pressed her lips to Kayden's burning,sweaty forehead.
"Drive," Elisa ordered the security detail up front."Manhattan Private Hospital. Do not stop for anything."
###Chapter 94

The black armored SUV slammed on its brakes in the ambulance bay of the Manhattan Private Hospital. The heavy tires screeched against the pavement.
Elisa kicked the door open before the vehicle fully stopped. She grabbed Kayden from Jewel's arms and sprinted through the sliding glass doors of the Emergency Room.
The trauma team Dr. Gottlieb had summoned was already waiting, a whirlwind of blue scrubs and focused urgency.
Nurses swarmed Elisa instantly. They ripped Kayden from her arms and placed the convulsing child onto a gurney. Elisa tried to follow them, but a male nurse placed a firm hand on her chest, physically shoving her backward.
"You have to stay here, Mom!" the nurse yelled over the chaos.
The heavy double doors of the trauma bay swung shut. The red light above the frame flared to life.
Elisa hit the wall. Her legs completely gave out.She slid down the cold, painted plaster until she hit the linoleum floor. She pulled her knees to her chest. Her hands were covered in Kayden's blood.Her fingers shook so violently she couldn't clasp them together.
Thirty agonizing minutes passed. Every second felt like a physical knife twisting in her gut.
Finally,the red light clicked off.
The lead attending physician pushed through the doors.He pulled down his surgical mask.
"She is stable," the doctor said, looking down at Elisa. "The fever broke after weadministered IV antipyretics. The head wound required twelve stitches, and she has a mild concussion. But her brain scans are clear. She is going to be fine."
Elisa let out a sound that was half-sob, half-scream. The suffocating weight on her chest instantly vanished. She placed her hands flat on the floor and pushed herself up.
"Can I see her?" Elisa gasped.
"We are moving her to the VIP pediatric wing for observation," the doctor replied gently. "My assistant will bring the admission papers directly to her suite for you to sign once you're settled in with her."
Elisa nodded frantically. She walked into the nearby public restroom. She turned on the faucet and scrubbed the dried blood off her hands with scalding hot water and harsh chemical soap until her skin was raw and red.
She took the elevator to the fourth floor.
The VIP pediatric wing was at the far end of a long,quiet corridor. The hallway was lined with massive,floor-to-ceiling glass walls, offering a clear view
into the ultra-luxurious recovery suites.
Elisa walked down the carpeted hall, her eyes fixed on the floor, her mind entirely focused on getting to Kayden.
As she passed suite 412, a flash of movement caught her peripheral vision.
Elisa's footsteps stopped. Her boots froze on the carpet.
The blinds on the glass wall of suite 412 were partially open.
Inside the room, Tate Mills was sitting in the center of a massive, adjustable hospital bed. He was holding a brand-new video game console, mashing the buttons aggressively. On his left forearm, the tiny cartoon band-aid had been replaced by a slightly larger, sterile white patch.
Kneeling on the floor beside the bed was August Chambers.
August, the man who had just tried to throw her best friend in jail. The man who had threatened to destroy her life.
He was holding a medical cotton swab. He dipped it into a bottle of antiseptic. With agonizing,meticulous gentleness, August dabbed the swab onto a microscopic scrape on Tate's knee. He blew softly on the skin to ease the sting.
Standing on the other side of the bed, Allena looked down at them. She had her hands clasped over her heart, a sickeningly sweet smile of
maternal pride plastered across her face.
They looked exactly like a perfect, loving family.
Elisa stood in the dim hallway. She stared through the glass.
A violent, uncontrollable montage flashed through her brain. Kayden's face covered in dark blood.Kayden's body convulsing on the hard wooden bench. Kayden screaming in terror.
Her daughter-August's actual flesh and blood-had nearly died tonight because of the boy sitting on that bed. And her husband was currently kneeling on the floor,playing the role of a doting father to the culprit, treating a scratch while his own child bled.
A strange sensation bloomed in Elisa's chest.
It wasn't anger. It wasn't sadness. It wasn't betrayal.It was a cold, absolute, desolate emptiness.2
A low, breathy laugh escaped her lips. She didn't shed a single tear. The seven years of pain,the desperate hope, the bitter resentment-it all instantly incinerated into gray ash.
Her heart turned into a solid block of black ice.
August Chambers was dead to her. He did not deserve to know he had a daughter. He did not deserve to breathe the same air as Kayden.
Elisa slowly turned her head away from the glass.She walked to the heavy metal door of the emergency stairwell and pushed it open.
The stairwell was freezing and smelled of dust.Elisa pulled her phone from her pocket. Her hand was no longer shaking. It was as steady as carved stone.
She dialed the encrypted line for Harvey Vance.
Harvey answered on the second ring. "Elisa. Are you safe?"
"I am fine," Elisa said. Her voice sounded like a machine. "Execute the seven-year clause. I want the final divorce decree finalized immediately."
Harvey hesitated. "Elisa, if we drop the formal papers now, Chambers Legal will instantly retaliate.They will freeze assets. They will drag this into a brutal,multi-year litigation."
"I don't care," Elisa stated coldly. "I am done playing games with this man. I want the legal severing of this marriage on paper,today."
"Understood," Harvey sighed. "I will have my assistant bring the physical documents to your location."
"Do it," Elisa ordered.
She hung up the phone. She stood in the stairwell for exactly five minutes. She let the freezing air chilI her skin. She allowed the last remnants of Elisa Chambers to die in the dark, leaving only Faye behind.
She pushed the stairwell door open and walked back into the hallway.
An hour later, a young man in a sharp suit hurried
down the hall. He handed Elisa a thick manila envelope sealed with the Vance Law Firm crest.
Elisa gripped the envelope. She turned and walked directly toward suite 412. It was time to end the farce.
###Chapter 95

Elisa's boots made absolutely no sound against the thick, plush carpet of the VIP wing.
She held the heavy manila envelope in her right hand. Her grip was relaxed, casual, completely devoid of the nervous tension that used to plague her around August.
She reached the door of suite 412. She did not look through the glass. The people inside that room were no longer human beings to her; they were just obstacles to be removed.
She raised her hand to knock.
Before her knuckles touched the wood, the door handle clicked. The heavy door swung inward.
August stepped out into the hallway. He was holding his cell phone to his ear, his brow furrowed in annoyance as he prepared to take a business call away from Allena's constant chatter.
He turned his head and practically walked straight into Elisa.
August physically jumped. He stumbled back half a step.
He looked at her standing in the shadows of the corridor. His eyes immediately narrowed, a defensive, aggressive wall slamming down over his features.
"What are you doing here?" August hissed,keeping his voice low so Allena wouldn't hear. "Are you stalking us now? Haven't you caused enough damage for one night?"
Elisa did not react to the insult. She just looked at him.

Her black eyes were completely hollow. They were two bottomless, dead wells. There was no anger,no hurt, no defiance. She looked at him the exact same way she would look at a broken chair or a stain on the wall.
August felt a sudden, icy chill crawl up his spine.The hairs on the back of his neck stood up. He opened his mouth to hurl another insult, but the words died in his throat. Her absolute, terrifying apathy paralyzed him.
Elisa raised her right hand. She slapped the heavy manila envelope flat against the center of August's chest.
The physical impact wasn't hard, but it carried a crushing psychological weight.
August instinctively brought his hand up and caught the envelope before it fell. He looked down at the Vance Law Firm crest, then back up at her face. His brow furrowed in genuine confusion.
"What is this?" August asked.
"Those are the final divorce papers," Elisa said. Her voice was perfectly flat, a monotone hum that lacked any human inflection. "Drafted in
accordance with the seven-year expiration clause of our prenuptial agreement."
The words hit August like a physical blow to the head.
His pupils violently contracted. His heart physically skipped a beat, stuttering in his chest.
He had always assumed her threats of divorce were just dramatic leverage. He thought she was throwing tantrums to get his attention, to force him to drop Allena. But the physical weight of the legal document in his hand, combined with the dead look in her eyes, triggered a sudden, massive wave of panic.
August quickly masked the panic with a sneer of arrogant mockery.
"Is that right?" August scoffed, tapping the envelope against his palm. "Have you finally done the math? Let me guess. You want half the liquid assets and the Hamptons house to go away quietly?"
Elisa's lips curved into a microscopic, razor-sharp smile.
"Read clause four, August," Elisa said softly. "I am waiving all rights to alimony. I don't want a single penny of your money. I just want you to sign the paper and get the hell out of my life."
The insult was surgical. It bypassed August's armor and stabbed directly into his fragile ego. She wasn't extorting him. She was discarding him. She was treating his billions as if they were toxic waste.
August's face flushed a dark, angry red. His hand gripped the envelope so tightly the thick paper crumpled.
"You think you can just walk away?" August snarled,leaning into her space, trying to use his physical size to intimidate her. "If you sign this, you lose the Chambers name. You lose the protection. You go back to being a nobody."
Elisa did not step back. She leaned forward,closing the distance until she could smell the stale coffee on his breath.
"Mr. Chambers," Elisa whispered,her voice dripping with absolute, freezing disdain. "Your name and your protection are garbage to me."
She tilted her head, her eyes flicking toward the partially open door of the hospital room.
"Keep your money," Elisa said,her tone laced with toxic pity. "You are going to need it to buy band-aids for your mistress's bastard brother. You two are truly a match made in hell."
August's face went completely white. The precision of the insult, the absolute lack of jealousy,broke his brain. He raised the envelope, his mouth opening to scream at her.
Elisa cut him off. She raised her left wrist and tapped the glass face of her watch.
"You have twenty-four hours to have your legal team review the terms," Elisa stated, her voice returning to a brisk, businesslike clip."I expect you
at the Vance Law Firm at ten o'clock tomorrow morning.Do not be late."
She didn't wait for his response. She turned on her heel. Her spine was perfectly straight, her shoulders pulled back. She walked down the hallway with the commanding, untouchable stride of a queen leaving a conquered territory.
August stood frozen outside the door.
He watched her walk away. The manila envelope in his hand suddenly felt like it weighed a thousand pounds. A sickening,hollow void opened up in his chest, as if a vital organ had just been surgically removed without anesthesia.

He took a half-step forward. He opened his mouth to call her name, to demand she come back.
His throat was completely dry. No sound came out."August?"
The door to suite 412 opened wider. Allena poked her headout. Her makeup was perfectly retouched.She looked at August's pale face, then glanced down the empty hallway.
"Who were you talking to?" Allena asked,her voice high and whiny. "Why aren't you coming back inside? Tate wants to show you his game."
August snapped his head toward her. He looked at Allena's face. For a split second, the whining tone grated against his eardrums like nails on a chalkboard. A violent surge of irritation flared in his chest.
He forced it down. He cleared his throat and tucked the crumpled envelope under his arm.

"It was nobody," August muttered. He turned and walked back into the room, the heavy door clicking shut behind him.

At the end of the hall, Elisa pressed the button for the elevator. She looked at her reflection in the polished metal doors.

She let out a long, slow exhale. The invisible chains that had choked her for seven years were finally broken.

She had absolutely no idea that the envelope August was holding contained a bomb.
###Chapter 96

August walked into the private sitting area of suite 412.

The room smelled of sterile hospital cleaner and Allena's heavy floral perfume. Allena was sitting on the plush leather sofa, carefully peeling an apple with a silver paring knife. Devon Browning was slouched in an armchair across from her, typing rapidly on his laptop.
August walked to the glass coffee table. He threw the crumpled manila envelope down. It hit the glass with a heavy, dull thud.
Devon paused his typing. He looked at the envelope, then up at August's dark, stormy face.
"What's that?" Devon asked, a mocking grin spreading across his face. "Did some startup CEO send you a threat?"
August rubbed his temples. A dull headache was throbbing behind his eyes.
"It's from Elisa," August said, his voice a low, cold rumble. "It's her divorce papers."
The silver knife in Allena's hand slipped. She accidentally sliced a thick chunk of apple flesh off,but she quickly recovered. She kept her head down,desperately hiding the massive, triumphant smile that threatened to split her face in half.
Devon let out a loud, obnoxious whistle.
"Finally," Devon laughed, leaning back in his chair."The leech is finally letting go. Come on,open it up. Let's see how many millions the crazy bitch is trying to extort from you."
August stared at the envelope. He remembered Elisa's dead eyes. He remembered her saying she didn't want a single penny.
He reached out and ripped the sealed flap open.He pulled out the thick stack of legal documents.He flipped past the cover letter and scanned the first page of the actual agreement.
His eyes darted across the dense legal text.Suddenly, his reading speed slowed. His pupils violently contracted. He blinked hard, thinking he had misread the words, and read the paragraph again.
The blood rushed to his face, turning his skin a dark,mottled red. The shock instantly mutated into a blinding,explosive rage.
"What is this?" August hissed, his voice shaking.
He flipped to the next page.
Clause 4.1: The Husband agrees to forfeit one hundred percent (100%) of his equity shares in the Chambers Group, transferring absolute ownership to the Wife.
August's hands began totremble. He flipped to the final page.
Clause 7.3: As compensation for emotional distress,the Husband agrees to place fifty-one percent (51%)of his personal voting shares into a blind trust, to be administered by the Wife's legal counsel for a period of ten (10) years.
A low, dark,terrifying laugh ripped out of August's throat. It sounded like a physical bark of madness.
Devon frowned. He stood up and walked over,leaning over August's shoulder to look at the papers.
Devon's eyes widened. He stared at the equity transfer clause, then read the voting shares clause.Devon threw his head back and erupted into a fit of hysterical,exaggerated laughter.
"Are you kidding me?!" Devon roared, slapping his knee. "She wants the whole company? And she wants to control your voting rights from beyond the grave? August, this isn't a divorce settlement!This is a script for a bad Hollywood extortion movie!The woman has completely lost her mind!"
Allena quickly set the apple down. She stood up and walked over, her eyes scanning the text.When she saw the voting trust clause, she immediately deployed her weapon.

Allena's lower lip trembled. Tears instantly welled in her eyes.
"She put that in there just to hurt me," Allena whimpered, pressing her face against August's shoulder. "She knows we want to be together.She ic so evil August. She is trving to trap vou."
August stared at the paper. The panic and the hollow feeling he had experienced in the hallway completely vanished.
It was replaced by a twisted,arrogant relief.
She wasn't discarding him. She didn't want to leave him at all. This absurd, impossible document was proof. She had drafted terms so ridiculous, so legally impossible to fulfill, because she wanted him to reject it. She was throwing a massive,theatrical tantrum to force him to negotiate, to force him to pay attention to her.
Her cold, dead eyes in the hallway? It was all an act.A pathetic, desperate act from a woman who couldn't bear to let go of his wealth and status.
"Crazy bitch," August muttered, his voice dripping with absolute, venomous contempt.
He grabbed the thick stack of legal papers with both hands. His knuckles turned white.

With a violent, aggressive jerk, August ripped the entire document in half.
The loud, sharp sound of tearing paper echoed through the sitting area. Allena physically jumped,letting out a small squeak of surprise, but her eyes gleamed with vicious satisfaction.
August stacked the two torn halves together. He gripped them and ripped them again. He tore the contract into tiny, jagged shreds.
He walked over to the metal trash can in the corner of the room and violently slammed the handful of
shredded paper into the bin.
He turned around. His face was a mask of cold,ruthless corporate tyranny.
"Devon," August commanded. "Text the Legal Department right now. Tell them to file an immediate injunction. I want every single bank account, trust fund, and credit line associated with Elisa Wilder frozen by midnight."
Devon grinned like a predator smelling blood. He pulled out his phone and started typing frantically."Consider it done. We'll starve her out."

"She wants to play extortion games?" August sneered, adjusting his cuffs. "I will make sure she doesn't have enough money to buy a cup of coffee.She will crawl back on her knees and beg me to drop the injunction."
August walked back to the sofa. He wrapped his arm around Allena's waist, pulling her tightly against him. He kissed the top of her head.
"Don't worry," August murmured. "She is not going to ruin anything."
Allena smiled, resting her head on his chest. She looked past him, staring at the shredded pieces of paper in the trash can.
Meanwhile, five miles away, in a dark, ultra-luxurious apartment on the Upper East Side.
A burner phone resting on a mahogany desk lit up.
A single text message appeared on the glowing screen: Target destroyed document.Proceeding.
A wrinkled, heavily manicured hand reached out of the shadows and picked up the phone. Germaine Chambers stared at the screen. A cold, reptilian smile stretched across her face, perfectly mirroring the madness unfolding in the hospital
###Chapter 97

The air inside the dark walnut study of the Chambers Long Island estate was thick with the scent of expensive cigars and old money.
Germaine Chambers sat perfectly straight in her high-backed leather chair. She placed the encrypted burner phone face down on the polished mahogany desk. The text message confirming the destruction of the divorce papers had just secured her empire.
A soft, hesitant knock echoed from the heavy oak door.
"Enter," Germaine commanded.
Brenda, the head housekeeper of the Manhattan penthouse, slipped into the room. She kept her eyes glued to the floor, her hands nervously wringing the fabric of her apron. She walked to the edge of the desk and stopped, trembling slightly.Germaine picked up her bone china teacup. She took a slow,elegant sip.
"You executed the swap perfectly, Brenda,"Germaine said, her voice smooth and chillingly calm. "The bonus has already been wired to your offshore account."
Brenda swallowed hard. Her mind flashed back to three days ago.
Flashback: Elisa had packed a single suitcase and walked out of the penthouse. Before leaving, Elisa had placed the real, legally sound divorce agreement into the master bedroom safe,instructing Brenda to hand the sealed envelope to Harvey Vance's courier when he arrived.
The second the elevator doors closed, Brenda had received the call. Following Germaine's explicit orders, Brenda used the emergency master override code. She opened the safe. She removed the real documents and slid them into a shredder.Then, she took the absurd, legally impossible fake contract-drafted by Germaine's personal fixer-and sealed it inside the Vance Law Firm envelope.When the courier arrived, Brenda simply handed over the tampered package.
End of flashback.
"Madam," Brenda whispered, her voice shaking."What... what if Mr. August finds out? What if he checks the security logs on the safe?"
Germaine let out a short, sharp scoff. She set the teacup down with a clink.
"My son is currently blinded by his own pathetic ego and that cheap Sinclair whore," Germaine sneered. "He will not check the logs. He needed an excuse to believe Elisa wasn't truly leaving him.That fake contract gave him the perfect narrative.He thinks she is extorting him. He will fight her,and that fight will buy me the time I need."
Brenda frowned, her confusion overriding her fear.
"But Madam... you despise Elisa. Why not just let them divorce and be rid of her?"

Germaine's eyes flashed with sudden, terrifying anger. She reached across the desk and grabbed a thick,leather-bound financial prospectus,slamming it down hard.
"Because next month is the Global Al Summit!"Germaine snapped, her voice cracking like a whip."The Chambers Group is finalizing a multi-billion dollar funding round for the Aethel joint venture. If the Wall Street short-sellers smell blood-if they see the CEO embroiled in a massive, messy divorce and a potential liquidation of marital assets-they will attack the stock like sharks. We would lose billions."

Germaine leaned back, her eyes narrowing into cold slits.
"The divorce will happen," Germaine stated. "But it will happen on my timeline. After the funding is secured. Until then, they remain legally married.I had already paid off the junior clerk at Vance's firm.He simply took the 'correct' envelope Brenda handed him without asking questions."
She paused, her fingers drumming a slow, rhythmic beat on the desk.

"Furthermore," Germaine murmured, almost to herself. "Elisa's recent behavior is highly irregular.A subservient nurse does not suddenly produce fifty million dollars in hush money. She does not suddenly possess the spine to defy me. She has
backing. And I will find out who it is."
Germaine pressed a silver intercom button on her desk. "Send him in."

The heavy oak doors opened. A man in a gray trench coat walked in. He had the cold,dead eyes of a reptile. He was the most expensive, ruthless private investigator in New York.
Germaine slid a thick manila folder across the desk."This is my daughter-in-law, Elisa Wilder,"Germaine ordered. "I want twenty-four-hour physical and digital surveillance. I want every bank
account, every phone call, and every associate tracked. I don't care where she goes-Long Island,Brooklyn, or the damn moon. Find out why a penniless nurse is making so many secretive trips out of the city."
The investigator picked up the folder. He gave a single, sharp nod. "If she has a secret, I will dig it up."He turned and walked out of the room.

Germaine stood up and walked to the massive floor-to-ceiling window. She looked out over the manicured lawns of the estate. Anyone who threatened the stability of the Chambers family would be crushed into dust.
Behind her, Brenda's phone suddenly vibrated.
Brenda pulled it out of her apron. She read the text message from Germaine's chief of staff. All the
blood drained from Brenda's face.
"Madam," Brenda stammered, her voice high and panicked. "I... I just received a priority alert. Elisa was at the Manhattan Private Hospital tonight."
"I know she was there," Germaine said without turning around. "August told me about the altercation."
"No,Madam," Brenda swallowed hard. "The report says Elisa didn't just come to fight. She brought a patient into the ER. A little girl. She was bleeding."
Germaine's entire body froze.
She slowly turned around. Her eyes locked onto Brenda with the terrifying intensity of a predator that had just smelled fresh blood.
"A little girl?" Germaine whispered.
The words hung in the air. Germaine's brilliant,paranoid mind instantly began connecting invisible dots. The secret trips out of the city. The sudden need for massive amounts of money. The desperate, fierce defiance.
"Find out exactly who that child is," Germaine commanded, her voice dropping to a lethal,vibrating hum. "Get me a name. Get me a face.Now."

A massive, invisible net had just been castinto the dark, and it was falling directly over Kayden.
###Chapter 98

At exactly 9:50 AM the next morning, Elisa stepped

The act of destruction back at the hospital had filled the hollow void in August's chest with a familiar, comforting rage. arrogant victory.

Overlaid on the photo was a block of neon text typed by Devon: Boss just tore up the extortion letter. Getting rid of the greedy leech who tried to steal the company.Cheers!
Elisastared at the screen.

Elisa's eyes darted down the page.
Notice of Immediate Injunction: Asset Freeze.

Every single bank account with your name on it-including the offshore trust holding the fifty million dollars-is completely frozen. You cannot move a single cent."Elisa's fingers curled into tight fists. Her fingernails dug into her palms.

Elisa closed her eyes. The image of Kayden's pale,stitched forehead flashed behind her eyelids.

whispered, her voice dropping to a terrifying,mechanical frequency. "They think the law is their weapon."

He answered immediately. "Faye."

###Chapter 99

Elisa was sitting on the edge of the hospital bed.She was holding a damp cloth, gently wiping the sweat from Kayden's sleeping face. When the door slammed against the wall, Elisa jumped, her heart leaping into her throat.

)r. Gottlieb stopped walking. He looked at lugust's extended hand. He did not reach out.

###Chapter 100

The setting sun cast a blood-red glare across the glass facade of the Manhattan Private Hospital.

revolving doors spun wildly.

She instantly threw her body sideways, slamming her back against the open rear door of the SUV,using her own flesh to physically block his line of sight into the back seat. Her black eyes locked onto his, burning with a desperate, feral warning.
August stopped exactly three feet away. He looked at her defensive posture. He sneered.
"What are you hiding in there, Elisa?" August demanded, his voice a low, vibrating threat."Are you stealing hospital equipment now?"
Inside the back seat, the loud, aggressive sound of August's voice pierced through Kayden's feverish haze.
The little girl shifted on the leather seat. She turned her head toward the open door. Through the narrow gap between Elisa's body and the doorframe, Kayden saw the back of Elisa's head.
Her brain,boiling at 104 degrees, stripped away all her careful training and secrecy. She just wanted comfort. She reached her tiny, trembling hand out toward the gap.
"Mommy!" Kayden cried out.
The word was high, clear, and laced with absolute,desperate dependency. It rang out across the freezing drop-off zone like a gunshot.
August's entire body went completely rigid.
It was as if he had been struck by lightning. His breathing stopped. He slowly, mechanically, turned his head. His eyes bypassed Elisa's face and locked onto the narrow gap in the doorway.
August's heart violently seized in his chest.
He stared at the child. He saw the exact shape of his own dark eyes. He saw the sharp, unmistakable curve of his own jawline. The genetic mirror was so
absolute, so terrifyingly precise, that his brain completely short-circuited.
A massive,world-shattering thought attempted to form in his mind: Elisa has a child. But the sheer impossibility of it violently clashed with his reality.His ego, his absolute certainty in his control over his own life, forcefully rejected the data. Impossible,his mind screamed. She was never pregnant. I would have known.
August took a slow, stumbling step forward. His face was pale as a ghost. His voice trembled,completely stripped of its billionaire arrogance,battling the cognitive dissonance tearing his mind apart.
"What..." August gasped, pointing a shaking finger at the window. "What did she just call you?"
Before Elisa could even open her mouth, a blur of motion exploded fromthe back seat.
Jewel threw her entire body across the leather seats. She grabbed Kayden and violently yanked the child into her chest, burying Kayden's face in her shoulder to break the eye contact.
Jewel looked up at Elisa. Her face was twisted in a mask of absolute, hysterical panic.
"Elisa! Get in the car!" Jewel screamed at the top of her lungs, tears flying from her eyes. "She's at 104 degrees! She's completely hallucinating! She doesn't even recognize me! She's calling her Auntie Mommy again! We have to go!"
The medical logic was flawless. Febrile delirium. A child so sick she couldn't tell her own mother from her godmother.
Elisa snapped out of her paralysis. She seized the lifeline.

She spun around, grabbed the edge of the heavy armored door, and violently slammed it shut right in August's face.
Elisa threw herself into the front passenger seat.
"Drive!" Elisa roared at the security detail. "Go! Now!"The driver slammed his foot on the gas. The massive engine roared. The SUV tore out of the drop-off zone, its tires screeching against the pavement as it merged recklessly into the Manhattan traffic.

August stood frozen on the curb. A cloud of gray exhaust fumes washed over his expensive coat.
But as August stood there in the dark, the image of those dark, familiar eyes burned into his retinas.He shook his head aggressively, forcing the phantom image away. It was a setup. A trap. She had found a child actor, a look-alike, to manipulate him. It was the only logical explanation. And yet, the resemblance... it was unnervingly perfect. He filed the anomaly away, a piece of corrupted data to be analyzed later. For now, it was a distraction he couldn't afford.
###Chapter 101

losing hand."

###Chapter 102

"Mr. Vance," August said. His voice dripped with condescending authority. "It seems the entry requirements for your little appraisals have severely dropped in quality."

The surrounding billionaires stopped talking. The silence in the immediate vicinity became absolute.Everyone waited for the blood to spill.

Elisa's fingers tightened around the stem of her champagne flute. She did not look away. She stared directly into August's dark, furious eyes, the invisible war between them igniting theair.
###Chapter 103


August's insulting words hung in the quiet air of the gallery.

Allena covered her mouth with a manicured hand and let out a soft, breathy giggle. She looked at Elisa with wide, fake-apologetic eyes, playing the role of the embarrassed but amused girlfriend perfectly.

Preston Vance frowned. The amusement faded from his face. He despised crude social bullying,especially directed at someone he was currently evaluating for a nine-figure investment. He opened his mouth to defend his guest.

Before Preston could speak, Cyprian stepped out from behind August's broad shoulders.

Cyprian held a glass of red wine. His face was flushed. He looked Elisa up and down with a sleazy,mocking sneer.

"What's the matter, Elisa?" Cyprian taunted, his voice loud enough for the surrounding crowd to hear."Did your bank accounts dry up so fast that you have to wear Aethel's corporate uniforms to sneak in here for free drinks?"
A few of the younger trust-fund heirs in the crowd let out low, cruel chuckles. They waited to see the disgraced woman break down or run away.

Elisa did not flush. She did not yell. She did not even turn her head to look at Cyprian.

She slowly raised her glass and took a calm,elegant sip of champagne.

She looked directly at Preston. Her voice was a flat,clinical monotone, as if she were discussing a laboratory experiment.

"Mr. Vance," Elisa asked, her tone perfectly even."When you are preparing to short a failing company, do you ever waste your time listening to the barking of their guard dogs?"

Preston blinked. For a split second, he was stunned by the sheer, cold-blooded precision of the insult.Then, a low, genuine laugh rumbled in his chest.He shook his head.

"No," Preston replied smoothly. "That would be a terrible misallocation of resources."

Cyprian's face instantly turned the color of raw meat. The veins in his neck bulged. He pointed a shaking finger at Elisa's face.

"Are you calling me a dog, you crazy bitch?!"
Cyprian roared.

Elisa finally turned her head. She locked her dead,black eyes onto Cyprian.

"A man who cannot even manage his own trust fund," Elisa stated, her voice slicing through the air like a surgical blade. "A man who relies entirely on the charitable dividends of the Chambers Group to maintain his pathetic illusion of wealth. What other survival skill do you possess, Cyprian, other than barking at your master's enemies?"

The words hit Cyprian like a physical slap to the face. It was the absolute, humiliating truth. He opened his mouth, but his throat seized. He stood there, completely paralyzed, gasping like a fish on a dock.

August's face turned a dangerous, stormy gray.Striking his loyal dog in public was a direct,unforgivable strike against his own authority.

August took a heavy step forward. His massive frame loomed over Elisa, his physical presence radiating a suffocating threat.

"Watch your mouth, Elisa," August growled, his voice dropping to a lethal frequency. "Remember exactly who you are talking to."
Alastair moved.

He stepped fully in front of Elisa, his broad shoulders completely blocking August's line of sight. The two billionaire titans stood chest to chest, the physical tension between them thick enough to choke on.

Alastair let out a cold, dismissive scoff. He did not look at August. He looked directly at Allena.

"Speaking of identity, Mr. Chambers," Alastair said.He used the sharp, ruthless cadence of a Wall Street merger executioner. "I find your recent investment logic to be deeply confusing."

Alastair gestured lazily toward Allena's vulgar,diamond-encrusted dress. His voice dripped with the ultimate arrogance of a self-made tech god looking down on inherited rot.

"You completely abandoned a top-tier asset capable of generating exponential, infinite growth,"Alastair stated clearly. "And instead, you dumped massive capital into a highly depreciating,toxic asset. One that possesses absolutely zero core technology and relies entirely on expensive, fake packaging to hide its negative value."

The surrounding hedge fund managers and bankers instantly decoded the metaphor. Several of
the older billionaires actually covered their mouths to muffle their laughter.

Allena did not understand the complex financial terminology, but the words toxic asset and the mocking laughter from the crowd pierced her thick skull. Her face turned a blotchy mix of pale white and furious red.

She violently shook August's arm. "August! They are bullying me! Make them stop!" she whined,her voice cracking.

August's eyes burned with a humiliating,helpless rage. He could not physically attack Alastair here,and worse, he could not logically refute the insult.In the pure, brutal language of Wall Street,Alastair's assessment was perfectly accurate.

Preston watched the exchange, his eyes gleaming.His admiration for Elisa skyrocketed. She didn't just survive an ambush; she and her ally had completely slaughtered the attackers.

Preston decided to intervene before punches were thrown. He raised his bourbon glass.

"Gentlemen," Preston said, his voice carrying the authority of the host. "Tonight is strictly for art and capital allocation. Let's leave the personal grievances at the door."
August let out a harsh, breathy exhale through his nose. He shot one last, murderous glare at Alastair,grabbed Allena by the waist, and dragged her toward the opposite side of the gallery. Cyprian scurried after them like a beaten animal.

Elisa watched their retreating backs. The rigid tension in her shoulders dropped a fraction of an inch,but the freezing cold in her eyes remained.

Alastair turned his head slightly. "Are you alright?"he asked quietly.

Elisa gave a single nod. "I can handle a little nausea."

Preston stepped closer to Elisa. He handed her a fresh flute of champagne from a passing tray.

"That was a spectacular counter-attack, Faye,"Preston said, his voice laced with genuine respect.

Elisa accepted the glass with a polite nod,ready to steer the conversation back to the offshore server funding.

She did not notice that in the dark shadows of the far exhibition corner, August had stopped walking.He stood perfectly still, his eyes locked onto Elisa and Preston. A dark, twisted, suffocating jealousy clawed at his throat, demanding blood.
###Chapter 104


The gallery's central exhibition space began to glow as the overhead lights dimmed. The crowd of elites slowly drifted toward the center, holding their drinks, preparing for the main event.

Preston guided Elisa toward a massive,seventeenth-century oil painting displayed on a freestanding wall.

For the next ten minutes, they stood shoulder to shoulder.The conversation flowed effortlessly from the authenticity of the brushstrokes to the current limitations of Al image-recognition algorithms in detecting art forgeries.

Elisa's deep, terrifyingly vast technical knowledge,combined with her razor-sharp commercial instincts, completely captivated Preston. He was used to women who agreed with everything he said to get his money. Elisa challenged him, corrected him, and elevated the dialogue.

Preston looked at her profile. The harsh gallery spotlights softened against her pale skin. A look of undisguised, intense attraction burned in his eyes.
He reached into the inner pocket of his suit jacket and pulled out a heavy, gold-embossed private business card. He held it out to her.

"Elisa," Preston said, his voice dropping to a low,intimate register. "I want to formally invite you to lunch tomorrow. Just the two of us. I want to discuss funding your offshore node in detail."

Elisa looked at the card. This was the lifeline. This was the money she needed to bypass August's financial chokehold. She raised her hand to take it.

"Mr.Vance."

The voice came from directly behind them. It was freezing cold and heavy with absolute, crushing authority.

Elisa's hand froze in mid-air.

August materialized from the shadows like a ghost.He stepped so close to Elisa that the fabric of his tuxedo sleeve brushed against her navy suit. The smell of his cedarwood cologne invaded her lungs,making her stomach twist.

"I highly recommend you conduct a thorough due diligence before you commit a single dollar to this woman," August stated. His eyes were locked onto the gold card in Preston's hand.
Preston slowly lowered the card. He turned to face the undisputed king of New York real estate and commerce. He did not back down,but his posture became guarded.

"What exactly do you mean by that, Mr.Chambers?" Preston asked.

August turned his head. He looked down at Elisa.He did not look at her like a husband looking at a wife. He looked at her with the blank, disgusted stare of a stranger looking at a rat in the subway.

"Some people in this city," August sneered, his voice loud enough to draw the attention of the surrounding billionaires, "are very skilled at fabricating their resumes and wildly inflating their actual value to secure funding."

Preston's brow furrowed. He was a man who trusted his own intellect above all else.

"You seem to know a great deal about Miss Wilder's background," Preston challenged.

The nearby guests stopped talking. They leaned in,their eyes darting between the three figures.

Allena hurried over, her diamond dress scraping against the floor. She grabbed August's arm,her eyes wide with panic, terrified he was about to
publicly admit he was married to the woman he had just brought his mistress to confront.

August stared into Elisa's dead, emotionless eyes.The memory of her handing him the divorce papers, the absolute apathy in her voice when she told him she didn't want a penny of his money,fueled a blinding, destructive rage inside him.

He raised his chin. He delivered the most vicious,calculated lie of his life.

"I don't know her well at all," August said. His voice was a block of solid ice. "She is simply a low-level employee | recently fired from one of my subsidiaries. Because she couldn't keep her hands off company property."

The words dropped into the quiet gallery like a live grenade.

A collective gasp rippled through the crowd.Whispers instantly erupted.

Elisa's heart violently contracted. A sharp, physical pain pierced her chest. She had stopped expecting basic human decency from August a long time ago,but this public, malicious assassination of her professional character still made bile rise in her throat.
Allena let out a massive, silent exhale of relief. A sick, triumphant smile spread across her face.

Preston's expression didn't change, but his mind raced. What a laughably transparent lie. He knows I've seen the FDA hearing reports. He knows I know who Faye is. The realization hit him with the force of a physical blow. August wasn't trying to convince him; he was sending a message. He is willing to publicly humiliate himself with a lie this crude just to attack her. This wasn't business. This was a declaration of total, irrational war. And Vance Capital did not invest in warzones.

Preston took a physical half-step backward,creating distance between himself and Elisa.

He slipped the goId card back into his pocket.

"Mr. Chambers' intel is always... fascinating,"Preston said. His voice was now polite, distant, and completely closed off, masking his strategic retreat. "I appreciate the perspective.Unfortunately, Vance Capital operates under strict risk-management protocols. We do not invest in assets that are currently engaged in active warfare with the Chambers Group."

Elisa did not scream. She did not defend herself.
Her analytical brain calculated the variables
instantly. Because of the NDA Germaine had forced her to sign, she could not legally scream, I am his wife of seven years! If she threw a tantrum without proof, she would only cement the label of a "crazy,fired employee."

She placed her empty champagne flute onto a nearby cocktail table.

She looked up at August. She didn't look angry. She looked at him with absolute, freezing pity.

"Your acting," Elisa whispered, her voice so low only August could hear it, "is even more nauseating than your taste in women."

August's left eye twitched violently. The muscle in his jaw jumped, but he forced his face to remain an arrogant mask of victory.

Elisa turned her back on Preston and August. She walked away from the crowd, heading toward the dimly lit corridor that led to the restrooms. She needed exactly sixty seconds of isolation to force oxygen back into her lungs.

Behind her, Allena loudly praised August. "You are so brave for exposing a scammer like that, honey!"she cheered, drawing nods of agreement from the sycophants.
In a matter of seconds, Elisa had been violently rebranded. She was no longer a tech genius;she was a scheming gold-digger and a thief.

August watched Elisa's straight back disappear around the corner of the dark corridor.

He felt no victory. The hollow, burning sensation in his chest only grew worse. He roughly peeled Allena's hands off his arm.

"I need to make a call," August muttered.

As August stepped away from the crowd, Simon materialized from the shadows, matching his boss's long stride. "Sir," Simon murmured, his voice barely audible over the ambient gallery noise. "We just intercepted the intel. Preston Vance's private agenda tonight was to discuss funding Aethel's offshore server node with Faye."

August's eyes turned to absolute ice. He dismissed Simon with a sharp wave of his hand and kept walking, his feet moving on autopilot, tracking Elisa into the shadows.
###Chapter 105


The corridor leading to the gallery's private restrooms was a stark contrast to the brightly lit exhibition hall. The walls were painted a matte charcoal, and the floor was made of highly polished, black marble. The lighting was intentionally dim and moody.

Elisa walked quickly down the hall. Her high heels clicked sharply against the stone. Her chest heaved with rapid, shallow breaths. Her hands were curled into fists so tight her fingernails were cutting half-moons into her palms. She was fighting a violent,physical urge to turn around and smash a bottle of wine across August's face.

She pulled her phone from her pocket. She needed to text Alastair. The Preston Vance funding route was dead. August had successfully poisoned the wel. They needed a backup plan immediately.②

She kept her eyes on the glowing screen, her thumbs flying across the keyboard.

She did not see the small puddle of spilled champagne slicking the black marble floor near the corner.
Her right heel struck the liquid.
The polished stone instantly lost all friction.Elisa's foot shot forward. Her center of gravity violently collapsed. She let out a sharp gasp as her body tipped backward into empty space.

She squeezed her eyes shut, her muscles bracing for the bone-crushing impact against the solid marble.

The impact never came.

A massive, incredibly strong arm wrapped violently around her waist.

August had been walking fast behind her. When he saw her slip, his reflexes took over. He lunged forward, his arm catching her just inches before she hit the ground.

The sheer momentum of her fall slammed her backward. Her spine crashed heavily against his broad,solid chest.

The physical shock of the contact paralyzed Elisa for a microsecond. The scent of cedarwood and expensive scotch instantly flooded her senses. The heat radiating from his body burned through the thin fabric of her navy suit. It was the exact physical sensation she had known for seven years, now
twisted into something terrifying.

August looked down. He held her suspended in his arms. Her soft body was pressed flush against him.
A few strands of her dark hair had fallen loose,resting against the pale skin of her neck.

In that split second, the blinding rage that had consumed him in the main hall mutated. The physical contact triggered a dark, twisted,possessive hunger in his gut. His heart skipped a heavy beat.

Elisa's eyes snapped open. The realization of who was holding her sent a violent jolt of revulsion through her nervous system.

She thrashed wildly in his grip.

"Let me go!" Elisa hissed, keeping her voice to a furious whisper. She planted her hands against his hard forearms, trying to pry his grip off her waist.

August did not let go. Instead, his arm flexed. He yanked her tighter against his chest, completely lifting her feet off the ground for a second, trapping her in a highly intimate, suffocating hold.

He lowered his head. His lips brushed against the shell of her ear.
"You were dancing around that hedge fund brat
just fine a minute ago," August mocked, his voice a dark, gravelly whisper that sent a shiver of disgust down her spine. "Why can't you even walk straight now?"

Elisa stopped struggling. She went completely rigid. She tilted her head back just enough to glare into his eyes.

"As long as I don't step in the filth you leave behind,I walk perfectly fine," Elisa shot back,her voice dripping with venom.

August's eyes darkened to pitch black. He reached up with his free hand and gripped her chin, his fingers pressing hard into her jawbone, forcing her to look up at him.

"Do not forget who you are, Elisa," August warned,the cruelty returning to his tone.

"Until a judge signs that final decree, you are still Mrs. Chambers. And next week is the family's annual New Year ski trip to Aspen."

August stared down at her lips, then back up to her eyes. He issued a command that left no room for negotiation.

"According to clause seven of our prenuptial agreement, you are legally required to attend family
milestone events. You will be on that plane, and you will play your part."③

Elisa's pupils contracted. The memory of the stolen, altered contract flashed in her mind.Because of Germaine's trap and the asset freeze,she was technically still bound by the legal nightmare of their marriage.

But she refused to bow.

"Your mistress is already wearing the diamonds and playing the lady of the house," Elisa sneered, her voice ice-cold. "Take her. Don't come near me."

August let out a low, dark chuckle. The sound vibrated against her back.

"If you do not show up in Aspen," August threatened, his voice dropping to a lethal whisper,"I will do more than just poison the well with investors. I will instruct Chambers Legal to file a formal lawsuit against Aethel Intelligence for corporate espionage, claiming Project Chiron was developed using stolen Chambers Group intellectual property. I will bury your precious project and your tech-god protector in a legal swamp they will never crawl out of."①

Elisa's breath hitched. A cold spike of shock hit her chest. He wasn't just blocking her; he was
threatening to burn Alastair's entire empire to the ground.

She stared at the man holding her. The hatred in her eyes was so absolute it could have frozen boiling water.

Before she could formulate a response, the distinct sound of footsteps echoed from the corner of the corridor. Someone was coming.

Elisa seized the distraction.

Her free hand, which had been pushing against his forearm, suddenly shifted. Her fingers, seemingly moving at random, pressed into a specific point just below his wrist. It was a precise, targeted strike on the radial nerve. A jolt of sharp, electric pain shot up August's arm, making his fingers go numb and involuntarily spasm open.

August let out a sharp, muffled grunt of pain.The muscles in his arm involuntarily relaxed for a fraction of a second.

Elisa violently twisted her body and ripped herself out of his embrace.

She stumbled forward, quickly regaining her balance. She aggressively smoothed down the front of her wrinkled suit jacket, putting three feet
of physical distance between them.

She glared at him, her chest heaving.
"You will regret this, August," Elisa promised, her voice a terrifying, hollow whisper.②

She turned on her heel and marched toward the restroom doors, disappearing into the women's lounge just as the footsteps rounded the corner.
###Chapter 106


The footsteps stopped abruptly at the entrance of the dark corridor.

Preston Vance and Allena Mills stood side by side,frozen in place.

Three minutes earlier, Allena had noticed August's prolonged absence from the main hall. Paranoia had immediately set in. She excused herself,pretending she needed to use the restroom, and hurried toward the back. At the exact same time,Preston had walked toward the corridor to escape a group of overly aggressive investment bankers.

They had turned the corner at the exact moment August caught Elisa.

Because of the angle and the dim lighting, they did not see Elisa slip on the champagne. They did not see her initial panic.

From Preston's vantage point, the dim lighting obscured the violence of the encounter.He saw August pinning Elisa between his body and the dark marble wall. He saw Elisa's hands planted firmly against August's forearms. Because of the angle,Preston could not hear the venom in their whispers.
He only saw the extreme, undeniable physical proximity and the suffocating tension radiating between them.

Allena stood next to Preston. When she saw August holding Elisa, a wave of pure, acidic jealousy burned the lining of her stomach. Her long acrylic nails dug so hard into her palms they nearly broke the skin.①

But Allena was a predator of opportunity. She caught the look of guarded calculation on Preston's face out of the corner of her eye.

Her brain shifted gears instantly. She did not scream.She did not throw a tantrum.

Allena let out a sharp, trembling gasp. She slapped a hand over her mouth, and her eyes instantly filled with thick, welling tears.

"Mr. Vance," Allena whispered. Her voice was a masterpiece of broken, humiliated dignity. "I am so sorry you have to see this. Elisa...she just refuses to let August go."

Preston turned his head. He looked at Allena's tear-filled eyes. A wave of genuine, calculated sympathy washed over him. The lie provided a convenient,messy narrative for the physical entanglement he had just witnessed.
Allena let a single tear roll down her cheek. "She was fired months ago, but she holds this insane grudge. She constantly stalks us at events, forcing herself on him, trying to use her body to ruin our relationship and extort August for money. It's a nightmare."

The lie was flawless. It perfectly contextualized the visual evidence Preston had just witnessed. It didn't matter to Preston if she was a genius AI architect; getting caught in a desperate, physical confrontation with a former boss and his fiancée was the definition of a severe moral hazard. The logical loop closed in his mind, sealing Elisa's fate.

At that exact moment, Elisa executed her nerve strike, broke free, and marched down the hall.

As she approached the corner, she came face-to-face with Preston and Allena.

Elisa's footsteps slowed for a fraction of a second.She saw the fake tears on Allena's face. She saw the cold, judgmental fury in Preston's eyes.Her brilliant mind instantly calculated the optics of what they had just seen.

She didn't stop. She didn't flinch. She looked at Allena with a gaze so dead and empty it was like looking at a piece of trash on the sidewalk.
As she passed Preston, Elisa gave a slight,professional nod, intending to walk away with her dignity intact.

Preston physically took a half-step backward,pulling his arm away as if her proximity might infect him with a disease. He looked down his nose at her.

"Miss Wilder," Preston said, his voice dripping with absolute, freezing contempt. "I am officially retracting my invitation for lunch tomorrow. Vance Capital operates on strict ethical guidelines. We do not invest in individuals who bring this level of chaotic, personal toxicity into the professional sphere."

Elisa stopped walking.

She looked into Preston's eyes. She saw the absolute certainty of his misunderstanding.

A suffocating wwave of exhaustion hit her. She could not defend herself. The NDA Germaine held over her head legally gagged her. She could not scream that she was the legal wife, that she was the one being stalked and threatened. She was trapped in a cage of silence.①

Elisa's lips curved into a microscopic, mocking smile.
"Suit yourself, Mr. Vance," Elisa said, her voice a flat, emotionless hum. "I sincerely hope your financial investments yield better returns than your judgment of character."

She didn't wait for his reaction.She turned and walked into the restroom, letting the heavy wooden door swing shut, locking the toxic judgment outside.

August limped slightly as he walked up the corridor,shaking his hand to restore feeling. He saw Allena wiping her eyes and Preston's rigid posture. He frowned.

Allena immediately threw herself against August's chest.

"It's okay, honey," Allena cooed loudly, playing the role of the forgiving angel. "I know she ambushed you. I don't blame you at all."

Preston looked at August. He gave a firm,respectful nod.

"Mr. Chambers," Preston said, his tone shifting to one of camaraderie between men dealing with crazy women. "You have an incredibly gracious and loving fiancée. I highly suggest you protect her from that kind of harassment."
August heard the word fiancée. A sharp spike of irritation flared in his chest, but he looked at the closed restroom door. He swallowed the irritation and gave Preston a tight nod, accepting the false narrative.

Preston smiled, feeling a surge of protective goodwill toward Allena. It was a feeling that would heavily influence the auction that was about to begin.①
###Chapter 107


Twenty minutes later, the private appraisal event transitioned into the main spectacle of the evening:a highly exclusive, VIP-only closed-door auction.

The auction room was located in the subterranean level of the gallery. It was designed like a miniature Roman amphitheater. The guests sat on plush,semi-circular leather sofas that sloped downward toward a brightly lit central stage.

Elisa had washed her face with freezing water,forcing her heart rate back to a steady rhythm.She sat in the back left corner of the room, hidden in the shadows. Alastair sat beside her, his presence a silent, immovable wall of support.

In the dead center of the front row, occupying the best seats in the house, sat August and Allena.

Preston Vance stood near the auctioneer's podium,personally overseeing the presentation of the artifacts.

The auctioneer tapped the microphone, drawing the room's attention. "Welcome, ladies and gentlemen. Before we begin, please note that several elite items tonight carry a unique seller's
stipulation: should bidding exceed ten times the base appraisal, the seller grants the auction house the right to select the winning bid based on strategic corporate merit, rather than pure capital."

The first five items were contemporary sculptures and minor impressionist sketches. Elisa paid no attention. She stared down at her encypted tablet,her fingers flying across the screen as she desperately tried to calculate alternative routing for the offshore server funding.

"Lot number six," the auctioneer announced, his voice echoing through the room.

An assistant rolled a velvet-draped pedestal onto the stage. The auctioneer grabbed the fabric and pulled it away. A single, intense spotlight hit the glass display case.

"A flawless, late-Victorian era Antique Enamel Locket, set in 18-karat gold and encrusted with Ceylon sapphires."

Elisa's eyes casually flicked up from her tablet.

The moment her gaze locked onto the necklace,her entire body went completely rigid.

Her fingers instantly clamped down on the edges of her encrypted tablet. Her grip was so sudden and
violently tight that the metal casing let out a faint,strained groan under the pressure. Her breathing completely stopped. Alastair immediately sensed the rigid, unnatural stiffness of her posture and the sudden halt of her breathing.①

"Faye?" Alastair whispered, leaning in. "What is it?"

Elisa did not answer. She stared at the locket. The intricate gold filigree. The specific, asymmetrical placement of the blue sapphires.

It wasn't just an antique. It was her grandmother's dowry. It was the exact necklace her mother had wept over, the one her gambling-addict uncle had stolen and pawned ten years ago. It was the only physical piece of her family's true history left in the world.④

Elisa's hands began to shake. She gripped the edge of the leather sofa.

"I have to get that," Elisa breathed, her voice trembling with raw, unfiltered emotion. "Alastair, I have to buy that necklace. No matter what."

Alastair didn't ask questions. He reached into his jacket and pulled out a solid black titanium credit card. He slid it into her hand.

"It has no limit," Alastair said quietly. "Considerit an
advance on your Aethel salary.Take it."
"We will start the bidding at one hundred thousand dollars," the auctioneer declared.

Elisa did not hesitate. She immediately raised her numbered paddle high into the air.

In the front row, Allena had been bored, inspecting her acrylic nails. But when she saw the auctioneer point to the back corner, she turned her head.She saw Elisa holding the paddle.

A vicious, predatory light ignited in Allena's eyes.She had no idea what the necklace was or why Elisa wanted it. But the simple fact that Elisa desired it was enough. If Elisa wanted it, Allena was going to take it.①

Allena grabbed August's bicep, shaking it excitedly.She pointed a manicured finger at the stage.

"Oh, August, look!" Allena cooed, her voice loud and whining. "That sapphire necklace perfectly matches my eyes! It's so beautiful. Please buy it for me,honey?"

August followed her finger. He saw the necklace.Then, he turned his head and looked at the back row.He saw Elisa.

He remembered her cold rejection in the hallway.
He remembered the way she had looked at him with absolute pity. His massive, fragile ego demanded blood. He needed to remind her that he was the god of this city, and without him, she could have nothing.

August gave a slow, arrogant nod. He raised his paddle.

"One hundred and fifty thousand," August's deep voice boomed across the room.

Elisa's jaw clenched so hard her teeth ached. She raised her paddle again. "Two hundred thousand."

August didn't even blink. He didn't look at the stage; he kept his eyes locked on Elisa. "Four hundred thousand."

A low murmur swept through the amphitheater. The billionaires in the room instantly realized this was no longer an auction. It was a public execution. It was a billionaire using his wallet as a weapon to crush his ex-employee.

Allena turned around in her seat. She looked directly at Elisa, a sickeningly sweet, victorious smile plastered on her face. She intentionally leaned her head onto August's shoulder, rubbing her cheek against his tuxedo.
Elisa's fingers gripped the wooden handle of the paddle until her knuckles turned white. She looked at Alastair. Alastair gave her a firm,reassuring nod.

Elisa raised the paddle. "One million dollars."

The crowd gasped. Bidding a million dollars for a necklace worth maybe two hundred thousand was sheer madness.

August's face darkened. The muscles in his neck bulged. He had not expected her to have the capital to fight back. He shifted his gaze to Alastair.He instantly assumed Alastair was funding her. The thought of another man financing 'his wife's defiance pushed August over the edge of sanity.

He would not be humiliated in front of Wall Street.

August slowly raised his paddle. His voice was terrifyingly calm, carrying the absolute weight of the Chambers empire.

"Five million dollars."

The subterranean room went dead_silent. You could hear a pin drop. Five million dollars for a Victorian locket was financial insanity.

Elisa stared at August. The fight drained out of her body. Her arm slowly lowered, the paddle resting on her lap.
She could not do it. Even with Alastair's black card,her logical, architectural brain refused to let her drag Aethel Intelligence into a five-million-dollar loss over a personal vendetta. She had hit her absolute limit.

She closed her eyes. The crushing weight of defeat settled into her bones. She had lost her mother's memory to the man who had destroyed her life.①
###Chapter 108


The echo of "Five million dollars" hung in the subterranean auction room like a physical weight.

The auctioneer gripped the edges of his podium,his hands visibly shaking. A commission on a five-million-dollar sale was life-changing.He raised his wooden gavel high into the air.

"Five million dollars going once!" the auctioneer cried out, his voice cracking with excitement."Going twice!"

Elisa sat in the shadows. She bit down on her lower lip so hard she tasted the sharp, metallic tang of her own blood. She stared at the glass case,watching the physical embodiment of her family's history slip away, destined to be worn around the neck of the woman who had stolen her husband.

Just as the wooden gavel began its downward arc,Preston raised his hand.

"Hold the hammer," Preston commanded.

The auctioneer froze, the gavel hovering an inch above the sounding block.
Preston walked to the center of the stage. He stood next to the velvet pedestal, looking out over the crowd of billionaires.

"Ladies and gentlemen," Preston said, his voice smooth and authoritative. "The anonymous seller of this specific artifact placed a unique stipulation in the contract. If the final bidding price exceeds ten times the appraised value of the item,the seller grants the auction house the right to select the buyer based on strategic merit, not just the highest dollar amount."

Preston's eyes swept the room. For a brief second,his gaze landed on Elisa in the back row. His eyes were cold and judgmental. He remembered the scene in the hallway. He had no intention of letting a manipulative gold-digger win.

Preston turned his attention entirely to August.

"Mr. Chambers," Preston said, a sharp, predatory businessman's smile forming on his lips. "Five million dollarsis an incredibly generous offer.However, Vance Capital values long-term, strategic partnerships over immediate cash injections."

The room held its breath.

"I am willing to sell this locket to you for its base reserve price of two hundred thousand dollars,"
Preston offered publicly. "On one condition. The Chambers Group immediately divests its entire thirty percent stake in the Solaris Tech merger and sells it to Vance Capital at a twenty percent discount. The transfer must be executed by market open tomorrow."

A collective, stunned gasp erupted from the hedge fund managers in the room.

It was a masterstroke of Wall Street extortion.Solaris Tech was a direct competitor to one of Preston's key holdings. Forcing August to sell would not only give Preston a massive financial windfall but would also cripple his rival. He was leveraging a piece of jewelry to execute a brutal,short-term corporate kill.

Allena gripped August's sleeve. She didn't understand what a merger was, but she heard the murmurs of the crowd. She knew it meant giving up something huge. She looked at August nervously.

August sat perfectly still. He looked at Preston,acknowledging the ruthless brilliance of the move.Then, he slowly turned his head and looked at the back row.

He saw Elisa sitting there, her face pale, her eyes fixed on the necklace.
