The twisted, sadistic thrill of absolute domination flooded August's veins. He wanted to crush her. He wanted to show Alastair Cromwell and every man in this room that his wealth and power were infinite.He would burn tens of millions of dollars just to watch Elisa bleed.

August turned back to Preston. He gave a single,arrogant nod.

"Deal," August stated. "Pleasure doing business with you,Vance."

The room erupted into furious whispers. The billionaires shook their heads in disbelief. August Chambers had just traded a fortune in corporate leverage to buy a bauble for his mistress. It was a display of wealth so obscene it bordered on madness.

Allena let out a high-pitched squeal. She threw her arms around August's neck, kissing his jaw repeatedly. "Thank you, thank you, baby! You are the best man in the world!"

Preston smiled, satisfied. He had secured a massive win for his fund, and he felt morally justified in helping the "wronged" fiancée.

Preston personally unlocked the glass case. He removed the sapphire locket, placed it into a black
velvet box, and walked off the stage. He handed the box directly to August.

August took the box. He did not hand it to Allena.

Instead, August stood up. He turned his back on the stage and began walking up the carpeted steps of the amphitheater, heading straight for the back row.

The whispers died instantly. Every single pair of eyes in the room tracked August's movement.
August stopped directly in front of Elisa. He stood tall, his broad shoulders blocking the stage lights,casting a dark shadow over her.

He looked down at her. His eyes were filled with a cruel,mocking triumph.

August slowly opened the velvet box. He tilted it forward, presenting the flawless sapphire locket just inches from Elisa's face. She could smell the faint, metallic scent of the old gold.

"Do you like it?" August asked. His voice was a low,venomous whisper designed to inflict maximum psychological damage. "It's a shame. You can't afford it. And it seems your new tech-billionaire sugar daddy can't afford it either."

Elisa's entire body began to tremble. Her eyes
burned with unshed tears of pure, unadulterated humiliation. Her chest tightened so severely she felt like her ribs were cracking. But she locked her jaw. She refused to let a single tear fall in front of him.

August stared at her trembling form for two agonizing seconds. Then, he snapped the velvet box shut with a loud, sharp crack.

He turned around and walked back down the stairs to Allena.

He opened the box, took out the necklace, and personally clasped it around Allena's neck. The blue sapphires rested against her collarbone,glittering under the lights.

Allena touched the stones. She looked up the stairs, locked eyes with Elisa, and flashed a massive, victorious, taunting smile.

In the back row, Alastair's face contorted with violent rage. He planted his hands on his knees,preparing to stand up and physically drag August out of the room.

Elisa's hand shot out. Her fingers clamped around Alastair's wrist with desperate, bruising force.

"Don't," Elisa choked out, her voice barely a
whisper. "If you hit him here, we both lose everything. Let it go."

She closed her eyes. She took one long,shuddering breath.

When she opened her eyes again, the humiliation was gone. The temperature in her black eyes had dropped to absolute zero.②

Elisa stood up. She did not look at August. She did not look at the necklace. She turned around and walked out of the auction room, her spine rigid,leaving the toxic theater behind.①
###Chapter 109


Elisa pushed through the heavy glass doors of the gallery.

A violent blast of freezing wind hit her instantly. The temperature in Manhattan had plummeted. Thick,heavy snowflakes were falling from the black sky swirling aggressively in the streetlights.

She wore only her thin navy suit jacket, but she couldn't feel the cold. The humiliation and rage burning inside her chest were a physical furnace.She stood at the top of the concrete stairs, her boots planted firmly, her eyes locked onto the circular driveway.

Ten minutes later, the gallery doors opened behind her.

August walked out, his arm wrapped tightly around Allena. Allena was draped in a massive, luxurious white mink coat, laughing at something August had said.

At the bottom of the stairs, Simon stood holding the rear door of August's signature black Rolls-Royce Phantom open. The massive engine idled with a low,powerful hum.
Elisa took a deep breath. The freezing air burned her lungs. She marched down the concrete stairs,her heels clicking sharply against the ice, and stepped directly into the path of the Rolls-Royce door.

Two of August's massive bodyguards instantly lunged forward to intercept her.

August raised a single, gloved hand. The bodyguards froze and stepped back. August looked at Elisa standing in the snow, her suit jacket whipping in the wind. A dark, twisted amusement danced in his eyes. He wanted to see how far she would bend.

Elisa ignored Allena's disgusted glare. She locked her eyes onto August. Her voice shook, betraying the raw, desperate emotion she was trying to suppress.

"That necklace," Elisa said, her voice tight. "It was my grandmother's dowry. It is my mother's only remaining artifact. Name your price, August. How much money do you want for it?"

It was the first time in seven years August had seen genuine, unfiltered pleading in her eyes.

A strange, sharp pain twitched in the center of August's chest. For a microsecond, the image of
her standing in the snow, begging for her mother's memory,pierced his armor.

But then his mind flashed back to the dark hallway.He remembered the nerve strike on his arm.He remembered her standing shoulder-to-shoulder with Alastair, mocking him. The sadistic need to punish her violently overrode his momentary hesitation.

August let out a cold, breathy laugh. He reached into his overcoat pocket and pulled out the black velvet box.

August stopped. He turned his head and looked at Allena. He extended his open palm toward her, his voice carrying an unquestionable, dark authority."Give it to me."

Allena blinked, momentarily confused, but she obediently unclasped the heavy sapphire locket and dropped it into his hand. She watched with a sick, rising thrill, knowing exactly what he was about to do.

August dropped the locket into the black velvet box and casually tossed the five-million-dollar box a few inches into the air and caught it, mocking its value.

"Name my price?" August sneered, his voice
dripping with absolute arrogance. "You can't even swipe a debit card to buy a cup of coffee right now.What exactly are you going to pay me with, Elisa?"

Elisa swallowed the bile in her throat. "I will sign over my equity options in Aethel Intelligence to you.I will give you everything. Just give me the locket."

The mention of Aethel-of the company owned by the man who had humiliated him-caused August's face to harden into a mask of pure cruelty.

He stopped tossing the box. He turned and handed it directly to Allena.

"It is no longer my property to sell," August said, his eyes locked onto Elisa's pale face. "It belongs to Allena."

August leaned in slightly, delivering the final, lethal blow. "If you really want it, you can get on your knees and beg her for it. Maybe if she is feeling charitable, she will throw it to you."

The words acted like a physical knife plunging directly into Elisa's heart.

Beg the woman who had stolen her husband? Beg the daughter of the man who had abandoned her dying mother?

Allena let out a high, triumphant giggle. She
stroked the velvet box lovingly. "Oh, I could never give this away," Allena taunted. "It's a symbol of August's love for me. It's far too precious to give to an outsider."

Elisa stared at the two of them. The snow fell heavily around her, landing on her dark hair and melting against her freezing cheeks.

The last, desperate thread of hope snapped. The pleading vanished from her eyes, replaced by a terrifying,bottomnless void.

She took one step backward. Her spine straightened.

"You will pay for everything you have done today,August," Elisa whispered. Her voice was softer than the falling snow, but it carried a lethal, absolute certainty. "I will make sure of it."⑥

August scoffed, completely dismissing the threat.He placed a hand on Allena's back, guiding her into the warm, luxurious interior of the Rolls-Royce.He climbed in after her.

The heavy door slammed shut with a solid,expensive thud.

The Rolls-Royce's engine revved. The massive tires spun slightly on the wet pavement before gripping
the asphalt. As the car pulled away, the tires splashed a wave of freezing, dirty slush onto the hem of Elisa's navy trousers.

Elisa stood perfectly still in the snow. She watched the red taillights disappear into the Manhattan blizzard. A single tear escaped her eye, sliding down her cheek before the freezing wind turned it to ice.

The heavy gallery doors opened behind her.Alastair jogged down the stairs. He strippedoff his heavy, cashmere overcoat and draped it over Elisa's trembling shoulders, pulling the Japels tight to shield her from the wind.

Elisa did not turn around. She stared at the empty street.

"Alastair," Elisa said, her voice completely devoid of emotion. "Book me a flight to Aspen."

Alastair froze. He looked at the side of her face in shock. "Aspen? Elisa, that is Chambers territory. It's an absolute trap. Why would you walk into the lion's den?"

Elisa slowly turned her head. The fire of pure,unadulterated vengeance burned in her black eyes.

"Because," Elisa whispered, "I am going to wait
until they are at the absolute peak of their arrogance. And then, I am going to push them off the mountain."⑤
###Chapter 110


Two days later, the massive engines of a Gulfstream G650 screamed as the private jet touched down on the icy runway of the Aspen/Pitkin County Airport in Colorado.

Elisa stepped out of the cabin and descended the metal stairs. She wore a thick, black cashmere turtleneck and a long, tailored wool trench coat.She pulled a simple black carry-on suitcase behind her.

She was here under the official capacity of Aethel Intelligence. Aethel was the primary sponsor of a joint Al medical symposium hosted by the JHU clinical team in Aspen.

Alastair had been furious she was going alone, but just before takeoff, his chief of security had burst into the hangar. Aethel's core servers were under a sustained, sophisticated cyberattack from an unknown, state-level entity. The assault was so complex that Alastair had to remain in the New York command center to personally direct the defense.He had doubled her tactical security detail and sworn to fly to Aspen the second the digital war was won, leaving Elisa to navigate the hostile
territory entirely alone for the first forty-eight hours.

A massive, black Cadillac Escalade idled near the edge of the tarmac. A driver in a heavy winter parka stood beside it, holding a digital tablet that read:Dr.Wilder (Faye).

Elisa approached the vehicle. She handed her bag to the driver and climbed into the heated back seat.

As the Escalade navigated the winding mountain roads, Elisa stared out the tinted windows. The Rocky Mountains were buried under feet of pristine white snow. The landscape was breathtakingly beautiful, but to Elisa, it felt like a massive, frozen cage.

She noticed the route. The vehicle was not heading toward the St. Regis or The Little Nell, the luxury hotels where the symposium was officially being held.

Instead, the Escalade turned off the main highway.It began climbing a steep, private access road heavily lined with towering, snow-covered pine trees.

Ten minutes later, the trees cleared. The vehicle pulled up to a jaw-dropping,three-story modern chalet. The architecture was a brutalist mix of dark
timber and massive, floor-to-ceiling glass walls overlooking the valley.

Elisa's stomach instantly tightened. She recognized the security detail. Four men in black tactical winter gear stood near the entrance. They were Chambers family private security.

Before Elisa could demand the driver turn around,the heavy oak front door of the chalet swung open.

Simon stepped out onto the snow-covered porch.He wore a gray cashmere sweater, looking entirely too comfortable.

The driver opened Elisa's door. She stepped out,the freezing mountain air biting at her cheeks.

Simon walked down the steps and politely took her suitcase from the driver.

"Welcome to Aspen, Miss Wilder," Simon said smoothly. "Since Aethel Intelligence so generously sponsored the symposium, Mr. Chambers felt it was only appropriate to host the core JHU medical team here at the family's private estate."

Elisa's eyes narrowed into dangerous slits. Her brain processed the trap instantly. August had used the corporate sponsorship as a flawless excuse to legally and socially isolate her on his private
property.

"I am staying at the hotel in town," Elisa stated coldly. She turned around, reaching for the door handle of the Escalade.①

Two of the massive bodyguards immediately stepped forward. They didn't draw weapons, but they positioned their bodies like solid brick walls between Elisa and the vehicle.

Simon let out a polite, apologetic sigh. "I am afraid that is impossible. As the primary sponsor, I'm sure you understand the need for enhanced security.Mr.Chambers has upgraded the entire symposium to a Level Four security protocol. All official attendees,including the entire JHU team, are required to stay on this estate. We will be transporting everyone to the conference venue in secured convoys. Anyone who does not comply will have their access credentials immediately revoked to protect the integrity of the intellectual property being discussed."③

Simon took a step closer, lowering his voice."Furthermore, the entire JHU team is already inside. If the lead Aethel represetative suddenly refuses the host's hospitality and flees, it will cause a massive professional scandal. It would look very...unstable."
Elisa locked her jaw. August had checkmated her using her own professional obligations. If she threw a tantrum and ran into the snow, she would validate the "crazy woman" narrative he had been spinning.

She took a slow, deep breath, suppressing the violent urge to punch Simon in the throat. She turned and marched up the wooden steps,entering the chalet.

The interior was suffocatingly luxurious. A massive stone fireplace roared in the center of the grand living room. The air smelled of burning pine and cinnamon.

Elisa saw several doctors from the JHU team,including Dr. Finch, lounging on the leather sofas.When they saw her walk in, a palpable wave of tension shot through the group. They instantly sat up straighter, their conversations ceasing mid-sentence. They looked like terrified students whose notoriously strict professor had just entered the room. They offered stiff, panicked nods of greeting before quickly averting their eyes, suddenly finding the burning logs in the fireplace fascinating. The air became thick with their fear and the awkwardness of the forced proximity.

Simon guided her past the living room and up a floating glass staircase to the second floor.He led
her down a long, carpeted hallway to the heavy wooden door at the very end.

Simon pulled a black electronic keycard from his pocket and handed it to her.

"Your suite, Madam," Simon murmured,intentionally using the title she despised. He bowed his head slightly and walked away.

Elisa gripped the keycard. She swiped it against the lock. The light flashed green, and she pushed the door open.

The suite was massive. A wall of glass'provided a panoramic view of the darkening,snow-covered mountains.

But as Elisa stepped into the room,her blood instantly turned to ice.

In the center of the room sat a massive, king-size bed.

Draped casually over the velvet sofa at the foot of the bed was a heavy, dark gray men's overcoat.Next to it, resting on a luggage rack, was a custom leather duffel bag stamped with the gold Chambers family crest.

The sound of running water suddenly stopped.
The frosted glass door of the en-suite bathroom clicked open.

August Chambers stepped out into the bedroom.
His dark hair was wet, water droplets trailing down his neck and over his heavily muscled chest.He was wearing nothing but a white towel slung low around his waist. He held a glass of amber whiskey in his right hand.

August stopped. He looked at Elisa standing frozen in the doorway, her eyes wide with absolute,horrified realization.

A slow, dark, predatory smile spread across August's face. He raised his whiskey glass in a mocking toast.

"I trust Simon found you suitable accommodations?" August murmured, his voice a low purr of victory. "After all, the Chambers family always takes care of its...associates."
###Chapter 111


Elisa stood frozen in the doorway of the master suite. The heavy scent of cedarwood body wash and expensive amber whiskey hit her lungs, making her chest physically tighten.

August stood a few feet away, water dripping from his dark hair onto his bare, heavily muscled chest.The white towel slung low on his hips was the only thing separating them. He raised his crystal glass,the ice clinking sharply in the quiet room, his lips curved into a dark, victorious smile.

Elisa did not speak. She spun around. Her hand clamped around the heavy brass door handle. She yanked it downward with all her body weight,desperate to escape the suffocating air of the room.

The handle did not budge. A faint red light glowed on the lock's panel. The suite's security protocol had been activated remotely.

A low, dark chuckle vibrated from the center of the room. August leaned his bare shoulder against the bathroom doorframe. He took a slow sip of his whiskey, his eyes tracking her panicked movements
like a predator watching a trapped animal hit the glass of its cage.

Elisa squeezed her eyes shut. She forced a deep,freezing breath into her lungs. The panic receded,replaced by a wall of solid ice.

She turned around. Her black eyes locked onto the half-naked man, completely devoid of any human warmth.

She walked past him, her boots sinking into the thick carpet. She stopped at the velvet sofa at the foot of the king-size bed. August's heavy cashmere overcoat was draped across the cushions.

Elisa grabbed the expensive fabric. Without a single ounce of hesitation, she hurled the coat onto the floor, clearing a small, physical space that belonged entirely to her.

The amusement vanished from August's face. His jaw muscles flexed violently.

He set his whiskey glass down on the nightstand with a sharp crack. He closed the distance between them in three long strides. His large, damp hand clamped around her slender wrist like an ion vice.

"Do not test my patience in this room, Elisa,"August warned, his voice dropping to a dangerous,
gravelly frequency.
Elisa looked down at his hand, then up to his eyes.She violently ripped her wrist out of his grip.

"If you touch me again," Elisa stated1, her voice a flat, dead monotone, "I will pick up that floor lamp and smash the floor-to-ceiling window. I will let the freezing wind blow in, and I will scream until every single JHU doctor in this chalet comes running to see the billionaire CEO trying to force himself on his estranged wife."

August's pupils contracted. He stared at her,searching for a bluff. He found none. She was completely unhinged, willing to trigger a massive,humiliating public scandal just to keep him away.

His extreme obsession with his public image won.August let out a harsh, breathy scoff.

"You flatter yourself," he sneered. He turned his back on her and walked toward the massive bed,sliding beneath the heavy down comforter.

Midnight crept into the Aspen valley. The wind howled against the reinforced glass.

Elisa did not take off her clothes. She curled into a tight ball on the narrow velvet sofa, pulling her wool trench coat over her body. She kept her eyes fixed
on the dark ceiling, maintaining a shallow, hyper-vigilant state of sleep.

Hours passed. The first piercing rays of morning sunlight sliced through the glass walls, hitting the carpet.

Elisa groaned softly. The old, deep ache in her lower back flared up from sleeping on the stiff cushions. She shifted her weight, rolling onto her side.

Instantly, a heavy, radiating heat pressed against her spine.

A thick, muscular arm wrapped violently around her waist, yanking her backward. Her back slammed flush against a broad, bare chest.

August had left the bed. In the freezing, high-altitude temperature of the room, his unconscious body had sought out the nearest source of body heat.

He buried his face into the curve of her neck. His stubble scratched against her sensitive skin.

Elisa's entire body went rigid. Her heart hammered against her ribs. She pulled her elbow back,preparing to drive it directly into his ribs.
Before she could strike, a rough, sleep-heavy
whisper brushed against her ear.

"Allena..." August mumbled, his voice thick with blind, unconscious affection.

The name acted like a live electrical wire snapping against her bare skin.

A violent, uncontrollable wave of nausea exploded in Elisa's stomach. The bile rose so fastit burned the back of her throat. The sheer, physical disgust of being held by him while he dreamed of the woman who destroyed her family was unbearable.

Elisa let out a choked gasp. She shoved her elbows backward with explosive force, breaking his grip.

She scrambled off the sofa, her legs shaking.She sprinted across the room and slammed the bathroom door open.

She dropped to her knees in front of the cold marble toilet. She gripped the porcelain edges, her knuckles turning white, and violently dry-heaved.Hot tears of pure physiological rejection spilled from the corners of her eyes.

The loud, echoing sounds of her gagging shattered the quiet morning.

August jolted awake. He sat up on the sofa, his brow furrowed in deep confusion. He stood up and
strode quickly to the open bathroom door.

He looked down at Elisa. She was pale, shaking,and coughing over the bowl.

A sudden, terrifying thought struck August's brain like a physical blow. The memory of the socialite's rumor at the club flashed behind his eyes. She is trying to secretly get pregnant.

August's pupils dilated. The color drained from his face.

He stepped into the bathroom. He grabbed Elisa by the shoulder, his fingers digging painfully into her collarbone.

"Are you pregnant?" August demanded. His voice cracked,laced with a raw, suffocating panic.

Elisa shoved his hand away. She stood up on shaking legs and turned to the sink. She turned the silver faucet on full blast, splashing freezing water onto her pale face to force the nausea down.

She grabbed a towel and pressed it to her mouth.She slowly lifted her head.

She looked at August's reflection in the mirror.His face was twisted in absolute terror at the thought of her carrying his child.
A cold, hollow laugh scraped its way out of Elisa's throat.

She turned around. She leaned back against the marble counter, crossing her arms over her chest.She looked him dead in the eye, weaponizing her words.

"You watched me swallow a pill every single morning for seven years like I was a thief," Elisa said, her voice dripping with venomous mockery."Your sperm never even had a chance to survive in my body,August."

August's jaw locked. The blunt, crude humiliation of her words hit him hard. His fingers twitched,wanting to grab her again.

Elisa didn't stop. She tilted her head, her black eyes burning with absolute hatred.

"And even if every other man on this planet died,"Elisa whispered, "I would rather sterilize myself than allow your tainted genetics to continue for another generation."

The words hit the exact center of his fragile,massive ego. It was a direct, lethal strike to his pride.

August physically recoiled. He dropped his hand as
if her skin had suddenly turned to burning acid.
The panic in his eyes vanished, instantly replaced by a cold, murderous arrogance.

"You better remember exactly what you just said,"August hissed.

He turned on his heel and marched out of the bathroom, heading straight for the walk-in closet to dress.

Elisa stood alone in the bathroom. She gripped the edges of the marble sink so hard her fingernails dug into the stone. Her chest heaved.

Only she knew the truth. Only she knew how terrifyingly close his panicked guess had come to the five-year-old secret currently hiding in New York.
###Chapter 112

Half an hour later, the heavy wooden door of the master suite clicked open.

Elisa stepped out. She wore a minimalist, cream-colored cashmere turtleneck and tailored dark trousers. The high collar hid the pulse jumping in her throat. Her face was a mask of perfect,impenetrable calm.

She walked down the floating glass staircase and entered the main lobby of the chalet.

The space was massive. A giant, raw-edge wooden dining table dominated the center ofthe room,loaded with silver platters of European pastries,fresh fruit, and steaming carafes of coffee.The massive stone fireplace crackled loudly, throwing heat into the cold morning air.

A group of core JHU medical team members stood near the coffee station. They held ceramic mugs,speaking in low,hushed tones.

The moment Elisa's boots clicked against the hardwood floor, the conversation died. The air in the lobby instantly thickened with a subtle, hostile awkwardness.
Tessa Vaughn, a junior researcher from the Aethel team, sat at the far end of the table. She waved enthusiastically.

Elisa walked over and pulled out the heavy wooden chair next to Tessa.

As Elisa reached for a coffee cup, her sleeve pulled back slightly. The warm firelight caught the face of the vintage Patek Philippe watch strapped to her left wrist.

Tessa's eyes widened. She leaned in, her voice dropping to a loud, conspiratorial whisper.

"Oh my god, Elisa," Tessa gasped. "Is that a Patek?Did your legendary, secret billionaire husband buy that for you as a New Year's gift?"

Elisa bought the watch herself using her first massive paycheck as Faye. But to the outside world, she was still just a former ER nurse.

Tessa's loud whisper carried across the quiet room.The JHU doctors at the coffee station turned their heads. One of them murmured to his coleague,just loud enough to be overheard, "I heard she's the reason Project Chiron is so far behind schedule." The other snorted. "It's always the ones who marry into money that are the least competent." A collective sneer formed on their
faces.
The Chief of Surgery, a man in his fifties who worshipped August Chambers, took a slow sip of his coffee, hearing the exchange and seizing the opportunity to lead the charge.

"Well, that certainly explainsa lot," the Chief said,his voice dripping with condescension. "No wonder Professor Finch said your data models are completely detached from clinical reality, Miss Wilder. I suppose sitting in a cozy office typing on a keyboard is much easier than standing at an operating table for ten hours. It must be nice having that kind of... private capital backing you up so you don't have to face real patients."

A female doctor standing next to him let out a sharp,mocking laugh.

"Honestly," the woman chimed in, "to keep a man that rich atotal secret for this long, he must be old enough to be her grandfather. I bet he needs a nurse more than a wife."

A low, cruel wave of laughter rippled through the JHU team. The blatant, sexist assumption hung in the air,thick and suffocating.

Elisa's fingers slowly tightened around the handle of her ceramic mug. She did not flush. She did not
yell. She simply turned her head and let her dead,black eyes sweep over the group of doctors,looking at them as if they were a fascinating species of insect.

"Actually, Chief," Elisa said, her voice a flat,mechanical hum that cut through the laughter."Professor Finch's exact words were that my data models highlighted the uncomfortable, undeniable reality of your surgical outcomes. My algorithms predicted the exact complication rate of your last three coronary bypasses. You dismissed the Al's warning as 'detached from reality,' and your patients suffered a twenty percent longer recovery time."

Elisa took a slow, calm sip of her coffee, her gaze piercing right through his fragile ego. "Perhaps if you spent less time worrying about my private capital and more time reading the actual charts,you wouldn't be begging Aethel for a fifty-million-dollar bailout."

The laughter from the JHU team died instantly.The doctors froze, their eyes widening in shock. The female doctor's face drained of color. Tessa's jaw dropped open in pure shock. The absolute silence in the lobby was deafening.

The Chief of Surgery's face turned a violent shade
of purple, his mouth opening and closing like a suffocating fish, unable to refute the raw, clinical data she had just weaponized against him in front of his entire staff.

Right as the tension reached a breaking point, the soft, rhythmic clicking of high heels echoed from the top of the spiral staircase.

Every head in the lobby turned.

Allena Mills descended the stairs. She wore a disgustingly lavish, pure white mink ski suit. Her blonde hair was perfectly styled. Her arm was looped tightly through the arm of the man walking beside her.

August Chambers wore a custom, dark charcoal ski suit. He walked with one hand casually tucked into his pocket. He moved with the slow, heavy arrogance of an emperor inspecting his subjects.

They looked like a flawless, untouchable advertisement for Wall Street royalty.

The JHU doctors instantly snapped out of their shock. They scrambled forward, their faces twisting into desperate, fawning smiles.

"Good morning, Mr. Chambers! Good morning,Miss Mills!" they chorused, desperate to please the
man who had just donated fifty million dollars to their hospital.

Allena soaked up the attention. She pressed her breasts tighter against August's arm and offered the room a sickly sweet, practiced smile."Good morning, everyone! It's so beautiful outside!"

August did not look at the doctors. His dark,predatory eyes scanned the room like a radar.

He instantly locked onto the corner of the dining table. He saw Elisa sitting there. She was sitting perfectly straight, sipping her coffee, radiating an aura of complete, untouchable isolation.

August's jaw clamped shut with bone-crushing force. The muscles in his neck bulged. The sight of her sitting there, completely unfazed by the hostile environment, ignited a dark, toxic fire in his chest.She didn't need him. She didn't need anyone.

Elisa picked up her coffee cup. She took a slow,calm sip. She stared blankly at the 'perfect couple'standing on the stairs, treating them with the exact same emotional weight as the wooden furniture.

The silent, suffocating war in the Aspen chalet had officially begun.
###Chapter 113

August guided Allena down the final steps.

The Chief of Surgery practically tripped over his own feet rushing to the head of the massive wooden table. He pulled out the heavy, carved chair at the head of the table, bowing his head slightly.

"Please,Mr. Chambers, sit here," the Chief offered,his voice dripping with sycophantic desperation.

August sat down. Allena immediately took the seat directly to his right. She acted like the undisputed queen of the castle, waving a manicured hand at a passing server to pour August a cup of black coffee. She shot a triumphant, gloating look across the table toward Elisa.

The atmosphere in the lobby shifted. The casual morning energy vanished, replaced by a rigid,suffocating tension. The JHU doctors sat up straighter, terrified of making a mistake in front of the billionaire.

The Chief of Surgery, still stinging from the public humiliation of Elisa's data-driven takedown, saw a chance to regain favor by shifting the attack back
onto her.
The Chief picked up his glass of orange juice.He forced a casual chuckle and looked directly at August.

"Mr. Chambers," the Chief said, his voice carrying across the quiet room. "Before you came down,we were actually just discussing Miss Wilder's mysterious husband."

August's hand froze halfway to his coffee cup.

The muscles in his broad shoulders locked. His dark, piercing eyes slowly lifted, shooting straight down the length of the table to lock onto Elisa.

Elisa did not look up. She kept her head down, her silver knife slicing methodically through the fried egg on her plate. Her movements were perfectly smooth, entirely mechanical, as if she hadn't heard her own name.

The Chief, completely oblivious to the lethal tension, kept talking.

"We were just saying," the Chief laughed, "that every man in New York looks entirely inadequate standing next to you, sir. I'm sure Elisa's husband is no exception."

Tessa shifted uncomfortably in her seat. She
cleared her throat, trying to change the subject."The weatherlooks like it might clear up for the symposium-"

"Oh, I'm actually very curious," Allena interrupted.Her voice was high-pitched and coated in fake,sugary innocence.

Allena rested her chin on her hands and stared directly at Elisa.

"Tell us, Elisa," Allena cooed, her eyes flashing with pure malice. "What kind of man is he? He must be so... unique. I mean, that wedding ring is so...simple. It looks like something his father would have picked out. Didn't August ever bother to buy you something that reflects his own taste? Oh, wait.I meant, didn't your husband bother? Is he someone you have to hide in the dark?"①

The implication was brutal and obvious. Allena was publicly painting Elisa as a desperate woman trapped in a miserable, loveless marriage, or worse,a mistress to an ugly old man.

A few of the JHU doctors let out low, knowing chuckles. They stared at Elisa, waiting for her to break.

Tessa's chest expanded as she took a sharp breath. She planted her hands on the table,
preparing to verbally defend her colleague from the blatant bullying.

Under the table, Elisa's hand shot out. She gripped Tessa's wrist with bruising force, pinning her in place. She shot the young researcher a single,freezing warning glance. Do not engage.

Then, Elisa slowly placed her knife and fork down on the porcelain plate.

She lifted her chin. Her black eyes met Allena's mocking gaze. Her face was a mask of absolute,terrifying calm.

"You are absolutely right, Allena," Elisa said. Her voice was flat, carrying no anger, only a cold,clinical certainty. "He is exactly that. A hidden piece of trash."

The entire dining table gasped. The sound of breathing stopped.

Nobody expected a woman to describe her own husband with such raw, visceral disgust in a public setting.

August's face turned the color of a bruised storm cloud. The blood drained from his lips. His fingers clamped around the handle of his ceramic coffee mug so hard the joints popped. He knew exactly
who she was talking about. She was looking right at his mistress and calling him trash to his face.

But he was trapped. He had built the lie of his single status. He could not scream, I am her husband! without destroying his own narrative.

Allena, completely missing the subtext, thought Elisa was just pathetically self-deprecating. Her smile widened into a grotesque display of victory.She turned to August, seeking validation.

A young JHU researcher, eager to join the bullying,leaned forward.

"Well, regardless of Elisa's situation," the researcher said, raising his coffee mug toward Allena, "Miss Mills is definitely the luckiest woman here. After all, we are sitting with the future Mrs.Chambers."

The title future Mrs. Chambers echoed loudly in the cavernous room.

Every single person at the table froze. They looked at August. In the brutal, calculated world of Wall Street, billionaires never allowed anyone to use legally binding titles like "fiancée" or "Mrs." unless the contracts were already signed.

August stared at Elisa. He saw the comnplete,dead
emptiness in her eyes. The urge to punish her for calling him trash overrode every logical circuit in his brain.

He chose to use the weapon handed to him.

August did not correct the researcher.Instead,he remained perfectly silent. He slowly lifted his hand and gently tucked a stray blonde curl behind Allena's ear.

It was a physical, undeniable confirmation of the title.

The table erupted. The JHU doctors clapped their hands, raising their glasses, offering loud, fawning congratulations to the "future boss lady."

Allena's eyes filled with fake, happy tears. She looked at Elisa, her face glowing with the ultimate,undisputed victory.

Elisa sat perfectly still. She watched her legal husband of seven years publicly hand her title, her dignity, and her place in the world to the woman who had destroyed her family.

She felt no rage. She felt no urge to cry.
Her chest simply hollowed out, leaving nothing but a cold, empty cavity. The event didn't feel like a final snap, but like another layer of ice forming over
a frozen lake, making the darkness below even more profound and unreachable.O

Elisa picked up her white linen napkin. She elegantly dabbed the corner of her mouth,her movements as precise as a machine.

She turned her head to Tessa.

"I'm full," Elisa whispered, her voice completely dead. "Let's go to the conference room and prep the data."

She pushed her chair back, the wood scraping harshly against the floor, ready to leave the rotting corpses of her past behind.
###Chapter 114


Elisa stood up. The heavy wooden chair scraped against the floorboards, the sound cutting sharply through the sickening chorus of congratulations.

Tessa stood up immediately after her. But the young researcher couldn't hold her tongue any longer. The injustice was suffocating.

Tessa buttoned her blazer. She turned her small frame and locked a nervous, but defiant gaze directly onto August at the head of the table.

"Mr. Chambers," Tessa said. Her voice trembled slightly, but it carried across the quiet room. "As Aethel's primary partner in this venture, I have a professional curiosity I'd like to satisfy."

The laughter and clapping around the table died instantly. The JHU doctors shrank back into their seats, sensing the sudden, awkward shift in the room's barometric pressure. A junior researcher was addressing the billionaire directly.

August leaned back slowly into his chair. He crossed his hands over his flat stomach. His dark eyes narrowed into dangerous slits.
"Go on," August commanded, his voice a block of ice.

Tessa swallowed hard, gesturing nervously toward Elisa.

"Aethel's background checks are extremely thorough, especially for someone with Elisa's high-level security clearance," Tessa began, enunciating her words carefully. "I've heard rumors that Elisa's husband is actually a man of immense power.A titan in the New York medical and financial sectors."

Tessa paused. She let the silence stretch for two agonizing seconds. She leaned forward slightly, her anxiety masked by genuine curiosity.

"You are the undisputed king of New York commerce, Mr. Chambers," Tessa said."Given your extensive network, are you truly telling me you have absolutely no idea who this powerful man is?"

The question dropped into the center of the table like a live fragmentation grenade.

The room went dead silent. The JHU doctors looked back and forth between the researcher and the billionaire, completely confused. They thought Tessa was just using Elisa to challenge August's intelligence network.
But Allena understood the threat. The blood instantly vanished from her face. Her skin turned a sickly, translucent white. She stared at August in absolute terror, her fingernails digging into her own palms. If August lost his temper and claimed his territory, her entire fairy tale would burn to the ground.

Elisa's boots stopped moving. Her spine locked into a rigid, perfectly straight line. She did not turn around. She kept her back to the table, but every muscle in her body pulled tight.

This was it. For the first time in seven years,someone had dragged the ghost of her marriage into the light and shoved it directly into August's face.

August's pupils contracted to pinpricks. He stared at Tessa. He saw the trap perfectly laid out before him.

If he admitted he knew the husband,he would be forced to explain his intimate knowledge of a "low-level nurse," destroying his untouchable facade in front of Allena and his sycophants.

If he denied it, he would be publicly executing his own marriage, throwing his title as her husband into the dirt for everyone to step on.
Time froze. August's gaze drifted past Tessa. He looked at the back of Elisa's cream-colored sweater. He looked at her rigid posture. He remembered her calling him trash.He remembered the dead, empty look in her eyes.

A sick, twisted wave of jealous pride violently hijacked his brain. He would not give this junior employee the satisfaction of a confession. He would rather destroy the truth than share it.
August slowly pulled his gaze back to Tessa. He picked up his coffee cup and took a slow,deliberate sip.

"Miss Vaughn," August said. His voice was entirely devoid of human emotion. It was the voice of a machine executing a deletion protocol. "My time is extremely valuable. I do not care, nor do I have the patience, to investigate the pathetic private lives of irrelevant,bottom-feeder employees."

August set the cup down. The ceramic clicked sharply against the saucer.

"Whether her husband is a homeless beggar or the Mayor of New York," August stated, his eyes cold and dead, "he means absolutely nothing to me."

The words echoed off the stone walls. They were absolute.Irrevocable.
The JHU doctors immediately nodded in vigorous agreement, relieved that the boss had put the arrogant tech researcher in her place. They sneered at Elisa's back, cementing her status as a nobody.

Allena let out a massive, shuddering exhale. The color rushed back to her cheeks, and she smiled,her victory absolute.

Tessa stared at August. A look of profound,unfiltered disappointment crossed her face. She shook her head slowly.

"I thought you were better than this," Tessa muttered under her breath, just loud enough for the table to hear.

Elisa stood frozen near the hallway.

Irrelevant,bottom-feeder employee.

The words didn't break her heart. They acted like a chemical sealant, freezing the last bleeding wounds in her chest into solid, unfeeling stone.⑤

She didn't gasp. She didn't cry. She took one slow,deep breath, pulling the freezing Aspen air into her lungs.She locked the vault on her past forever.

She did not turn around to look at the man she had married. She simply stepped forward, her boots
clicking steadily against the floor, and walked down the hallway toward the conference room, leaving the ghost of Mrs. Chambers dead in the lobby.
###Chapter 115


At ten o'clock sharp, the joint Al medical symposium officially commenced in the chalet's second-floor conference room.

The room was a masterpiece of modern architecture. Three walls were made of frameless glass, offering a blinding view of the snow-capped Rocky Mountains. The fourth wall was a massive digital projection screen, currently displaying the complex, glowing neural network architecture of Project Chiron.

Allena sat in a plush leather swivel chair near the head of the long mahogany table. She was still wearing her ridiculous white mink ski suit, looking like a lost reality TV star surrounded by doctors in tailored suits.

She had just finished a ten-minute opening monologue. It was a disaster of medical jargon,completely devoid of logic, mixing up basic cellular structures with machine learning terms. Yet,when she finished, the JHU team clapped enthusiastically, terrified of offending the billionaire's mistress.
Elisa sat at the very end of the table, as far away from Allena as physically possible. She kept her head down, her fingers flying across the glass screen of her encrypted tablet, completely ignoring the circus.

Allena soaked up the applause. She leaned back in her chair, a smug smile stretching across her face.

"And that is exactly why," Allena announced, her voice pitching higher to ensure everyone heard,"I am currently using my private network to reach out to the legendary Al architect, Faye."⑤

Allena paused for dramatic effect. "I plan to personally invite Faye to join my private medical think tank. With my clinical vision and Faye's coding,we will revolutionize the industry."

The JHU doctors gasped in manufactured awe.They showered her with compliments, praising her incredible connections and her ability torecruit a ghost that no one in Silicon Valley could track down.②

Sitting across the table,Tessa Vaughn let out a low,dark chuckle. The sound was thick with raw, biting mockery.

Allena's smile vanished. She glared at Tessa,her fragile ego instantly threatened.
She needed to reassert her dominance. She needed to remind everyone in the room who held the power. Her eyes darted around the room and locked onto the quiet woman at the end of the table.

Allena pointed a manicured finger toward the small luxury wet bar in the corner of the room.

"Elisa," Allena commanded, her tone sharp and degrading. "Go to the bar. August had his private reserve of Blue Mountain coffee flown in specifically for me. Brew it and serve it to the table."

The room went dead silent.

Asking the Chief Technical Advisor of a billion-dollar Al project to act as a waitress was a blatant,humiliating act of workplace bullyig.

Tessa's face turned bright red. She slammed her hands on the table, half-rising from her chair to defend Elisa.

Elisa didn't look up. She simply raised her left hand,extending one finger. Tessa saw the gesture and slowly sank back into her seat, her jaw clenched in fury. The absolute injustice of the moment burned in Tessa's chest, but she knew Elisa's silent command meant one thing: Do not engage.
Tessa leaned back in her chair. She crossed her arms over her chest. Her eyes locked onto Allena,burning with a dangerous, defiant light.

"Miss Mills," Tessa said, her voice a smooth, deadly purr. "Before you start ordering our Chief Technical Advisor around, perhaps you should make sure you know exactly how the legendary Faye likes her coffee."

The JHU doctors frowned, exchanging confused glances. The riddle went completely over their heads. Why was the tech researcher bringing the conversation back to Faye?

Allena scoffed, rolling her eyes. She thought Tessa was just trying to embarrass her.

"Faye will drink whatever I serve her, because I only serve the best," Allena retorted arrogantly. She shot a venomous glare at Elisa. "As for right now,bottom-feeders should do bottom-feeder work.Pour the coffee,Elisa."

Tessa's jaw locked. She opened her mouth, fully prepared to shatter her own professionalism and scream the truth into Allena's stupid face.

Click.
The sharp, crisp sound of Elisa snapping her
encrypted tablet shut echoed through the room.

It cut Tessa off instantly.

Elisa stood up. Her face was a mask of absolute,chilling indifference. There was no anger in her eyes, only the cold calculation of a machine.

She knew the math perfectly. If Tessa exposed her as Faye right now, Germaine's lawyers would instantly be notified of the NDA breach. She would lose the fifty-million-dollar war chest. Worse,Germaine would immediately cut off the funding keeping her grandfather alive at the memory care center.

She could not sacrifice her grandfather's life for a moment of petty revenge against an idiot. She had to swallow the glass.

"It's perfectly fine, Tessa," Elisa said, her voice smooth and empty. "It is just pouring coffee."②

Tessa stared at her, her brow furrowed in deep frustration. She hated watching her swallow this poison.

Allena's face twisted into a grotesque mask of pure, sadistic joy. Seeing the woman she hated forced into submission filled her with a sick high.

Elisa walked to the wet bar. She picked up the
heavy, silver serving tray. She carefully placed the delicate, expensive bone china cups onto the metal surface, filling them with the steaming, boiling hot black coffee.

She lfted the heavy tray. She turned around and began the long walk down the edge of the mahogany table, heading straight toward the woman sitting in the seat of power.
Every eye in the room watched her, waiting to see the discarded wife bow to the mistress.④
###Chapter 116


Elisa walked with absolute mechanical precision.The heavy silver tray balanced perfectly on her palms. The boiling Blue Mountain coffee steamed in the delicate bone china cups, filling the air with a rich, bitter scent.

She reached the head of the mahogany table. She stopped in the narrow space between Allena's plush swivel chair and August's empty seat-he had stepped out to take a call just as the seminar began and had just returned, standing right behind Allena.

Elisa kept her eyes blank. She bent her knees slightly,leaning forward to place the first steaming cup onto the leather coaster in front of Allena.

Allena looked at Elisa's face. She saw the perfect,unbroken calm. It infuriated her. She wanted tears.She wanted humiliation.

As Elisa's fingers began to release the handle of the cup, Allena's eyes flashed with a vicious,calculated malice.

Allena suddenly jerked forward, pretending to aggressively reach for a stack of printed data sheets on the far side of the table. She swung her
right arm out in a wide, violent arc.
Her elbow slammed directly into Elisa's wrist with bone-jarring force.

It wasn't an accident. It was a targeted physical strike.

Elisa's center of gravity shattered. The heavy silver tray violently tipped to the left.

Three bone china cups slid off the polished metal.They plummeted through the air, flipping upside down, sending a wave of boiling, black liquid directly toward the space between Elisa and Allena.

Allena instantly threw her hands over her face and let out a piercing, high-decibel scream, throwing herself backward in her chair to play the ultimate victim.

August, standing right behind Allena, reacted with pure,unthinking animal instinct.

He didn't assess the trajectory of the spill. He didn't look to see who was actually in danger. His dark eyes only registered the boiling liquid falling toward his mistress.

August lunged forward. His massive hands violently grabbed Allena's shoulders, his fingers digging into her white mink suit as he yanked her backward, out
of the path of the boiling liquid. In his explosive,frantic movement to shield his mate, August pivoted, throwing his broad body in front of hers.The violent turn caused his shoulder to slam into Elisa's chest like a battering ram.

He used the full, explosive force of a man trying to protect his territory.

Elisa was already off-balance. The sheer kinetic power of August's block lifted her feet off the carpet. She flew backward like a broken doll.

CRACK.

Elisa's lower back slammed into the sharp,unyielding edge of the solid mahogany conference table.4

A blinding, white-hot flash of agony exploded up her spine. It wasn't just a bruise. It was the exact same spot where Devon had shoved her at the Belmont Estate. The old, deep injury flared to life with a terrifying, bone-cracking intensity, shattering months of careful physical therapy in a single second. The air was violently punched out of her lungs.She let out a choked, breathless gasp as her body collapsed onto the floor, landing in a puddle of shattered china and scalding coffee.①

At the exact same second, the remaining boiling
coffee splashed heavily across August's extended right hand and the cuff of his custom suit.

The skin on his hand instantly turned angry red.Auguust let out a low grunt of pain, his jaw clenching hard.

The conference room erupted into absolute chaos.

The JHU doctors leaped out of their chairs,shouting in panic. They swarmed around August,desperate to check the billionaire's burned hand.

Allena threw herself out of her chair. She grabbed August's arm, tears streaming down her face.

"Oh my god, baby! Your hand!" Allena shrieked,sobbing hysterically. She turned her head and glared down at Elisa, her voice dripping with venom. "She did that on purpose! She tried to throw boiling water in my face because she's jealous! She's a psycho!"

The doctors glared down at Elisa with absolute disgust, fully buying the narrative of the crazy,violent ex-employee.

Not a single doctor stepped forward to help the woman bleeding on the floor. Tessa tried to run forward, but the Chief of Surgery grabbed her arm,holding her back, shaking his head in a stern
warning not to cross the billionaire.
Elisa was completely, utterly alone. She pressed a trembling hand against the floorboards. Gritting her teeth so hard she tasted blood, she forced her shaking arms to push her body up. Her face was the color of ash. She clutched her lower back, her breathing shallow and ragged from the sheer physical trauma of the impact.

August stood amidst the crowd of fawning doctors.He held his burned hand against his chest. He looked past the doctors and locked eyes with Elisa.

For one microscopic fraction of a second, the rage in August's eyes fractured. He saw the way she was holding her back. He felt the residual impact of his shoulder slamming into her chest vibrating in his bones. He hadn't meant to hit her that hard.It was a reflex.

He took a half-step forward, his mouth opening slightly.

Allena instantly tightened her grip on his arm,sobbing louder. "August, it hurts! My arm got splashed! Call the private doctor!"②

The sound of Allena's voice snapped the leash back onto August's neck. The brief flash of guilt vanished, buried under his need to maintain
control.
August looked away from Elisa. He turned his back on her. Surrounded by his security detail and the sycophantic doctors, he walked out of the conference room toward the private medical suite,leaving Elisa broken on the floor.

Elisa leaned her weight against the cold glass wall.She watched August's broad back disappear through the door.

A slow, chilling smile touched the corners of her pale lips.

That shove didn't just bruise her spine. It shattered any lingering illusion that he saw her as human.
###Chapter 117


Half an hour later, the chaos in the conference room had dissolved. Tessa had offered to help Elisa back to her room, but Elisa had gently pushed the younger woman away. Leaving the chalet now would look like a guilty retreat.

She needed ice.
Elisa walked slowly down the dimly lit, carpeted hallway of the second floor. Every step sent a sharp, electric jolt of pain radiating from her lower spine up to her neck. The pain was profound, a sickening reminder that her body was breaking under the weight of this family. She kept her right hand pressed firmly against the wall, using the solid wood to keep her body upright.

The hallway was dead silent. The thick carpet absorbed the sound of her uneven footsteps.

As she approached the corner near the staircase,the heavy oak door of the private medical suite suddenly clicked open.

August stepped out into the hallway.
He was alone. He had changed out of his coffee-
stained suit and was now wearing a thick, black cashmere turtleneck. On the back of his right hand,a pristine white, sterile medical dressing covered the burn.

They both froze.

The narrow hallway forced them into immediate,suffocating proximity. The air between them instantly turned to heavy, toxic lead.

August's dark eyes immediately dropped. He saw the way Elisa was heavily favoring her left leg. He saw her pale, bloodless knuckles gripping the wall,and the unnatural stiffness in her posture.

The physical memory of his shoulder slamming into her chest flashed in his brain.

A dark, uncomfortable twisting sensation flared in August's chest. It was a rare, invasive strain of guilt that he absolutely despised. He was a man who never apologized, but seeing the physical damage he had inflicted on her small frame cracked his armor.

August took a slow step toward her.

"How bad is your back?" August asked. His voice was low, stripped of its usual mocking arrogance,carrying a stiff, awkward tone of genuine concern.
"I can have the private doctor come to your suite and look at it."①

Elisa stopped breathing. She slowly lifted her chin.She looked at the man who, just thirty minutes ago,had treated her like a physical threat to be neutralized.

She didn't answer his question. Instead, her black,dead eyes drifted downward.

She stared directly at his right hand.

Right on the edge of the pristine white medical dressing, stamped clearly against the sterile fabric,was a bright, crimson half-circle.

It was a fresh lipstick print.

Allena had kissed his bandaged hand in the medical suite, marking her territory, playing the role of the devoted,heartbroken lover.

The bright red ink looked like a drop of fresh blood on snow. It was the absolute, undeniable proof of their intimacy.

The sight of that lip print acted like a shot of adrenaline straight into Elisa's heart. The physical pain in her spine vanished, replaced by a wave of pure, unadulterated disgust. His guilt was a joke.His concern was a cheap, worthless coin he was
trying to toss to a beggar.

Elisa's lips curved into a razor-sharp, freezing smile. She took a deliberate half-step backward,pulling herself out of his shadow.

"Don't worry about me, Mr. Chambers," Elisa said.Her voice was a flat, icy blade. "We bottom-feeders have incredibly thick skin. We survive."

She threw his exact words from the lobby right back into his face.

August's jaw clenched instantly. The guilt evaporated, replaced by a hot flash of defensive anger.He hated having his own cruelty mirrored back at him.

He stepped closer, reaching his hand out to grab her arm. "Stop talking to me like that. What happened in the conference room was a reflex.I didn't mean to hurt you."

Elisa violently twisted her torso, dodging his touch.She stared at the red lip print on his hand with overt revulsion.

"A reflex," Elisa repeated, her voice dripping with venom. "Your deepest, most primal instinct was to sacrifice my body to protect hers. That wasn't an accident, August. That was the purest expression
of exactly who you are."

She looked up into his eyes, her gaze burning with absolute contempt.

"Take your fake, secondhand concern and stay the hell away from me," Elisa sneered. "You smell like her, and it makes me sick."

The words struck August like a physical blow to the chest. His massive ego, already bruised by her rejection, violently rebelled. He had lowered himself to offer her pity, and she had spat in his face.

August's eyes darkened to pitch black. The cold,ruthless billionaire returned.

"Fine," August sneered, his voice dropping to a lethal whisper. "If you want to play the martyr,suffer alone."

He adjusted the collar of his cashmere sweater,restoring his flawless, untouchable posture.

"Tonight is the family's New Year's Eve dinner at the main estate," August commanded, his tone leaving no room for argument. "Fix your face. Put on a dress. And do not embarrass me in front of my mother."③

He didn't wait for her response. He brushed past her, his shoulder intentionally grazing the wall to
avoid touching her, and disappeared down the stairs.

Elisa leaned her head back against the cold wall.She closed her eyes. She forced the moisture back down her throat.

She pushed off the wall and limped toward the ice machine, letting the physical freezing cold numb the rotting corpse of her marriage.
###Chapter 118


By late afternoon, the violent Aspen blizzard finally broke. The howling wind died down, leaving the sky a bruised, freezing,deep indigo.

Elisa stood in front of the mirror in her suite. She had taken painkillers to dull the throbbing in her spine. She pulled on a conservative, long-sleeved black wool gown. The high neckline hid her pale skin, and the heavy, flowing skirt perfectly concealed the massive, purple bruising blooming across her lower back. She looked like she was attending a funeral.②

She grabbed her small clutch and opened the door.

Simon stood in the hallway, his hands clasped behind his back. He looked like a perfectly tailored ghost.②

"Miss Wilder," Simon said smoothly. "Mr. Chambers is waiting for you in the private garage. Please follow me to the service elevator."

Elisa's brow furrowed. The main entrance of the chalet was grand and easily accessible. Taking a service elevator to the basement was how they moved luggage, not guests.
She didn't argue. She followed Simon down a narrow, uncarpeted back corridor, stepping into a metal freight elevator that smelled faintly of industrial cleaner.

The doors opened into the cavernous, concrete underground garage.

A massive, armored black Maybach sat idling in the center of the space. Thick white exhaust plumed into the freezing air.

Simon opened the heavy rear door.

August sat in the far corner of the back seat,swallowed by the shadows. He was wearing a flawless black tuxedo. A lit cigar was clamped between his fingers, the cherry glowing bright red in the dark.

Elisa climbed inside. She slid across the leather bench, pressing her shoulder hard against the opposite door, putting as much physical distance between them as the car allowed.

The heavy door slammed shut, sealing them inside the soundproof cabin. The Maybach smoothly accelerated, climbing up the winding, ice-covered mountain road.

The air inside the car was suffocating. The smell of
rich tobacco and August's cedarwood cologne mixed into a toxic cloud.

Elisa stared out the tinted window at the passing snowbanks. The silence stretched until it felt like a physical weight on her chest.O

She turned her head. She looked at August's perfect, arrogant profile. A cold, mocking smile touched her lips.

"Is this how the great August Chambers travels now?" Elisa asked, her voice slicing through the quiet hum of the engine. "Sneaking his legal wife out through the basement like a thief in the night?"

August took a slow drag of his cigar. He exhaled a thick cloud of smoke, not bothering to look at her.

"It is a basic security protocol to avoid unnecessary complications," August replied, his voice flat and bored.

Elisa let out a sharp, genuine laugh. It held no humor.

"Complications," Elisa repeated. She turned her body slightly, locking her dead eyes onto him."You mean you didn't want to walk through the lobby and risk your JHU fan club seeing you with the 'bottom-feeder' you just publicly disowned."
August's fingers twitched around the cigar. He didn't answer.

Elisa leaned forward slightly, her analytical brain dissecting him with surgical precision.

"You play the devoted, protective fiancé in front of Allena to feed her ego and your own sick hero complex," Elisa stated, her voice dropping to a lethal,freezing whisper. "But the second the sun goes down, you drag me into a car to parade me in front of your mother and the family board."

August finally turned his head. His dark eyes locked onto hers, burning with a sudden, violent warning.

"Because next week is the Al Summit and the Aethel merger announcement," Elisa continued,hitting the exact nerve she knew drove his every action. "You are terrified. If a messy divorce scandal breaks right now, the Chambers Group stock price will tank. The board will question your stability. You don't want me here, August. You are just too much of a coward to face the financial consequences of your own pathetic sex life."

The temperature in the Maybach plummeted to absolute zero.
Elisa had just ripped off his mask of absolute power, exposing the terrified, capital-driven coward underneath.

August violently crushed the lit cigar into the crystal ashtray built into the door.

He lunged across the leather seat. His dark eyes locked onto hers, burning with the lethal intensity of an apex predator whose territory had just been breached. His massive, uninjured left hand shot out,clamping around Elisa's jaw. His long fingers dug painfully into her fragile cheekbones, the heat of his skin searing against her cold flesh. At the exact same moment, his burned right hand instinctively clenched into a tight, violent fist.The pristine white bandage stretched and a fresh drop of dark crimson blood seeped through the sterile fabric. A sharp hiss of pain escaped his lips, but the fury in his eyes completely overrode the physical agony. The physical force of his movement pinned her back against the cold glass of the window,stealing the oxygen from her lungs.

"Women who think they are smarter than the men who own them usually end up with nothing, Elisa,"August growled, his voice vibrating with a terrifying,suppressed violence.

His grip tightened. "Your only job tonight is to sit at
that table, smile, and play the obedient wife. If you do anything to embarrass me in front of my family,I will make sure you never see a single dime of your precious settlement."

Elisa was forced to look up into his furious eyes.Her jaw throbbed under his crushing grip.③

But there was no fear in her black eyes. Only a deep,bottomless pity.

She violently jerked her head to the side, breaking his grip. She rubbed her jaw,her expression turning to stone.

"Don't worry, August," Elisa whispered coldly. "I want this disgusting play to end just as badly as you do."
