August was sitting in a high-backed leather armchair in the corner of the room. He had not spoken a word since they left the hallway.
Suddenly, a low, dark sound echoed from the corner.
It was a laugh. But it was completely devoid of humor. It was a cold, metallic sound that made the hairs on the back of Devon's neck stand up.
August slowly raised his head. He was holding his encrypted Bloomberg terminal tablet. The screen cast a pale, eerie glow across his sharp features.He had been silently watching Devon's tantrum, his expression unreadable, letting the raw data on the screen paint a picture far more terrifying than Devon's empty threats.
"If you execute a short order against Aethel right now," August said, his voice a lethal, quiet monotone, "your entire fund will face a margin call and be liquidated within forty-eight hours."
Devon froze. "What? August, their tech is garbage.
We can crush them."
August didn't argue. He simply slid the heavy tablet across the glass coffee table. It stopped right in front of Devon.
The screen was flashing with urgent, red-and-green breaking news alerts from the deepest, most restricted tiers of the financial wire.
"Ten minutes ago," August stated, his eyes locking onto Devon. "Sequoia Capital and Bridgewater Associates filed a joint SEC disclosure. They just closed a Series B funding round for Aethel's Project Chiron."
Devon's breath hitched. "How much?"
"Two hundred million dollars," August said, the number dropping like a physical anvil in the quiet room.
Devon stumbled backward,his legs hitting the edge of the sofa. He collapsed onto the cushions. Two hundred million. From the two most ruthless, data -driven venture firms on the planet. His threat to short the stock was like threatening a hurricane with a water pistol.
August leaned forward, resting his elbows on his knees. His gaze shifted from Devon to Allena.The look in his eyes was no longer protective. It was dissecting.
"Read the sub-clause on the term sheet, Devon,"August commanded.
Devon leaned over, his hands shaking as he read the highlighted text on the screen. He swallowed hard. "The capital injection is... 'solely contingent upon the continued involvement and architectural oversight of the individual known as Faye.'"
August sat back. "Two hundred million dollars.Blindly handed over to a ghost."
He looked directly at Allena. The memory of the nurse's voice in the hallway echoed in his brain.Your handwritten notes... saved that man's life.
"Allena," August said, his voice dropping an octave,becoming dangerously soft. "Are you absolutely,unequivocally certain you understood the data Elisa presented in that room?"
Allena's heart slammed against her ribs.The walls were closing in. August was not stupid. He was a predator, and he was beginning to smell blood.
"I... I am a doctor, August," Allena stammered,her voice cracking. "I know clinical outcomes. Her algorithm is dangerous. I swear it."
August stared at her for five agonizing seconds. He didn't blink.
Then, he stood up. He buttoned his suit jacket with precise, sharp movements.
"Until I find out exactly who Faye is, and what kind of leverage Elisa Wilder actually holds," August ordered,his voice echoing with absolute authority,"neither of you will make a single move against Aethel. Do not speak to her. Do not look at her. Am I understood?"
He didn't wait for an answer. He turned and walked out of the lounge, leaving them in terrified silence.Allena stood frozen. The golden pedestal she had built for herself was crumbling. August was
doubting her medical expertise. If he investigated the ER notes, he would find out she was a fraud.He would leave her. She would lose the money, the status, everything.
Pure,unadulterated panic mutated into a violent,psychotic desperation.
She needed a distraction. She needed a catastrophe so massive, so undeniable, that it would permanently brand Elisa as a monster and force August to destroy her.
Allena's eyes darted toward the door. Down the hall, in the VIP pediatric wing,her seven-year-old brother Tate was recovering from his viral fever.
A horrific, venomous plan materialized in her mind.It was a flash of pure, reptilian genius-a way to not only destroy Elisa but to make herself the ultimate victim, forever binding August to her through sympathy and rage.
She grabbed her designer bag. She slipped out of the lounge, her high heels making no sound on the carpet. She moved like a shadow,hugging the walls,avoiding the security cameras, heading straight for her brother's room.
###Chapter 198

Elisa walked out of the hospital's records room,carrying a thick stack of printed clinical data files.She was exhausted, the muscles in her back screaming in protest against the rigid brace,but her mind was sharp. The meeting had been a flawless victory.
Suddenly,a horrific sound shattered the quiet hum of the hospital.
BEEP-BEEP-BEEP-BEEP.
It was the Code Blue alarm. It was coming from the VIP pediatric wing at the end of the hall.
"Help! We need a crash cart in here now!" a nurse screamed from the doorway of Room 4.
Elisa didn't think. The seven years of muscle memory as an ER trauma nurse overrode her exhaustion. She dropped the heavy files onto the floor and moved with a speed that completely defied her injuries. She half-ran, half-limped down the hall, her jaw locked as she ignored the agonizing, tearing sensation in her lower spine.When she finallyreached the door, a massive spike of pain made her vision go black for a fraction of a second, but she pushed through.
She burst through the doors of Room 4.
Tate Mills was thrashing violently on the hospital
bed. His face was swollen to twice its normal size,covered in angry, raised red hives. His lips were turning a terrifying shade of blue, and he was making a horrific, high-pitched wheezing sound as he desperately fought for air.
Anaphylactic shock.
His airway was closing fast. He had less than two minutes before his brain started dying from oxygen deprivation.
Two young floor nurses were frozen in panic,fumbling with the crash cart drawers.
"Move!" Elisa barked, shoving one of the nurses aside with her shoulder. "He's in full anaphylaxis!Draw 0.3 milligrams of Epinephrine IM, right now!Get the intubation kit ready!"
Elisa grabbed a pair of trauma shears and ripped the sleeve of Tate's hospital gown open to expose his thigh for the injection.
Before the nurse could hand Elisa the syringe,a body slammed violently into Elisa's back.
"Get away from him!"
Allena shrieked, her voice tearing through the room like shattered glass. She grabbed Elisa by the shoulders and yanked her backward with psychotic strength.
Elisa stumbled, her heavy back brace throwing off her balance. She hit the edge of a metal medical tray,sending instruments crashing to the floor.
August and Cyprian sprinted into the room, drawn
by the screaming.
August saw the blue-faced boy on the bed and froze, his face draining of color. "What the hell is happening?"
Allena spun around. Tears were streaming down her face, her makeup smeared. She pointed a trembling, accusing finger directly at Elisa.
"She did this!" Allena screamed, her voice echoing with hysterical terror. "I saw her! | saw Elisa sneak into the room and inject something into Tate's IV bag! She's testing Aethel's illegal Al drugs on him!She's trying to murder my brother!"
The room plunged into absolute, dead silence. The only sound was Tate's horrific wheezing.
It was a masterclass in evil. Allena was weaponizing the public fear of unregulated Al medicine to frame Elisa for the attempted murder of a child.①
Elisa's eyes darted to the IV bag hanging above the bed. The plastic port had a fresh, visible puncture mark.
A wave of pure, freezing horror washed over Elisa.Allena hadn't just lied. She had actually injected her own seven-year-old brother with a lethal allergen just to frame her.
"You psychotic bitch," Elisa hissed. She lunged forward, trying to reach the syringe of Epinephrine on the counter. "He is suffocating! Get out of my way!"
Allena threw herself over Tate's body, physically
blocking Elisa. "August! Stop her! She's trying to finish him off! Call the police!"
August's brain short-circuited. He saw the punctured IV bag. He saw Elisa holding the medical shears. The panic of seeing a dying child overrode all logic. He stepped forward, his hands reaching out to physically restrain Elisa.
Suddenly, the Chief of Pediatrics burst into the room,followed by a rapid response team.
"Clear the bed!" the Chief roared, physically shoving Allena out of the way. He grabbed the Epinephrine syringe and jammed it into Tate's thigh."Bag him! Push oxygen!"
The medical team swarmed the bed, blocking everyone else out.
Allena,having been shoved aside, turned her manic,violent energy entirely onto Elisa.
"I will kill you!" Allena shrieked.
She lunged at Elisa, her hands raised like claws. Her long, acrylic nails, painted a sharp, blood-red,aimed directly for Elisa's eyes.
Elisa tried to step back, but her spine locked up.The brace prevented her from twisting away. She raised her arms to protect her face, bracing for the tearing impact.
It never came.
A large, heavy body suddenly moved into Elisa's peripheral vision with shocking speed.
RIIIP.
The sickening sound of fabric tearing and flesh parting filled the air.
Allena screamed and stumbled backward, staring at her hands. Her red nails were dripping with fresh,dark blood.
Elisa lowered her arms, her eyes wide with shock.Standing directly in front of her, acting as a human shield,was Cyprian Thatcher.
Cyprian's expensive custom suit jacket was torn at the shoulder. Three deep, bloody gouges ran down the side of his neck, missing his jugular by an inch.Blood was rapidly soaking into his white collar.
The entire room froze. Even August stopped moving.
Cyprian stood there, his chest heaving, his hand slowly coming up to touch the bleeding wounds on his neck. He looked at the blood on his fingers with absolute,bewildered horror.
He had been watching Allena's hysterical, twisted face, and for the first time, he realized she wasn't a victim-she was a genuine, terrifying psychopath.He didn't fully understand why he had done it. He hated Elisa.He had mocked her for years. But when he saw the raw, murderous insanity in Allena's eyes,some deep, uncontrollable, primitive instinct had forced his legs to move.
Allena stared at Cyprian's bleeding neck,her mouth opening and closing in silent terror.
Elisa looked at the man bleeding for her. The shock faded instantly, replaced by a cold, calculating realization. The enemy's armor was cracking from the inside out. And the war was about to go nuclear.
###Chapter 199

The frantic beeping of Tate's heart monitor finally settled into a steady, rhythmic pulse. The Epinephrine had worked. The boy's airway was opening.
But the atmosphere inside the VIP room was suffocating.
August stared at the blood dripping from Cyprian's neck. The sight of his most loyal friend taking a physical attack meant for Elisa sent a violent shockwave through his brain. A dark, ugly surge of betrayal and possessive rage erupted in his chest.
He shoved past the medical equipment and marched directly up to Elisa. His massive frame blocked out the light, casting a heavy, threatening shadow over her.
"What the hell did you do to him?" August snarled,his voice a low, vibrating rumble. "What kind of manipulative psychological game are you playing to make my friends bleed for you?"
Elisa did not step back. She tilted her head up,meeting his furious gaze with eyes that were completely,terrifyingly dead.
"I didn't do anything, August," Elisa said, her voice dropping the temperature in the room to freezing."Maybe even your attack dogs are starting to realize they are taking orders from a psychopath."
Allena heard the word psychopath. She saw August's attention shifting. She needed to regain control of the narrative immediately.
She backed away from Cyprian's blood and pulled her phone from her designer bag. Her hands were shaking, but her eyes burned with a vicious,triumphant light.
"I have the direct number for the managing editor of the Wall Street Journal," Allena announced,her voice shrill and echoing off the tile walls.
August turned his head, his brow furrowing. "Allena,put the phone down."
"No!" Allena cried, pointing at Elisa. "She tried to kill my brother with her illegal Al drugs! I am calling the press right now. I am going to expose Aethel Intelligence for conducting unauthorized, lethal human trials on children!"
The threat hung in the air like a live grenade.
If that headline hit the wire-even if it was a complete lie-the SEC would immediately freeze Aethel's operations. The two-hundred-million-dollar funding round would vanish. The upcoming IPO would be dead on arrival. It was corporate extortion on a massive scale.
"Unless," Allena sneered, her true motive finally surfacing through the fake tears. "Unless she immediately resigns from Aethel and signs over every single patent associated with Project Chiron to my company. Then, I won't press charges."
She wanted the tech. She wanted the money. She was willing to murder her own brother to get it.
August's jaw locked. He looked at Allena, a flicker of genuine unease crossing his face. This was reckless. This was insane. A dark flash of displeasure crossed his eyes, but then a cold realization settled in his mind: Allena's psychotic plan, while crude and unhinged, gave him the absolute perfect leverage. It was the ultimate opportunity to finally crush Elisa's newfound independence and force her permanently back under his control. He turned back to Elisa.
He adjusted his cuffs, adopting the posture of a benevolent savior offering a lifeline to a drowning rat.
"You are cornered, Elisa," August said, his tone dripping with arrogant condescension. "If she makes that call, you go to federal prison. But if you admit you made a mistake with the IV, I will have my legal team step in. I will pay Allena off. I will keep you out of a cell."
He waited for her to crumble. He waited for the tears, the panic, the desperate plea for his protection.
Elisa looked at the man who was offering to "save"her from a crime his mistress had committed.
Slowly, Elisa took one step backward. The physical distance was small, but the psychological chasm it created was infinite.
Her face smoothed out into a mask of absolute
robotic calm. She took one deep, centering breath,her mind instantly shifting from defense to a cold,calculated counter-offensive.
"To protect the integrity of Aethel Intelligence and the upcoming IPO," Elisa announced, her voice ringing clear and loud enough for every nurse in the room to hear. "I am voluntarily stepping down from my position as Senior Technical Advisor,effective immediately, pending an independent investigation."
Allena gasped in pure, ecstatic triumph. She had won.
August, however, felt a sudden, violent drop in his stomach. Elisa's compliance was too fast. Too cold.There was no fear in her eyes.
Elisa turned her gaze to Allena. The ice in her eyes sharpened into invisible blades.
"But I will not sign over a single line of code," Elisa stated, her voice slicing through the room. "And I will use every legal resource at my disposal to ensure the person who actually poisoned that child rots in a federal penitentiary."
She turned back to August.
"As for your help, Mr. Chambers," Elisa whispered,the formal title hitting him like a slap to the face."Save your money. You are going to need it for her bail."
Elisa turned on her heel. She walked out of the hospital room, her spine perfectly straight,her
heels clicking rhythmically against the linoleum.
Cyprian watched her walk away, his hand still pressed to his bleeding neck. The strange,overwhelming urge to follow her flared in his chest,confusing and terrifying him.
August stood frozen by the hospital bed. He had expected to break her. Instead, she had looked at him like he was completely irrelevant. A wave of pure, impotent rage crashed over him. He turned and slammed his fist violently into the drywall, the impact cracking the plaster.
Out in the hallway, Elisa walked until she reached a blind spot in the security camera coverage.
She leaned heavily against the cold wall,her chest heaving as the adrenaline began to crash. She pulled out her encrypted phone and dialed Alastair.
"Alastair," Elisa said, her voice shaking slightly before solidifying into steel. "Change of plans.They used the media threat. I just publicly resigned."
Alastair roared through the phone. "What? I will bury that woman! I am calling my lawyers-"
"No," Elisa interrupted, a slow, dark,terrifying smile spreading across her face. "Don't call the lawyers.
The fish just swallowed the hook. Now, we reel her in and gut her."
###Chapter 200

The subterranean parking garage of Mather Medical Center was a cavern of concrete and flickering fluorescent lights. The air smelled of damp exhaust and industrial cleaner.
Elisa walked toward the black armored Maybach idling in the shadows.
Alastair was sitting in the driver's seat. He looked like a storm about to break. As soon as Elisa slid into the passenger seat, he slammed his hands against the leather steering wheel.
"You gave up your position," Alastair growled,his voice vibrating with barely contained fury. "You let that lying, talentless parasite force you out of your own company.I would have burned my stock to the ground before I let her win."
Elisa didn't flinch at his anger. She reached into the inner pocket of her cashmere coat.
"I didn't let her win, Alastair," Elisa said calmly. "I gave her enough rope to hang herself."
She pulled out a small, matte-black USB drive. It was secured with a biometric thumbprint scanner.She pressed her thumb to the sensor. A tiny green LED flashed.
She plugged the drive into the Maybach's central media console.
'vvnat is tnis?" Alastair asked, his brow furrowing.

I sent an encrypted ping to your cybersecurity team the moment l realized what she'd done, and they pulled this from the production company's cloud server five minutes ago."

"I need to go back up to the lobby," Elisa said,
opening the car door. "I have to sign the official HR suspension paperwork. The illusion of my defeat must be perfect."

"Elisa," Cyprian said, his tone dripping with condescending pity. "Look at what you've done to yourself. If you just go up there right now,apologize to August, and beg for his help, he might still protect you from the federal prosecutors.Don't be stupid."
He actually believed he was doing her a favor. He believed his bleeding neck gave him the right to lecture her.
Elisa stopped. She tilted her head slightly, looking at Cyprian.
Her eyes held no anger. Nohatred. Only a pure,clinical,physiological disgust, as if she were looking at a cockroach twitching on the floor.
She didn't speak a single word. She didn't even blink.1
Elisa simply stepped to the side,walked around him,and continued toward the desk,completely erasing his existence from her reality.

Ding.

This was the moment. She was going to walk up to him. She was going to break. She was going to ask for his protection.
Elisa walked toward the exit. Her path took her directly past August.
Ten feet. Five feet. Three feet.
August's breath hitched. He slightly turned his body toward her, ready to deliver his cold, conditional
offer of salvation.
Elisa kept walking. She didn't slow down. She didn't turn her head. The lobby was swarming with people,creating a chaotic blur of movement, but her path was razor-straight. She was acutely aware that ignoring him in such a public space technically violated the terms of their secret pact, but right now, she didn't care. She would rather face Germaine's wrath later than acknowledge his existence for a single second. Her eyes remained fixed on the revolving glass doors ahead.She walked past August Chambers as if he were a ghost.As if he were a blank patch of drywall.
The physical displacement of air as she walked past him hit August like a sledgehammer to the chest.
He stood paralyzed in the center of the crowded lobby. The sounds of the hospital faded into a dull ringing in his ears. He watched her push through the glass doors and disappear into the cold Manhattan night.
A sudden, violent panic seized his heart.It wasn't the panic of losing control. It was the apocalyptic realization that to Elisa Wilder, he no longer existed at all.

###Chapter 201


Elisa pushed her weight against the heavy revolving glass doors of Mather Medical Center.

The bitter November wind of Manhattan immediately whipped the hem of her black cashmere coat around her legs. She did not look back. She did not spare a single glance for the grand lobby where August Chambers stood frozen,realizing he no longer existed in her world.

She walked with rapid, sharp steps toward the black, bulletproof Maybach parked at the curb.

She pulled open the heavy passenger door and dropped into the leather seat. The intense heat of the car's climate control instantly wrapped around her freezing skin.

Alastair Cromwell sat behind the steering wheel. He should have been celebrating._Elisa had just secured the Netflix video file-the absolute proof that her half-sister Allena had poisoned her own brother. But Alastair was not smiling. He stared at the center console screen, his face so dark it looked like a thunderstorm.①
Elisa felt the immediate shift in the air. She pulled the seatbelt across her chest.

"Did Aethel's servers get hit?" Elisa asked,her brow furrowing.

Alastair grabbed a heavily encrypted Wall Street briefing printed on thick paper and shoved it across the console. His jaw was tight.

"Worse," Alastair said, his voice a low rumble."Allena's little stunt in the hospital worked. She screamed that you were running illegaldrug trials on children. The rumor hit the wire. It triggered a massive chain reaction."

Elisa looked down. Her pupils contracted into tiny black dots.

The brief showed the four top-tier venture capital firms scheduled for Aethel's Series B funding. Ten minutes ago, all four had sent official letters of withdrawal.

"The compliance departments on Wall Street have a zero-tolerance policy for pediatric medical scandals," Alastair ground out, slamming his fist against the leather steering wheel. "They didn't even wait for the NYPD to investigate. They just cut the financial cord."
A sharp, stabbing pain flared in Elisa's lower back.It was the L4 spinal disc injury, a permanent reminder of the day August's bodyguards had violently pinned her to the floor. The stress made the damaged nerves scream.①

She took a slow, deep breath. Her brain, trained as the elite Al architect 'Faye', immediately began calculating the cash burn rate. Aethel Intelligence needed hundreds of millions to keep Project Chiron alive. Without that money, the Al would die before it ever reached the market.

The silence in the heavy armored car was suffocating.

Suddenly, the burner phone sitting on the center console vibrated. The screen lit up with an unknown Silicon Valley area code.

Elisa picked it up and pressed answer.

"Well, well," the arrogant, oily voice of Preston Vance echoed through the speakerphone.

Preston was a billionaire venture capitalist. He was also a man Elisa deeply despised.

"I hear Aethel ran into a little cash flow problem this evening," Preston said, his tone dripping with predatory amusement. "I happen to have three
hundred million dollars in liquid capital sitting idle."

Alastair's head snapped toward the phone. His eyes turned instantly lethal. He reached out, his massive hand aiming to snatch the phone and crush it.

Elisa slammed her cold hand down on Alastair's wrist, pinning it to the console.

She felt a violent wave of physiological nausea hit her stomach. Preston was a vulture. But right now,she saw the face of her five-year-old daughter,Kayden,sleeping safely in Long Island. Kayden was the secret she had hidden from August for five years. To protect Kayden, to build a fortress strong enough to withstand the Chambers empire, Elisa needed Aethel to survive. She could not let the company go bankrupt.

"What level of equity dilution are you demanding for the term sheet?" Elisa asked. Her voice was flat,mechanical,completely devoid of human emotion.

Preston let out a low,wet laugh.
"I don't care about the equity, Faye," Preston said."The term sheet for three hundred million is yours.The only condition is that you have dinner with me tonight.Alone."
It was a naked, humiliating transaction. He was buying her time with his checkbook.

"Go to hell!" Alastair roared, his chest heaving as he tried to rip his arm free.

Elisa dug her fingernails into Alastair's skin. She held him down.

"Time. Place," Elisa said into the phone. She sounded like a machine executing a line of code.

"Le Coucou. Manhattan. Eight o'clock," Preston said smugly. The line went dead.

Alastair stared at Elisa in absolute disbelief. The veins in his neck bulged.

"Why would you submit to that piece of trash?"Alastair demanded, his voice shaking with rage."We can find another way. I can liquidate my personal assets."

Elisa closed her eyes and let her head fall back against the headrest.

"We are out of time, Alastair," Elisa whispered, the exhaustion finally bleeding through. "I need that money as a war fund. Tonight is just a transaction."

At exactly eight o'clock, the Maybach pulled up to the curb outside Le Coucou.
Elisa stepped out into the freezing night. She had changed into a black, deep-V haute couture gown.The fabric clung to her like a second skin. She did not look like a woman going on a date. She looked like an assassin walking to an execution.

She pushed through the heavy brass doors. The restaurant was a sea of crystal chandeliers and hushed, expensive conversations.

She spotted Preston sitting in a semi-private booth in the corner, swirling a glass of dark red wine.

Elisa walked over and sat down. She did not look at the menu. She reached into her designer clutch,pulled out a thick stack of Aethel's financial projections, and slammed them flat on the white tablecloth.

Preston smiled. He reached across the table, his hand moving to cover hers.

"You look breathtaking when you are desperate,Faye," Preston murmured, his eyes dropping to her chest.

Elisa's eyes went dead.

In a fraction of a second, she pulled a heavy,solid-brass fountain pen from her clutch. She flipped it backward and drove the sharp metal nib directly
into the soft skin over Preston's radial artery on his wrist.

She didn't break the skin, but the pressure was precise and agonizing.

"Keep your hands on your own side of the table,"Elisa said, her voice dropping to a freezing whisper.

Preston gasped, his face turning pale. He jerked his hand back, rubbing his wrist awkwardly.

Right at that moment, a massive commotion erupted at the entrance of the restaurant. The waiters scrambled, parting like the Red Sea.

August Chambers walked through the doors.

He wore an iron-gray, three-piece bespoke suit.He was surrounded by four older European investment bankers. He looked like a king inspecting his territory.

His dark, predatory eyes swept the room. Like a laser, his gaze locked onto the corner booth.

He saw Elisa. He saw the black, revealing dress.And he saw Preston sitting across from her, pulling his hand back from what looked like an intimate touch.

August stopped dead in his tracks.
His pupils dilated massively. A violent, toxic wave of pure jealousy exploded in his chest. His breathing hitched. The man who had treated her like garbage for seven years was now staring at her with the possessive rage of a madman.

Elisa felt the heavy, burning weight of his stare.

She slowly turned her head. Her eyes met August's across the crowded room.

A slow, mocking smirk curved the corners of Elisa's lips. She deliberately picked up her crystal wine glass. She raised it in the air, looking directly into August's eyes, and toasted Preston Vance.
###Chapter 202


August's jaw locked so tightly the muscles looked like they might snap under his skin.

He stood frozen in the center of the Michelin-starred restaurant. The European bankers beside him stopped talking, confused by the sudden,terrifying drop in temperature radiating from the billionaire.

August forced himself to tear his eyes away from Elisa's mocking toast. His chest heaved once.He turned his broad shoulders and marched toward the VIP private dining room at the very back of the restaurant, his heavy footsteps echoing like a death march.

Elisa watched his retreating back. The smirk vanished from her face, replaced by absolute ice.

She brought the crystal glass to her lips and swallowed the red wine in one fluid motion. She set the empty glass down with a sharp clink.

"Sign the term sheet, Preston," Elisa demanded,her voice flat. "Right now."

Preston leaned back against the plush leather
booth. A sly, greasy smile spread across his face.He picked up the three-hundred-million-dollar investment intent document and slowly slid it back across the table toward himself.

"This piece of paper is just the appetizer, Faye,"Preston said, his eyes scanning her body with blatant disrespect. "The actual signature requires you to prove you have enough...commitment to this partnership. My hotel suite is five minutes away."

A cold, homicidal spike of adrenaline shot through Elisa's veins.

He never intended to give her the money.He knew Aethel was bleeding out. He was just playing a sadistic game, trying to humiliate a woman he thought was cornered.

Elisa did not yell. She did not throw her drink.

She stood up with terrifying grace. She picked up her black cashmere coat and draped it over her shoulders. She looked down at Preston as if he were a piece of gum stuck to the bottom of her shoe.

"Your chips are too cheap for this table, Mr. Vance,"Elisa said, her voice ringing with absolute, crushing authority.
She turned on her heel and walked away. She left the financial documents sitting on the table. She left Preston sitting alone, his face flushing a violent,ugly red as he realized he had just been publicly discarded.

Elisa pushed open the heavy front doors of Le Coucou. The freezing Manhattan wind hit her face,but it felt clean compared to the air inside.

Alastair's black Maybach was already idling at the curb.

She walked quickly down the stone steps.

Suddenly, a shadow moved behind the glass.August Chambers appeared at the floor-to-ceiling window of the restaurant. He stood there, his hands shoved deep into his pockets, his dark eyes burning holes into her back as she walked away from Preston.

Elisa felt his stare. She did not turn around. She pulled open the heavy car door, slid into the passenger seat, and slammed it shut. The thick armored glass instantly severed August's visual tether.

Alastair put the car in drive and merged into the heavy New York traffic. He gripped the steering wheel,his knuckles white.
"Well?" Alastair asked, his voice tight.

Elisa rubbed her temples. A dull, throbbing headache was building behind her eyes.

"It was a complete tactical failure," Elisa admitted,staring out the window. "Preston is a predator.He wanted to use the term sheet to force me into a hotel room. He has no intention of funding Project Chiron."

Alastair slammed his foot on the brake. The heavy Maybach lurched to a violent stop at a red light.

He turned his head. His eyes were blazing with unhinged fury. No one treated his Chief Architect like a prostitute.

Alastair reached into his jacket and pulled out his phone. "I am wiring three hundred million from my offshore holding company in the Cayman Islands right now. I will plug the hole myself."

Elisa reached out and grabbed his wrist. Her grip was surprisingly strong.

"No," Elisa said firmly. "If you dump three hundred million of untraceable offshore cash into Aethel right now, it will trigger an immediate red flag with the SEC. The Securities and Exchange Commission will freeze our accounts. Allena will use that to
prove her fake rumors about our illegal activites."

Alastair took a deep, shuddering breath. He dropped the phone into his lap. He knew she was right. The financial laws of the United States were a cage they could not simply break with brute force.

"Then what is the next move?" Alastair asked.

Elisa reached into her bag and pulled out her tablet.She tapped the screen, bringing up a highly detailed schedule.

"Tomorrow morning,the Global Smart Healthcare Venture Summit begins in Miami," Elisa said. The lethal, calculating light returned to her eyes."Half the independent venture capitalists in America will be there. We are going to Miami. We are going to bypass Wall Street entirely and raise the money directly."

She looked at Alastair. "It also gets us out of New York. It removes Preston's geographical leverage."

Fifteen minutes later, they walked into the top-floor executive suite of Aethel Intelligence.

Elisa walked straight to the floor-to-ceiling windows overlooking the city. She pulled out her encrypted phone and dialed Jewel.

Jewel answered on the first ring.
"Is the extraction complete?" Elisa asked, keeping her voice low.

"Yes," Jewel said, her voice tight with anxiety."The unedited Netflix footage of Allena poisoning her brother is locked inside an offline cold wallet. But it's legally useless as is. I have to fly to Miami tomorrowv. I tracked down a former Netflix security architect who lives there. He's the only person who can provide the forensic certification to make this video an irrefutable piece of evidence in federal court. Without his digital signature, the Chambers legal team will tear it apart as a deepfake."

Elisa's stomach tightened. That video was the nuclear bomb she needed to destroy Allena and the Chambers family forever.

"Be careful, Jewel," Elisa whispered. "Do not let anyone see that drive."

Elisa ended the call. She turned around. Alastair was standing by his desk, holding two printed boarding passes.

"First class to Miami. Wheels up at 7 AM," Alastair said. His voice left no room for argument. "I am going with you. I failed to protect you from that garbage tonight. In Miami, no one gets within ten feet of you."
Elisa looked at the fierce, unyielding loyalty in Alastair's eyes. A small, genuine warmth bloomed in her chest. She nodded.

At that exact moment, back in the VIP room of Le
Coucou, August Chambers was losing his mind.

He sat at the head of the table. The European bankers were presenting a billion-dollar acquisition strategy, but August heard nothing but static. His brain was looping the image of Elisa sitting across from Preston.

August violently ripped his silk tie loose. He pulled out his phone and hit the speed dial for his Chief of Staff, Simon.

"Simon, I want a full surveillance package on Preston Vance's movements in Miami," August barked into the phone, his voice dropping to a freezing, psychotic register. "He's making a play that could disrupt our summit agenda. I want to know every meeting he takes, every call he makes.As for Elisa Wilder... report her location to me as well. I won't have that woman creating a media circus that reflects poorly on the Chambers name."

Five minutes later, a text message pinged on August's screen.

[Elisa Wilder is booked on a 7 AM flight to Miami.
Preston Vance has filed a flight plan for his private jet to Miami tomorrow evening.]

August stared at the glowing screen. The two separate pieces of information violently collided in his brain, forming a completely illogical but devastating conclusion.

They are going to Miami together. They are going to a hotel.

August stood up so fast his heavy oak chair crashed backward onto the floor.

"Prep the jet," August snarled into the phone,ignoring the shocked bankers. "We are going to Miami."
###Chapter 203


The air in Miami was thick, humid, and smelled o salt and expensive sunscreen.

Elisa and Alastair walked through the massive,sliding glass doors of the Four Seasons Hotel. The lobby was a cathedral of polished marble, towering palm trees, and the low hum of extreme wealth.

Elisa walked directly to the VIP concierge desk. She pulled her black titanium credit card and her passport from her bag and placed them on the cool marble counter.

"Checking in. Elisa Wilder. I have the top-floor oceanfront suite reserved," Elisa said, her voice crisp and professional. She had booked this room a month ago to ensure she had a secure base of operations for the summit.

The front desk manager, a man in a sharp suit,typed her name into the system.

Suddenly, his fingers stopped. A bead of cold sweat broke out on his forehead. He looked at the screen, then looked up at Elisa, his face pale with absolute panic.
"Ms. Wilder," the manager stammered, his voice trembling. "I am profoundly sorry. There has beer a... a force majeure system error. Your suite was transferred thirty minutes ago to an ultra-tier VIP guest."

Alastair stepped forward. His massive frame immediately dominated the space.

"A system error?" Alastair growled, his eyes narrowing dangerously. He knew exactly how luxury hotels operated. "You don't accidentally transfer a ten-thousand-dollar-a-night suite. W/ho used capital leverage to steal her room?"

Before the manager could answer, the sharp,rhythmic clicking of high heels echoed across the marble floor.

"Oh, it wasn't a system error, Alastair."

Elisa's spine went completely rigid. She recognized that high, grating, utterly fake voice instantly.

Allena strolled across the lobby. She was wearing a flowing, ostentatious designer resort dress. Her arm was wrapped tightly around the bicep of August Chambers.

August wore a casual, unbuttoned linen shirt, but his posture was as rigid and aggressive as a loaded
gun.
Allena walked right up to the front desk. She reached into her lmited-edition Hermès bag and pulled out a solid metal Centurion Card-the legendary Black Card of the Chambers family.She slapped it down on the marble counter with a loud,obnoxious smack.

"I'm so sorry, sister," Allena cooed, her voice dripping with toxic sweetness. "But August felt I needed the best ocean view to recover from the trauma of you trying to murder my little brother. So,he just used a little bit of family privilege to upgrade us."

Elisa stared at the Black Card. For seven years, as August's legal wife, she had never been allowed to hold that card. Now, he was using it to fund his mistress and publicly humiliate her.

August stood silently beside Allena. His dark eyes were locked onto Elisa, scanning her face for signs of devastation. He had flown to MMiami to catch her sleeping with Preston. Finding her standing in the lobby with Alastair Cromwell only twisted the knife of his jealousy deeper.

Elisa did not scream. She did not throw a fit.
She looked around the lobby. Several high-profile
tech investors were already gathering near the elevators. If she caused a scene now, Aethel's reputation would take another hit before the summit even began.

Elisa calmly picked up her passport. She turned to Alastair, completely ignoring Allena's existence.

"If they enjoy sleeping in rooms other people picked out, let them have the leftovers," Elisa said,her voice a flat, freezing monotone. "We will go to the W Hotel. It's closer to the venue anyway."

She turned on her heel and began walking toward the exit. Her movements were sharp, decisive, and completely devoid of any emotional attachment.She was throwing Allena's petty victory onto the floor like a piece of trash.

August watched her walk away.

A sharp, agonizing needle of panic pierced his chest. She was leaving. She was walking out of his sight, out of his hotel, and going somewhere with Alastair.

The thought of her sleeping in another building,completely outside his zone of control, made his blood boil.

"Wait," August commanded. His voice was a low,
vibrating rumble that echoed across the lobby.

He took three massive strides forward, physically blocking Elisa's path to the glass doors. His tall,broad body formed an impenetrable wall.

"The Chambers family does not take advantage of women," August said. His chin was tilted up, his tone dripping with the arrogant, suffocating condescension of a king offering alms to a beggar.①

He turned his head slightly toward the terrified manager.

"Open the Presidential Penthouse for her," August ordered, his voice leaving absolutely no room for debate. "The one that is kept empty for state visits.Put the entire stay on my tab."

A collective gasp rippled through the lobby. The Presidential Penthouse cost fifty thousand dollars a night. It was the absolute pinnacle of luxury. And August Chambers was just throwing it at his ex-wife.

Allena's smug smile instantly vanished. Her face twisted into a mask of pure, unadulterated horror and jealousy. Her fingernails dug so hard into her palms they almost drew blood. Why was he giving this bitch the best room in the city?
Alastair let out a harsh, mocking laugh.He stepped forward to reject the insulting charity.

Elisa raised her hand, stopping Alastair.
She looked up into August's dark, intense eyes.
She knew exactly what this was. This wasn't generosity. This was a leash. He was terrifed of losing sight of her. He wanted to trap her in his building, under his financial control, to prove to himself that he still owned her.

A slow, chilling smile spread across Elisa's face.It was a smile completely dev/oid of warmth.

She reached out and took the heavy gold keycard from the manager's trembling hands.

"Thank you for your extreme generosity, Mr.Chambers," Elisa said. Her voice was soft, but it cut like a surgical scalpel. "I sincerely hope spending this money makes your pathetic, fragile ego feel a little bit better today."

She held the gold card between her fingers,looking at August as if he were a sad, broken toy.

Then, she stepped around him and walked directly toward the VIP private elevator.

August stood frozen on the marble floor. The brutal, surgical precision of her words had
bypassed all his armor and stabbed directly into his deepest insecurity. He had tried to assert dominance, and she had just publicly castrated his pride.

The heavy steel doors of the VIP elevator slid shut,cutting off his view of her.

Inside the elevator, Elisa leaned back against the mirrored wall. Her eyes turned into dark, dangerous slits. He wanted her in his hotel? Fine. She was going to turn this building into a war zone.
###Chapter 204


The VIP elevator shot upward, the numbers on the digital display climbing rapidly.

At the forty-fifth floor, the elevator chimed and came to a smooth halt. The polished steel doors slid open.

Devon Browning and Allena stepped into the cabin.Allena had already changed into a skin-tight,sequined evening gown for the summit's opening reception.

The moment they stepped inside, the spacious elevator suddenly felt like a suffocating cage. The air grew thick with immediate, toxic hostility. The doors slid shut, locking the three of them in a moving steel box.

Devon looked down at the heavy gold keycard in Elisa's hand. The symbol of the Presidential Penthouse gleamed under the halogen lights. A vicious, ugly sneer twisted Devon's face.

He shoved his hands into the pockets of his tailored trousers.

"You really think sleeping in the Penthouse changes
anything, Elisa?" Devon mocked, his voice echoing loudly in the confined space. "You think it covers up the fact that you are a toxic, useless liability?"

Elisa kept her eyes fixed on the changing floor numbers. She didn't even blink.

Devon took a step closer, trying to use his height to intimidate her. "The four biggest VCs on Wall Street just pulled out of Aethel. Everyone knows you are a fraud. You are a cancer dragging Alastair Cromwell down."

Allena covered her mouth and let out a high-pitched, grating giggle.

"It's true, sister," Allena cooed, leaning against the handrail. "Building Al isn't like emptying bedpans in the ER. You should just surrender now. If you beg,August might still leave you a few crumbs to survive on."

Elisa took a slow, deep breath. The elevator chimed, signaling they had reached the top floor.

She stood up straight. She slowly turned her head and looked at Devon. Her eyes were so cold and dead they looked like black ice.

"The Browning Venture Fund posted a one-point-two percent return on investment last quarter,"
Elisa stated. Her voice was a flat, mechanical drone, delivering the data like a lethal injection."You are failing to outpace basic inflation."

Devon's smug smile vanished instantly. His face flushed a dark, angry red.

"lf I were you," Elisa continued, her voice dropping to a freezing whisper, "I would go back to my room and read a financial report, instead of worrying about how to value a ten-billion-dollar decacorn."

Devon opened his mouth to scream at her, but the elevator doors dinged and slid open.

Elisa stepped out into the plush hallway without looking back, leaving Devon and Allena choking on their own rage inside the steel box.

At eight o'clock that evening, the Grand Ballroom of the Four Seasons was packed with the most powerful venture capitalists and tech CEOs in the world.

Elisa walked through the massive double doors.She had changed into a midnight-blue velvet gown.The fabric was expertly draped, absorbing the light and perfectly concealing the rigid, bulky outline of her carbon-fiber back brace, making her look like an untouchable creature born from the shadows.Her dark hair was swept up, exposing the long,
elegant line of her neck.

The moment she entered, the low hum of conversation shifted. Eyes turned toward her. She was Faye, the architect of Project Chiron, the most mysterious and dangerous woman in the room.

In the center of the ballroom, August Chambers stood holding a crystal flute of champagne. He was surrounded by Silicon Valley billionaires.

He saw Elisa.

His breath caught in his throat. HHis hand tightened around the stem of his glass. He could not look away. The sheer, undeniable power radiating from her was intoxicating.

Allena, clinging tightly to August's arm, felt his muscles lock. She followed his gaze and saw Elisa.A wave of pure, acidic jealousy burned Allena's stomach. She deliberately dug her nails into August's arm and dragged him across the room,steering him directly toward Elisa to mark her territory.②

Elisa was standing near a pillar, deep in conversation with Jett Harkin, the eccentric CEO of a massive medical robotics firm. Jett was animated,clearly fascinated by Elisa's technical knowledge.
August and Allena stopped right next to them.August stared at Elisa with a dark, aggressive intensity, completely ignoring Jett.

Suddenly, Jett Harkin stopped talking. He frowned and leaned forward, sniffing the air between Elisa and August.

Jett was a notorious collector of rare perfumes.His nose was insured for millions, and rumors said he once sniffed out a tech mogul's secret mistress just by catching a whiff of her base notes in an elevator.

"This is absolutely incredible," Jett said, his,voice loud enough to cut through the surrounding chatter. He looked back and forth between August and Elisa wvith a bewildered expression. "Ms.Wilder... Mr. Chambers. Are you two wearing the exact same fragrance?"

The conversations around them instantly died.Several powerful investors turned their heads, their eyes wide with sudden, intense curiosity.

Jett closed his eyes and inhaled_deeply. "This is Jean-Claude's discontinued bespoke blend. There are only two bottles left in the world. The top note is freezing cedar, but the base note... the base note is an incredibly intimate, lingering ambergris."

Allena's face turned the color of chalk.
She stared at August in absolute horror. She had always thought that cold, sharp scent was just August's natural smell. She had buried her face in his neck a hundred times, inhaling what she now realized was a custom "couples" fragrance he shared with his ex-wife.

August froze completely. The champagne glass in his hand trembled slightly.

His psychological armor shattered. He had subconsciously refused to change his cologne for seven years because it was the only physical trace of Elisa he allowed himself to keep. Now, his deepest, most pathetic secret had just been publicly dissected by a stranger.

The surrounding investors exchanged knowing,mocking glances. The great August Chambers, who claimed to hate his ex-wife, was still wearing her scent.

Elisa looked at August's rigid, pale face. A wave of intense physiological disgust rolled through her stomach. He humiliated her, he destroyed her life,yet he clung to her smell like a sick, twisted stalker.

Elisa took a deliberate step backward, creating a physical gap between them. A look of absolute,crushing contempt settled on her face.
"You are mistaken, Mr. Harkin," Elisa said. Her voice was crystal clear, carrying effortlessly across the silent room. "That is just the stale stench of a mistake I threw in the trash years ago. I had no idea Mr. Chambers had developed a fetish for digging through the garbage."

She turned and walked away, leaving August standing in the center of the ballroom, his pride bleeding out onto the floor.
###Chapter 205


The ice water dripped slowly from Preston's chin,soaking into the collar of his custom tuxedo shirt.

He didn't yell. He didn't jump up. He slowly wiped his face with his hand, his eyes tracking Elisa's retreating back until she disappeared into the hotel lobby. A slow, deeply malignant smile stretched across his wet face. On Wall Street, prey that fought back only made the kill more satisfying.

The next morning, the Miami sun beat down relentlessly on the floor-to-ceiling windows of the Four Seasons' elite breakfast dining room.

August sat at a corner table overlooking the ocean.His right hand was tightly wrapped in white medical gauze, hiding the deep cuts from the shattered crystal. His face was a mask of exhausted,brooding granite. He hadn't slept a single second.

Cyprian sat across from him,-quietly sipping espresso. Allena sat next to August, picking at a plate of fruit, her eyes darting nervously between the men.

Preston Vance strolled into the dining room. He looked perfectly rested, completely unfazed by the
humiliation of the previous night. He pulled out a chair and sat down directly across from August.

Preston picked up a silver butter knife and pointed it casually toward the center of the table.

"That woman puts on a great show of being untouchable," Preston said, his voice loud enough to ensure Allena heard every word. "But I had my analysts do a deep dive on Aethel's corporate history last night."

August's jaw tightened. He stared out at the ocean,refusing to look at Preston.

"Aethel was founded exactly three years into your marriage, August," Preston continued, dropping the poison into the air. "Alastair Cromwell is a ruthless shark. He doesn't just hand over the keys to a billion-dollar Al project to a random ER nurse with a pretty face."

Preston leaned in, his voice dropping to a conspiratorial, filthy whisper. "It's obvious. She was sleeping with Cromwell the whole time. She played the dutiful wife in your penthouse while letting Alastair fund her little tech hobby. You wore horns for years, Chambers."

The words hit the table like alive grenade.
Allena's eyes widened in pure, ecstatic joy. This was the perfect narrative. She immediately slapped her hand over her mouth, feigning absolute shock.

"Oh my god," Allena gasped, looking at August with wide, pitying eyes. "August... that makes perfect sense. No wonder she signed the divorce papers so quickly. She already had her billionaire backup plan waiting in bed. You poor thing."②

August's hand, the one wrapped in gauze, twitched violently on the table.

A sharp, agonizing spike of pure jealousy drove straight through his ribs. The logical part of his brain knew Elisa. He knew her rigid, almost painful loyalty. But the image of her in Alastair's arms, the image of them laughing at him behind his back for years, was a virus that immediately infected his deepest insecurities.

He felt physically sick.

But August Chambers never showed weakness.He slowly turned his head. His dark, dead eyes locked onto Preston.

August let out a short, harsh breath that sounded like a laugh, but held zero amusement.

"A piece of garbage I threw out onto the street,"
August said, his voice dripping with absolute,freezing cruelty. "If another man wants to pick it up and pretend it has value, that is his problem. I don't care who she sleeps with."

Allena exhaled a massive sigh of relief. She reached out and stroked August's arm, believing she had finally won.

Meanwhile, on the second floor of the hotel,outside the main summit conference hall.

Elisa stood in a quiet corridor, holding a sleek silver
tablet. She and Alastair were running through the final data models for their pitch. Because of the VC pullout, Aethel had been pushed to the worst time slot of the day. They had exactly fifteen minutes to convince a room full of skeptical European investors to save their company.

Suddenly, the phone sitting on the small cocktail table next to Elisa began to vibrate violently.

The screen lit up. It was a FaceTime video request.The caller ID read: Preston Vance.

Elisa's brow furrowed. She reached out, hit the red decline button, and immediately blocked the number.

Five seconds later, the phone vibrated again.
FaceTime request. Hidden Number.
She declinedit.

Ten seconds later, it rang again. Then again. And again.

Preston was using an automated robocalling software to bypass her blocks. The phone buzzed relentlessly against the glass table, the sound echoing in the quiet corridor like a swarm of angry wasps.

A tight, suffocating pressure built in Elisa's chest.The constant vibration felt like a physical assault on her nervous system, dragging her focus away from the critical data on her tablet.

Alastair looked up from his notes. He saw the flashing screen. His face darkened instantly. He reached for the phone.

"Give it to me," Alastair growled, his voice vibrating with lethal intent. "I will have my cybersecurity team reverse-trace the IP and brick every electronic device he owns."

Elisa slammed her hand down over the phone,stopping him.

"No," Elisa said, taking a sharp breath. "If Aethel launches a cyberattack right now, it proves Allena's
rumors that we operate illegally. We cannot use company resources to fight a personal stalker."

The phone stopped ringing for two seconds, then immediately started buzzing again.

Elisa stared at the flashing screen. The exhaustion and the pressure suddenly crystallized into a cold,absolute fury. Running away from predators only made them hunt harder.

"If he wants to see me so badly," Elisa whispered,her eyes turning into chips of black ice, "then I will look him in the eye."

She picked up the phone. Her thumb hovered over the green accept button. She prepared to delver a legal threat so severe it would make Preston's lawyers bleed.

She pressed accept.

###Chapter 207


The screen of Elisa's phone flickered, adjusting to the bright light.

Preston Vance's face filled the high-definitior display. He was smirking, his eyes gleaming with the arrogant satisfaction of a hunter who had finally cornered his prey.

"Finally decided to stop hiding, Faye?" Preston sneered, his voice echoing slightly. "I was beginning to think you were going to cower in the hallway all day."

Elisa opened her mouth, the legal threats loaded on her tongue like hollow-point bullets.

But the words died in her throat.

Her eyes bypassed Preston's smug face and focused on the background of the video. The camera angle was wide, capturing the luxurious,sun-drenched VIP breakfast dining room behind him.

Sitting on a plush sofa, perfectly framed over Preston's left shoulder, were August and Allena.
Because Preston was using a top-tier phone, the image was devastatingly clear.

Elisa watched as Allena leaned heavily against August's side. Allena held a peeled green grape between her manicured fingers. She giggled, a sound Elisa couldn't hear but could perfectly visualize, and lifted the grape to August's mouth.

August Chambers-a man with severe germophobia,a man who despised public displays of affection, a man who had once flinched when Elisa tried to adjust his tie in public-did not pull away.

He leaned his head down, his lips parting, and took the grape directly from Allena's fingers.

The visual hit Elisa's retinas with the force of a physical blow.

Her breath hitched violently. A sharp, sickening pain twisted deep in her stomach. She had already decided to destroy him. She had already accepted that the marriage was a lie. But seeing the man who had treated her like a diseased untouchable for seven years, now openly, intimately indulging the woman who had stolen her family... it was a physiological violation.

Preston noticed the sudden, microscopic tightening of her jaw as her eyes shifted to something behind
him. He subconsciously adjusted his grip on the phone, causing the screen to wobble slightly. The new angle perfectly and accidentally framed August and Allena in the dead center of the shot.A cruel, delighted smile spread across his face as he realized what he had stumbled upon. Without moving the phone again, he raised his voice,shouting over his shoulder.

"Hey, August!" Preston called out, ensuring the microphone picked up every word. "Your ex-wife is finally on the line. She's a stubborn one. Poured water on me last night, dodged fifty calls this morning."

In the background of the video, August's head snapped up.

He looked directly at Preston's phone. Across the digital void, his dark, intense eyes locked onto Elisa's face on the screen.

Preston laughed, a filthy, grating sound. "Give me some advice, Chambers. How do you break a cold bitch like this? Do I need to use force, or do I just keep throwing money at her until she spreads her legs?"

Elisa stared at the screen. Her heart hammered against her ribs. She waited. She waited for August to show a single shred of human decency. She
waited for him to shut down the disgusting,degrading sexual harassment happening right in front of him.

August stared into the camera lens.

His eyes were completely dead. The jealousy and panic he had felt minutes ago were buried under a thick, impenetrable layer of toxic pride. He wanted to hurt her. He wanted to punish her for walking away from him.

A slow, terrifyingly cold smirk curved August's lips.

"Her?" August's voice transmitted through the phone speaker, deep, distorted, and utterly devoid of humanity. "She only understands capital.If the zeros on your check are big enough, Vance,she will sell you absolutely anything."

The words struck Elisa like a physical execution.
He didn't just fail to defend her. He actively participated in her degradation. He publicly reduced her to a prostitute, slapping a price tag on her soul just to protect his own fraĝile ego.

In the background, Allena let out a sharp, victorious laugh.

Elisa did not cry. The pain in her stomach vanished,replaced by a sudden, absolute, sub-zero
numbness. The last, pathetic, lingering ghost of the woman who had loved August Chambers was instantly incinerated.

"Thank you for the market analysis, Mr. Chambers,"Elisa said. Her voice was so flat, so devoid of any human inflection, it sounded like a machine. "Enjoy your life in the garbage dump."

She tapped the red button, severing the call. She held the power button down until the phone powered off completely, then dropped it into her coat pocket.

Alastair had been standing close enough to hear everything. His massive hands were clenched into fists so tight his knuckles were white. He was vibrating with homicidal rage.

"I am going to kill him," Alastair breathed, his voice a terrifying growl. "I am going to tear Chambers Group apart piece by piece on the NASDAQ until he is begging on the street."

Elisa turned to Alastair. Her eyes were completely clear. The hesitation was gone.

She reached into her bag and pulled out the encrypted USB drive containing the Netflix footage.

"Don't waste your anger on a corpse, Alastair,"
Elisa said, her voice chillingly calm. "He just gave me the final push I needed."

She handed the drive to Alastair. "As soon as we finish this pitch, contact your media fixers. I want this video broadcasted globally tonight. We are going to execute Allena, and we are going to let August burn with her."③
###Chapter 208


By three o'clock in the afternoon, the Miami sun was a blinding, oppressive force.

Elisa stood at the edge of the Four Seasons'exclusive, top-floor infinity pool. She had just survived a grueling three-hour pitch to the European VCs. Her brain was exhausted, but the adrenaline of the impending revenge kept her standing.

She wore a crisp, white linen pantsuit and dark sunglasses, waiting for a specific Swiss investor who had requested a private follow-up meeting.

The pool area was relatively quiet, dotted with wealthy guests lounging on pristine white daybeds.

"You look terrible, Elisa."

The arrogant, drawling voice broke her concentration. Elisa didn't turn her head. She knew exactly who it was.

Cyprian Thatcher walked up to her. He was wearing custom swim trunks and holding a bright pink Margarita. A thick white bandage covered the deep scratches on his neck-the scratches Allena had
given him when she lost her mind in the hospital.

Cyprian stopped a few feet away, looking her up and down with a sneer. He was deeply uncomfortable with the fact that he had instinctively protected her back in that New York hospital a few days ago, and he was overcompensating with cruelty.

"August was right about you," Cyprian said, taking a sip of his drink. "You bounce from Alastair to Preston without missing a beat. You are just desperate to keep your claws in this lifestyle, aren't you?"

Elisa did not bother to look at him anymore. She deliberately turned her back to the pool deck,facingthe endless horizon of the ocean behind the glass edge. Behind the dark lenses of her sunglasses, her eyes were dead.

"Cyprian," Elisa said, her voice dropping to a low,surgical register. "If your brain capacity only allows you to comprehend the world through the exchange of bodily fluids, I suggest you jump in the pool and cool off your pathetic lower half."

Cyprian's face flushed red. He opened his mouth to snap back, but Elisa cut him off.

"And before you try to lecture me on morality," Elisa
continued, her tone sharpening into a blade, "you might want to check on your 'sweet, innocent'Allena. After all, not everyone has a psychological fetish for injecting lethal poison into their own seven-year-old brother."

The words hit Cyprian like a physical blow. His hand instinctively flew up to touch the bandage on his neck. The memory of Allena's psychotic,bloodthirsty eyes flashed in his mind.

He opened his mouth, stammering, trying to form a defense.

At that exact moment, a commotion erupted from a nearby cabana. A man in a loud Hawaiian shirt, a mid-level investor Cyprian had been trying to impress all day,laughed boisterously.
"Go on, Leo, get the ball!" the man shouted.
His chubby, five-year-old son, wearing bright orange swim trunks, sprinted blindly across the slippery tiles after a heavy, wet volleyball. The boy's mother screamed for him to slow down, but the father just laughed, more interested in his phone than his child's safety.

The boy's wet feet slipped on a puddle.
He lost his balance and launched forward like a
fleshy missile.
His head slammed directly into the center of Elisa's lower back.①

It was the exact location of her L4 disc injury.

A sound like a dry branch snapping echoed in Elisa's skull. A blinding, white-hot agony exploded from her spine, shooting instantly down both of her legs.

Her nervous system short-circuited. Her legs simply ceased to function.

Elisa let out a choked, breathless gasp. Her body tipped backward, completely out of her control.

Cyprian's eyes widened in horror. He dropped his drink and lunged forward, his hand grasping desperately at the air, but his fingers only brushed the smooth linen of her jacket.

Elisa fell backward into the deep end of the infinity pool.

The freezing water swallowed her instantly. The impact drove the air from her lungs.

Underneath the surface, Elisa's eyes snapped open. She tried to kick her legs, tried to swim upward, but the nerve damage in her spine
triggered a massive, paralyzing muscle spasm.Her lower body was dead weight.

Water rushed into her nose and mouth.

Instantly, a horrifying psychological trigger fired in her brain. She was no longer in a luxury pool in Miami. She was seven years old again, trapped in the raging, muddy floodwaters of the Appalachian Mountains, watching her father drive away and leave her to drown.

Pure, primal panic seized her throat. She thrashed her arms wildly, but she was sinking fast.

On the pool deck, Cyprian stood frozen, his face pale with terror. He didn't know how to swim. He began screaming frantically for the lifeguard.

Directly above them, on the second-floor VIP terrace, August Chambers was standing with a cup of black coffee.

He had been watching Elisa and Cyprian argue.He had seen the boy crash into her.

For the first half-second, August's cynical brain told him it was a trick. A pathetic attempt to gain sympathy.

But then he looked down into the crystal-clear water. He saw Elisa's body contorting in unnatural,
agonizing spasms. He saw the frantic, desperate flailing of her arms slowly begin to weaken. He saw the trail of silver bubbles escaping her lips as she sank toward the bottom.

She was dying.

A catastrophic, world-ending explosion detonated inside August's mind.

Every single layer of his arrogance, his jealousy,his toxic pride-it all instantly vaporized. The only thing left was a raw, screaming, animalistic terror that tore his soul in half.

He couldn't breathe. He couldn't think.

He dropped the coffee cup. It shattered on the stone floor.

August didn't run for the stairs. He slammed his hands onto the glass railing of the terrace and vaulted his massive body over the edge,plummeting five meters straight down toward the water.
###Chapter 209

The impact was violent.

August smashed feet-first into the deep end of the infinity pool, sending a massive geyser of water exploding into the air. The sheer force of the five-meter drop drove him instantly to the bottom of the pool.

The water was a chaotic blr of blue and white bubbles. August ripped his eyes open, ignoring the stinging chlorine.

He saw her.

Elisa was suspended near the bottom, her white linen suit billowing around her like a shroud. Her eyes were closed. Her arms had stopped thrashing.She was drifting downward, completely still.

A soundless scream tore through August's chest.He kicked his legs with explosive, desperate power,shooting across the bottom of the pool like a torpedo.

He reached her. His strong arms wrapped around her waist, pulling her limp body flush against his chest. The moment he felt how cold and lifeless
she was, a violent shudder of pure terror ripped through his spine.

He planted his feet on the bottom tiles and launched them both upward.

They broke the surface. August gasped for air, his lungs burning. He immediately shifted his grip,supporting the back of Elisa's neck to keep her face above the water.

"Pull her up!" August roared at Cyprian and the lifeguards who had gathered at the edge.

Multiple hands reached down, grabbing Elisa's arms and hauling her dead weight onto the wet tiles.

August didn't wait for help. He grabbed the edge of the pool and hauled his massive frame out of the water in one fluid, adrenaline-fueled motion. His expensive clothes were ruined, plastered to his skin, but he didn't care.

He dropped to his knees beside Elisa.①

Her face was terrifyingly pale. Her lips had a sickening blue tint. She was not breathing.

August didn't hesitate. He interlocked his fingers,placed the heel of his hand on the center of her chest, and began aggressive CPR compressions.
"Breathe," August commanded, his voice cracking,sounding completely unrecognizable. "Elisa,breathe!"

He pressed down, the physical exertion burning his shoulders. He was terrified of breaking her ribs, but he was more terrified of losing her.

"Don't you dare do this," August whispered frantically, his wet hair dripping onto her face."Wake up!"

On the fifteenth compression, Elisa's body violently convulsed.

She rolled onto her side and vomited a massive stream of chlorinated water onto the tiles. She gasped, a horrible, rattling sound, her hands clutching her chest as her lungs desperately pulled in oxygen.

The moment August heard her breathe, the last thread of his sanity snapped.

He lunged forward and pulled her into his arms. He crushed her against his chest, burying his face in her wet hair. His massive body was shaking uncontrollably. He held her so tightly it bordered on painful, as if he were trying to physically fuse their bodies together so she could never slip away again.
"August!"
A shrill, hysterical scream shattered the moment.

Allena pushed through the crowd of onlookers.She saw August kneeling on the wet ground,holding his ex-wife like she was the only thing that mattered in the universe. Her face twisted into a mask of ugly,venomous jealousy.

She reached down and grabbed August's wet shoulder, trying to yank him away.
August's head snapped up.

He looked at Allena. His eyes were completely devoid of human emotion. They were the eyes of a predator looking at a threat.

"Roll," August snarled. The single word was a guttural, terrifying threat that made the lifeguards step back.

Allena stumbled backward, her face draining of color. She fell onto her hands and knees, trembling in fear.

August didn't look at her again. He scooped Elisa up into his arms, ignoring her weak protests, and carried her straight to the VIP elevator.

An hour later, inside the silent, freezing air
conditioning of August's Presidential Penthouse.
The concierge doctor had just left. He had administered a heavy dose of anti-inflammatories and warned that Elisa's fever would spike due to the water in her lungs and the severe shock to her damaged nervous system.

Elisa lay in the center of the massive king-sized bed,shivering violently under the thick duvet.

August sat in a leather armchair in the corner of the room. He had changed into dry clothes, but his mind was still drowning.

He watched her chest rise and fall. The sheer,apocalyptic terror he had felt when he thought she was dead was slowly morphing into something else. It was morphing into a violent, suffocating rage. He hated that she had this much power over him. He hated that he had exposed his ultimate weakness in front of a crowd. He hated the pathetic, desperate version of himself that had thrown his own life away to dive into that water without a second thought. She _had effortlessly stripped away all his armor, reducing him to a frantic madman for everyone to see. That loss of control was unacceptable. It had to be eradicated.

To survive, his pathological defense mechanisms slammed back into place. He began to rebuild the
ice wall in his mind.

Elisa groaned softly. Her heavy eyelids fluttered open.

The room was dark, lit only by the city lights outside the window. She tried to sit up, but a sharp pain in her spine forced her back down with a gasp.

"Why am I in your room?" Elisa rasped, her throat raw from the chlorine.

August stood up. He walked slowly to the edge of the bed. He looked down at her, his face a mask of absolute, impenetrable cruelty.

"You threw yourself into my pool to put on a show,"August said, his voice dripping with venomous sarcasm. "I dragged you out because I didn't want the Four Seasons making the evening news with a corpse. It's bad for business."

He leaned over, placing his hands on the mattress,trapping her.

"Did you really think a pathetic suicide stunt would make me feel sorry for you, Elisa?" August sneered."It's disgusting."

Elisa stared up at him. The fever was burning her brain, but his words were like liquid nitrogen.
Any tiny, microscopic fraction of gratitude she might have felt for him pulling her out of the water instantly died. He was a monster. He would always be a monster.

A weak, rattling laugh escaped her lips.
"Don't worry, Mr. Chambers," Elisa whispered, her eyes locking onto his with absolute, dead certainty."Next time, I will make sure I die much further away,so my corpse doesn't dirty your view."
###Chapter 210


The silence in the penthouse was absolute and suffocating.

Elisa's words hung in the air, a final, chilling declaration of death for whatever twisted connection still existed between them. August's jaw tightened, but he didn't say a word. He turned his back on her and walked to the leather sofa, sitting down in the shadows.

Elisa ignored the burning pain in her spine and the dizzying heat of her fever. She threw off the heavy duvet. Her bare feet hit the plush carpet.

She walked unsteadily toward the armchair where her wet clothes had been discarded. She began digging frantically through the pockets of her damp cashmere coat.

"What are you looking for?" August asked. His voice was low, laced with a dangerous, simmering irritation.

"My phone," Elisa rasped, not turning around. "I have a four o'clock virtual meeting with the Swiss VC group. It is the last chance to save Aethel."
August let out a short, cruel laugh.

He reached into the inner pocket of his suit jacket.He pulled out a rectangular object and tossed it onto the glass coffee table. It landed with a heavy,wet thud.

Elisa turned. It was her phone. The screen was shattered into a spiderweb of cracks, and water was actively dripping from the charging port.

"If you mean that piece of scrap metal," August said, his eyes cold and mocking, "it sat at the bottom of the pool for ten minutes. It's dead."

Elisa stared at the ruined phone. The blood drained from her face. Without that device, she couldn't access the encrypted video conferencing app. She couldn't call Alastair. She couldn't contact Jewel to check on the Netflix footage.

She took a sharp breath, fighting down the panic.
She turned and lunged toward the nightstand,reaching for the hotel's landline phone. She could call the front desk and have them connect her to
Alastair's room.

Before her fingers could touch the plastic receiver,a massive hand slammed down over hers, pinning it to the phone.
August had moved with terrifying speed. He stood right behind her, his chest brushing her back.

"Don't waste your energy," August said, his voice a low, vibrating hum right next to her ear. "When I brought you in, I had the hotel manager activate a Tier-One security protocol for this entire floor. All external communication lines have been severed and rerouted through my personal encrypted satellite network. It's a standard precaution against corporate espionage. It also has the convenient side effect of keeping you quiet."

It was a blatant, psychotic abuse of power,wrapped in the language of business.

Elisa spun around, her back hitting the nightstand.
She glared up at him, her chest heaving.
